I was very excited about this game as I am a huge fan of the first one. They changed so many things that worked really well in the 1st game, and made them just completely ass in this one.

The UI is just trash now. The inventory screen got better as I played, but the shop screens(most notably the blacksmith) is the worst UI. All the gear is under one tab. Luckily it bunches together sets, but having to scroll through rows of stuff is just terrible. Things should have been separated better to make it easier to read and compare.

Upgrading weapons is now a confusing mess. Instead of a Class 1 weapon with a Ranking of 2, the upgraded version of the same gear just has a different symbol on it. No numbers telling you if that symbol is a high ranking symbol, just an arbitrary symbol that you now have too compare to another arbitrary symbol. Is it higher than what you own? Who knows! There's so many upgrades that its almost impossible to keep track of what symbol means 1 and which means 9. You kept it super simple in the first game, why change it?

You now have a camp you have to go back to if you want to do anything. This is where all your shops are, and the only place you can level up and set skill points. So the flow of what made the first game fun is shot. I now have to stop my run and leave the map to level up, and then go back and start over. 

Yup starting over. Now there's only 2-3 spawn points in a map that you can teleport to. You have no control over that. You can teleport from all of them but can only go back to a few. Given that you now have to leave to level up or upgrade gear it becomes a slow aggravating process to move forward in this game the further you get.

Range combat is now limited to X amount of ammo, and when you run out you have to sit there holding down a button while you craft more. That crafting is an all or nothing thing also. You don't slowly gain ammo overtime, you have to wait for the process to finish before you get any ammo at all. 

Mage hunts were a bad idea. I'm sorry, but I don't want to chase a mini boss through the level slowly picking away at it's health while also fighting the bad guys from the level that re-spawn when you start a Mage hunt. Then we get to where we're going to start the real fight, after which the hunt ends and the level enemies you just killed re-spawn again. Oh also, while you are hunting a mage boss another one could just be in the map at the same time, and now you gotta deal with that one and it's minions at the same time.

It honestly all boils down to the fact that there was a wonderfully beautiful game that they made, and instead of following that up by upgrading a few systems they decided to go a fully different direction. It ended up hurting the game.

盐与献祭在一代的基础上进行了大刀阔斧的“革新”，融合了怪猎系列狩猎怪物的玩法，移动方式上增加了设计深度更高的钩爪，地图也进行了分割，不再是一代无出其右的一体式联通地图，而是以关卡的形式组成的小地图。

总体来说完全是扬短避长之作。首先惊艳的、探索性强大的地图没了。盐与献祭单个关卡的地图设计其实不差，但是珠玉在前，比较之下就显得没有那么惊艳，而且由于本作强推狩猎法师玩法，每张地图上都增加了若干需要吞噬冠名法师心脏才能解开的门，在没有地图的情况下，后续去寻找这些门是相当考验玩家记忆力，是逼迫玩家重复跑图。

其次，本作主推的狩猎法师玩法，前期还算比较有意思，但是中后期相当无聊，撑不起整个流程的体验。怪猎之所以敢把这个作为游戏的核心体验，是因为人家动作设计和交互体验相当出色，盐与献祭可以相比吗？很多法师BOSS设计的完全不及格，远程接近了以后零帧起手接0CD近战，加上本作人物韧性非常孱弱，有时候会像皮球一样被BOSS反复拍打，BOSS战相当无趣，通篇流程下来，甚至没有什么BOSS给我留下了深刻印象。

最后简单说说本作的武器，大锤、大刀和双手大剑是一周目开荒首选武器，尤其是前两者，非常强势。护甲方面还是推荐重甲，虽然重量较高，提供的架势和防御也高，可以提高容错。

本来应该给个不推荐的，但是考虑到15块入手的，这个价格下，游戏自然没话说。

I absolutely love Salt and Sanctuary; it’s one of my favorite games. Playing the first game was a fantastic experience; it reminded me of Dark Souls 1, but in 2D with a Metroidvania twist.

Now that I’m playing the sequel, Salt and Sacrifice, I’m pleasantly surprised by how different it is. I expected it to be a straightforward continuation of the series, but it offers a fresh perspective while keeping the core elements intact, like combat, movement, atmosphere, and visuals. 

What’s new is the addition of Monster Hunter elements, which blend really well with the original gameplay. Hunting Mages throughout the map is exciting, and I always look forward to discovering new weapons and outfits from them. Farming bosses is quick and enjoyable too; you can easily teleport back to where you fought the mages and explore the area. It’s rewarding to find new parts of the map or unlock new movement abilities, making the exploration feel fresh every time. The hook ability is especially fun, allowing you to use it on bosses after breaking their stances.

Overall, I’m having a blast playing Salt and Sacrifice. I admire the developers for taking a creative approach instead of just playing it safe with a direct sequel. I hope we see more unique combinations of genres like this in the future, and I’m looking forward to the next fantastic Salt game! 💖

I completely see why this game has such mixed reviews. The first game, Salt & Sanctuary was one of the early Souls-likes and a fantastic example of how to do the souls formula in a 2D style. It really nailed the sense of dread and loneliness that Dark Souls 1 gave me in my first playthrough - walking into an area and thinking 'Oh ♥♥♥♥.' And the light lore that's given is creepy and intriguing. And the level design was excellent, bordering on the metroidvania in terms of how good the exploration and secret hunting was.

Unfortunately, the sequel does away with this and instead gives you a hub area that is far too large for how little it is needed, getting from the blacksmith to the level up statue to the level selection portal is a chore and a chore you'll frequently have to undergo.
Speaking of the level portal, instead of a large interconnected world, you select from a small amount of large-ish levels at the hub and portal into them. Each area is done for good once you've gotten what you need from it. No backtracking for special secrets, or at least, very little.

The bosses. The bosses can be really fun, but it's a shame they changed things up with the mage hunting mechanic. This is the biggest flaw with the game for me. Instead of well balanced and interesting boss fights with their own arenas and vibe, you get a handful of those and a few dozen 'mages'. Roaming bosses that you have to find, smack the ♥♥♥♥ out of and eventually showdown with. It get's repetative after a while but the loot they drop does let you make boss armour and items which is cool.

So why do I recommend it?

It might not be as good as the first one, but the game is fun, and I did finish it. I really enjoyed the optional area and the bosses it had. The potential for broken builds is very good and a lot of fun, I got a boss melting poison build going that took the final boss out for me in one attempt.

If you haven't played the first game, get that instead, it's better for sure. But if you want to try this after the first, bare in mind these criticisms. If you can get past these then you'll have a good time. And it's certainly not bad for £16.75. Runs great on SteamDeck with very little battery usage (great for long trips).

Oh my god this game sucks. It's like they went "hmm copying dark souls worked the first time, let's copy monster hunter this time!" but EVERY big monster hunt is the exact same guy with slightly different attacks. The hub town is needlessly large and annoying to traverse, you need to go back here to level up, instead of being able to level up at any save point, which would be logical, sensible, and not waste the player's time. Basically every single last decision made in the development of this game feels like the wrong decision.

I will say there is some very nice ambient music, although you have to turn down sfx and turn it up to hear it. Also, whoever wrote the little conversations you have with the named mages after you kill them, that person deserves a raise. And..... yeah, that's about it for the good. And, I mean, the game is mechanically sound, it's the same engine as the *vastly superior* first game, obviously.

Now, I could talk for ages about all the things that are bad. Bad balance in equipment, light armor is trash and low equip load is not a big change in combat, just in movement speed... so heavy armor is just so much better. Bad boss fights, which are very repetitive and the mages all respawn randomly so they're in the way all the time. Bad damage scaling with the upgrades, some weapons have really trash upgrade/scaling potential and are just useless. Bad skill point system that locks you into a rather restricted playstyle and doesn't allow an actual full respec to try different things. The annoying hub town, the annoying main shop with a billion things to buy and no sorting options. Boring game design, boring and uninspired enemy attack patterns. Just boring. Repetitive. Way, way too easy. It's just bad. There's lots of other annoying things that if you make the mistake of buying this game, I'm sure you will discover for yourself. A really terrible follow-up to Salt and Sanctuary. Really disappointing. I hope their next game is great, and not like this one.

Salt and Sacrifice, developed by Devoured Studios in collaboration with Ska Studios, is the spiritual follow-up to Salt and Sanctuary, a game that carved out a niche for itself by translating the punishing mechanics and brooding atmosphere of Souls-like titles into a 2D action-platformer. With Salt and Sacrifice, the developers aim higher, attempting to blend the methodical combat and grim fantasy storytelling of its predecessor with elements borrowed from monster-hunting games, such as a loop centered around stalking and slaying corrupted Mages. It’s an ambitious fusion that seeks to deepen both combat and progression systems while maintaining the dark, oppressive tone that defined the original.

The game casts the player as a Marked Inquisitor, a condemned criminal conscripted into service as a hunter of Mages—beings who have twisted themselves and the world around them through forbidden magic. After a brief but brutal introduction, the player is thrown into a grim, deteriorating world teeming with danger. Exploration remains at the core of the experience, with large, interconnected zones filled with secrets, deadly enemies, and environmental hazards. These areas, although distinct in theme, often share a haunting aesthetic of decay and madness, supported by a hand-drawn art style that leans heavily into dark fantasy tropes. While the visuals are sometimes muted and rough-edged, there’s an undeniable charm to the game’s gothic design, which communicates its mood more through tone than polish.

Combat has seen an overhaul since Sanctuary, becoming faster and more layered. The game introduces a stamina-based system that forces players to balance aggression and defense, similar to its predecessor, but also incorporates new mechanics through the use of Runic Arts, ranged tools, and customizable weapons crafted from the remains of slain Mages. These Mages function as both bosses and prey in an elaborate hunt system that’s the backbone of progression. Chasing down a Mage often involves tracking them across various segments of a map, engaging them in short skirmishes before a final, more traditional boss fight. These encounters are chaotic and demanding, and while they offer a sense of exhilaration, the repetitive nature of the chase sequences can wear thin over time. Mages often flee mid-battle, summoning mobs or altering the terrain, which can disrupt the combat’s flow and make encounters feel more like prolonged errands than climactic duels.

The reward for defeating a Mage is the crafting material they leave behind, which can be used to forge weapons and armor themed around their abilities. This creates an incentive to hunt specific Mages multiple times to complete gear sets or unlock new playstyles. It’s a clever integration of monster-hunting progression into the Souls-like framework, and in the early game, it provides a compelling feedback loop. However, the grind required to obtain full gear sets can become tedious, particularly when coupled with the backtracking necessary to re-engage the same Mages repeatedly. The lack of fast-travel options between most areas exacerbates this problem, as players are often forced to run long distances through respawned enemies, making routine farming feel more like a chore than a challenge.

One of the most notable and divisive changes in Salt and Sacrifice is the removal of an in-game map. While this may be intended to increase immersion and encourage players to learn the world organically, it frequently leads to confusion and frustration. The game’s environments are complex and vertically layered, and without even a rudimentary map or compass, it’s easy to get lost or disoriented. This can be particularly aggravating when tracking a fleeing Mage or trying to locate a previously visited area for resource gathering. While some players might appreciate the increased sense of immersion, for many, it comes at the cost of fluidity and momentum, especially during extended play sessions.

Multiplayer is more prominent in Salt and Sacrifice, offering both cooperative and PvP options. Players can summon others to assist with difficult encounters or invade others’ games as hostile entities. While the inclusion of factions and unique gear rewards tied to online activity adds depth, the implementation is not always seamless. Connection issues, unclear summoning requirements, and cryptic faction systems can make engaging with multiplayer content more confusing than rewarding. That said, when it works, co-op can drastically change the feel of the game, turning otherwise grueling Mage hunts into coordinated dances of timing and teamwork. PvP, on the other hand, adds tension and unpredictability, but not all players will enjoy being invaded mid-hunt, especially given how unforgiving the world can already be.

Progression is handled through a sprawling skill tree, where players can invest points into attributes and unlock access to different weapon and armor types. The tree is massive and allows for significant build variety, but it can be daunting to navigate without much guidance. The game assumes a level of familiarity with RPG mechanics and offers little in the way of tutorials or explanations. As a result, early choices can feel overwhelming or even punishing if you realize too late that your character build isn’t suited to your preferred combat style. This is compounded by the limited ability to respec or undo choices, further reinforcing the game’s commitment to trial-and-error learning.

The narrative, while rich in tone and world-building, remains cryptic and fragmented. Lore is delivered in item descriptions, cryptic dialogues, and environmental storytelling, a hallmark of the Souls-like genre. There is an overarching story about the corruption of magic and the decay of the realm, but it unfolds in whispers rather than shouts. Players looking for a linear, character-driven plot will likely be disappointed, but those who enjoy piecing together mysteries through exploration and context will find much to uncover. The atmosphere carries the emotional weight, and moments of solitude in cursed forests or ruined keeps often say more than dialogue ever could.

Technically, the game runs reasonably well, though it's not without issues. Hit detection can sometimes feel inconsistent, especially in chaotic fights involving multiple enemies or environmental hazards. Animations, while serviceable, can appear stiff or abrupt, and some attacks lack the feedback needed to convey impact or danger. Sound design is atmospheric, with unsettling ambient noise and sparing use of music that accentuates the tension and loneliness of exploration. However, voice acting is minimal, and audio levels occasionally feel uneven, especially during fights or NPC interactions.

Ultimately, Salt and Sacrifice is a game that thrives on its atmosphere, combat complexity, and grim aesthetic, but it stumbles in areas of pacing, quality-of-life design, and repetitive progression. It is a bold attempt to evolve the formula established in Salt and Sanctuary, and in many ways, it succeeds—especially in the variety it brings to builds, the scale of its world, and the strategic depth of its Mage hunts. Yet, it also loses some of the focused elegance that made its predecessor so compelling. The Mage chase mechanic, while innovative, becomes a chore when repeated too often, and the absence of mapping or better navigation tools often slows the experience to a crawl. For those willing to persevere through these frustrations, the game offers a dark, challenging, and sometimes beautiful journey. For others, the layers of friction may prove too much to overcome. Either way, Salt and Sacrifice stands as a bold, if flawed, continuation of one of indie gaming’s most unique Souls-like experiences.

Один из худших сиквелов на моей памяти. Полная деградация во всём, начиная с "инновационного" геймплея, где ты мечешься по всей карте чтоб убить босса и заканчивая звуковым сопровождением, которое ну никак не звучит. В первой части всё было практически идеально и если я не ошибаюсь, то Salt and Sanctuary была первопроходцем которая переняла основную задумку Dark Souls'a и поместила её в 2D. У меня просто в голове не укладывается как можно было так облажаться при разработке сиквела, когда формула успеха у тебя перед глазами.

Был забавный момент во время прохождения, даже сначала не понял, что я убил финального босса, настолько локация о себе не кричала, а при битве играла усыпляющая музыка. 

Будто бы эта игра в целом не должна была быть связана с первой частью, настолько тут всё другое (в плохом смысле), даже основная папка игры называется Project Mages. Крайне сомнительный продукт, который не стоит даже рассматривать.

В целом впечатления от игры хорошие. Самый большой плюс по сравнению с первой частью — онлайн-кооп. В первой приходилось играть через Play Together, что с лагами было ужасно (у меня широкоформат, и подключившийся видел картинку сплющенной по горизонтали). Здесь подключение стабильное, пинга не было, лишь пару раз выкидывало, возможно из-за Стима.
Отличий от первой части минимум, непонятны претензии к изменениям. Всё почти так же, есть небольшой даунгрейд в деталях, но не критично.
Из странного или неудобного (СПОЙЛЕРЫ К БИЛДАМ):

1.1) Как и раньше, получаем способности в стиле метроидвании, но рывок и двойной прыжок мы так и не нашли, хотя на картах они есть. Возможно пропустили NPC, но до конца игры их так и не получили.

1.2) Был торговец временными бафами и смолами для оружия, но он появлялся только в сингле. В коопе его не было. Узнали про него ближе к концу игры и постоянно забывали к нему заходить. В итоге я использовал только одну сферу на реген стамины.

1.3) Элементальные гранаты оказались бесполезными. Использовал их только когда заканчивалась мана и стрелы. Прокачка их количества не имеет смысла.

2) У напарника периодически ломался крюк: либо не срабатывал, либо переставал работать. Возможно, это связано с тем, что он был подключённым игроком. У меня как у хоста проблем не было.

3.1.) Пушки тут хоть и очень разнообразные, но 90% из них полнейший мусор. Могу говорить только за мага, т.к. я за него играл, но почти все посохи бесполезны. Из неплохих только стартовый огненный, почти финальный стреляющий писаниями и самый топовый это ледяной. Но тот что падает с библиотекаря дается почти в конце игры, это единственный посох с кд на заклинаниях, первый спелл бьет по прямой, ультовый спелл бьет разовым дождиком по вертикали и по сути самый полезный это второй скилл который бьет много раз и он почти самонаводящийся (если враг стоит далеко или в другой стороны от места где слова начинают слетать с круга и атаковать то они мажут), а так же его удары не бьют через стену\пол. Самый топовый это ледяной, его единственный минус, что атаки происходят с задержкой в полсекунды пока формируются сосульки. Но этот посох имба, сосульки 100% самонаводящиеся если враг в радиусе досягания, даже если он на другом этаже, сосульки бьют раз 5 за выстрел вызывая у врага (даже босса) станлок, сбивая им каст, эффект мороза настакивается, заклинание можно спамить без КД. На хайлвлах у мага много фокуса поэтому пока не опустеет бак можно выстрелить десяток раз, после 2 бутылок еще 10 раз, каждый выстрел по 5 сосулек. Второй скилл плюс к этому выпускает облачко которое постоянно бьет по 1 сосульке. В итоге ты как с пулемета бомбардируешь противника почти безостановочно, некоторые боссы постоянно стоят в стаггере. И плюс того что этот посох дается почти в начале игры. И огромный плюс то что сосульки бьют и наводятся через стену, так что можно бить врагов стоя этажом ниже.

Остальные посохи я вообще не понял зачем нужны, они или создают что-то разрозненное по типу грибного поля которое невозможно попасть таргетно во врага, или бьют строго по горизонтальной прямой, так что если враг даже чуть выше, ты не попадешь. Многие заклинания бьют или в крошечном радиусе, например каменный посох, или вообще бьют вокруг тебя и ладно бы били мновенно, а бьют тоже с задержкой. А подходить близко к врагам на маге такое себе решение.

3.2) Жезлы, которые тратят аммуницию, отличаются мало. Взял ветряной только из-за отталкивания.

3.3) С одеждой тоже всё печально, хотя в Солсах по сути такая же ситуация была, ты одеваешь ровно то на что тебе хватит переносимого веса и чьи статы более менее средние по всем защитам. В остальном просто фэшн солс и одеваешь понравившийся внешний вид, т.к. разницы не так много что ты носишь. Разница есть только по сравнению с совсем голым, а так что тебя убивают за 2 удара, что за 2,2 удара, разницы никакой. Делать какой-то билд для определенного врага тут смысла нет, в 99% случаев боссы тут лёгкие.

4) Боссы простые и прямолинейные. Исключение — 4 головы в DLC. Не убили только опционального Библиомансера: его арена мала, удары книгой занимают почти экран и снимают почти всё ХП, заклинания как конфетти, плюс адды мешают. Все остальные боссы оказались лёгкими.

5) Играть за милика почти невозможно, по крайней мере лёгкого. Мой товарищ-мечник постоянно падал, его сбивали, отбрасывали. Даже с перекатами атаки наслаивались и каждый раз прилетал удар. В то время я спокойно расстреливал боссов магией. Баланс мили плохо продуман.

6) Ковенанты теперь только для онлайна, с сюжетом почти не связаны. НПС в них неинтересные, вещи у них бесполезные, разве что ради внешки. Единственный запомнившийся персонаж — библиотекарь, ставший финальным боссом.

7) Усиленные маги через портал нужны только ради челленджа. Повторять их нет смысла: механики примитивные. Гораздо интереснее было бы сделать постоянное выслеживание любого мага на уровне, как в первой охоте. Сейчас искать их по всей карте утомительно.

Вердикт.
Кому первая игра зашла, с 99% вероятности зайдет и эта, она сделана по сути под копирку, способность изменения гравитации разве что заменена на крюк кошку (к которой еще надо привыкнуть), ну и добавился вертикальный полет на глайдере. были заметны реюзы лелел дизайна, особенно в последней локации, т.к. даже чувство дежа вю появлялось. Но это мелочи.

В целом играть весело, особенно в коопе, игра генерирует уникальные ситуации а ля Солс когда полный звездец происходит или когда на ранних стадиях теряешь много душ, или когда влетаешь в новую комнату, а там какой-то дикий моб оказывается. После середины игры конечно уже пофигу на души становится, т.к. к этому моменту ключевые узлы ты уже прокачал и просто вкидываешься в статы. Игру рекомендую для тех у кого не горит пукан от Солсов.

When deciding on buying this game, please don't think of it as as a sequel to Salt and Sanctuary.
While similar in name, this is a very different game.
As a few people have mentioned, the gameplay loop is more a monster hunter type experience, with a central hub and different areas to visit.
There are a lot of possibilities for Co-op and PvP. Unfortunately due to the low player count, invasions are difficult to come by.
While the hunting of mages can feel a bit repetitive at times, there are still a lot of unique bosses and secrets to find.

If you think it looks interesting, the main story line takes about 20 hours to complete.

Only thing I wish they did better was incorporate the abilities you get more in the combat. At the moment they are mostly used for traversing the world.

As someone who loved the first game ...
Don't buy the second game. 

It lacks the charm and atmosphere of the first game. Instead of exploring neat environments and fighting at least occasionally cool bosses, you are running around what is basically a game lobby with random bosses constantly popping in, spawning minions, fighting each other, fighting the neutral monsters, running away 20 feet, repeating the process, etc. etc. all in a swirl of overlapping low-rez graphics. The game trades all sense of atmosphere, exploration, and curated experience for this constant meaningless churn. It just feels silly; you are supposedly hunting these mages, but really you are chasing them from room to room and back again, like a gaggle of kids chasing a soccer ball. The screen also becomes way too busy way to quickly; as soon as you have multiple bosses on screen you just have to stand back and let them fight it out.

There's brief moments in this game where you're reminded of how fun the first game is; you are a in a new area, it is some god-forsaken tomb a half mile under the ground, there is constant darkness and hideous undead and hidden traps, you can explore for a bit, maybe find some treasure, maybe find a boss, maybe find a spike-laden chasm. It has a real sense of place and atmosphere and horror and excitement. Then it's over and you're back to dealing with the mage spam. 

Other minor quibbles: the UI is kind of bad on PC, there's a lot of random niggling game flow annoyances, many times it is not clear what the stats or equipment are supposed to do. It just lacks anything to carry it beyond the somewhat goofy graphics and weird console UI and bad design.
Долго и упорно я играл, обнов хочется

По ощущениям тут история как с ну… знаете «Рика и морти»? И вы возможно слышали что новые сезоны поносят по сравнению с прошлыми, но вот только если их сравнивать ( лично я ) разницы вообще не увидел и считаю все сезоны нормальными. 
Тут также, игра просто нормальная, но её так ругают на почве сравнения с «первой частью» что удивляешься.

Так-что упомяну основный + и – игры, и затрону возмущения к игре.

«Основные недовольства у игроков»

Баланс и гринд – Я прошёл игру почти всеми билдами и разными стилями. В одиночку, по сети ( тут плохо всё ) и в коопе с другом. И во всех случаях я вообще не фармил или не встречал трудностей и нечестности в игре. Может это была проблема раньше или я такой везучий, но я убивал мага максимум два раза и то только его «именную» версию и «беземянную»( если хочешь плюшки у одного торгаша, надо убивать магов без имени. Это если что мини-задание, а не случайная встреча ) И мне на всё хватало всегда, тем-более каждый маг для своего класса и тебе просто ненужно абсолютно всё с мага. Я в первой части гриндил больше, чем тут ( ебучие жемчужины и соль для мутации под 100к и предметы) А баланс, мне тут нечего говорить. Тут в отличии от первой части каждый маг имеет слабость к определенной стихии и очень сильно от неё отхватывает.( в первой части сопротивление есть почти у всех, я могу назвать только два босса с уязвимостью и то только к свету и тьме) И трудностей боя я вообще не ощущал. 
И да маги мне тоже не мешали ( я про тех самый которые просто гуляют, иногда забавно понаблюдать когда два мага бьют друг друга )

«Плюсы по сравнению с первой частью»

1. Визуал стал приятнее и бегать без шлема стало не так кринжова как раньше. Он стал более живым по сравнению с первой частью. Волосы развиваются во время бега и тд. ( в первой части серость и безнадёга подстать стилю, но тут он другой и логично сделать другие эмоции )
2. Оружие стало больше не смотря на их теперь строгую ветку. Теперь нельзя взять копьё и щит, но зато теперь стало больше эффектов урона и упростили физ урон убрав дробящий и режущий, взамен добавив каждому эфэкт холода, молний, тьмы и тд. Например в первой части оружие со стихиями вообще не было ( если не считать талисманы ), а были буквально пару штук и то они сильно проигрывали по дпс. И ещё зачем-то убрали вес вооружению, ну и ладно.
3. Боссов стало больше ( по ощущению ), как и уникальных, как и магов достаточно добавляют разнообразия.
4. И главный плюс, это кооп. В первой части он был на столько кривым что проще было играть самому. А тут сделали нормальную систему, так-что можно спокойно поиграть с друзьями. 
5. Эмоции ну или анимации. Теперь можно посидеть, пожать руку или обнять других игроков. Мне как человеку который любит посидеть в локации и почилить это вообще подгон свыше. Мне прям нехватоло этого в первой части.

«Спорные моменты»
 
1. Парирование – в игре переработали парирование по сравнению с первой частью, спарировать теперь можно вообще всё. Начиная от простых атак, заканчивая буквально магией. И это крайне легко стало исполнять из-за чего ( при наличии реакции ) ты будешь бессмертным. Ведь при паририровании ты вообще нечего не получаешь, не урона, не урона по стамине( его слишком мало). Причём это можно делать вообще любым оружием с щитом ты просто получишь меньше урона если профукал тайминг и поставил блок раньше. Единственное что не спарировать это захват, но этот приём есть только у избранных, так-что даже так нету проблем с этим.

2. Убрали религии, что по логике упрощает игру ведь нету зависимости от веры и её не надо качать, расходники получаешь там и тут, а уникальные предметы могут купить все. Взамен пришли фракции, которые выглядят интересно, но они также не привязывают тебя к себе. Ты можешь спокойно бегать как за хороших и помогать другим, так-же можешь спокойно устроить пвп за другую фракцию и нечего тебе не будет. За выполнение цели фракции тебе дают жетон, который ты можешь у них потратить и купить доспехи и оружие ( уникальное ). Но представителя фракции надо ещё найти.
3. Нпс стало больше, но взаимодействие осталось прошлым. Также ищи этих придурков на локе чтоб поговорить и пойти дальше. Также есть нпс в хабе, говорить с которыми не о чём. Максимум они скажут «О ты принёс святую руду? Я могу сделать из неё расходник тебе» и всё дальше старые фразы на вопросы. В чём была проблема сделать прогрессию с нпс в хабе побольше? А не банальное «О ты нашёл руны, я теперь могу открыть тебе портал туды». Хотелось более живых болванчиков с своими реакциями на твою прогрессию. Блин, с магами говорить и то интереснее они даже награды дают, вправду только один… но даёт! ( да с магами после победы можно говорить и от них инфы и сюжета больше чем от людей в хабе.)
4. Наказание за смерть. В первой части ты просто терял соль и часть монет, но тут добавили тип «Проклятье» которое снимает часть здоровья и делает невозможным попытку вторжения к другим игрокам. Вернуть человечность могут кристаллы невинности, разбив которые ты перестаёшь быть «одет как чёрт» и можешь снова быть нормальным. 

«Минусы»

1. Система магии, а точнее её ОБЯЗАТЕЛНОСТЬ. Каждый меч или копьё и тд. имеет заклинания мага из которого это оружие было сделано оно делиться на каст от ярости и маны. Но прикол в том что обязательно нужно знать оккультные науки в ветке прокачки чтоб ими пользоваться. Даже если это ярость на воина. Из-за чего чтоб раскрыть полную силу твоего орудия нужна магия, в противном случаи ты будешь махать палкой которая наносит чутка стихийного урона. Из-за чего ты не сможешь отыграть в полной мере за тру воина. Нет ты конечно можешь не качать магию, но ты потеряешь большую часть эффективности твоего меча или молота. За этого всего убивается вся суть билда мага, ведь воин тоже имеет способности как у тебя но взамен он может больно уебать, а что ты? Ну плюс ещё одно заклинание которое скорей всего говно. Вот в первой части магия была хоть и кастрированной, но в разы интереснее где были свои билды. Таже игра с дисбалансом стихий для большего урона и тд.
2. Боеприпасы. Кто ♥♥♥♥♥ придумал сделать их ограниченными!? Я не могу купить 500 стрел и спокойно всех застрелить, мне нужно копать руду и то получать взамен штучек 14 стрел для использования. Закончились? Будь добр идти к «Костру» чтоб это всё восстановить. Это сделано из-за новой системы уникальный расходников ( кадилы и тряпки ), которые при использовании тратят твой боеприпас. Но в чём была проблема сделать как в первой части и дать два использования кадилом который качался бы как в первой части, и чтоб они восстанавливались у «костра». И тем самым вы дали бы жизнь монетам на покупку боеприпасов, а то серебро некуда и так тратить.
3. Фракции. Да этот аспект был в «Спорных», но тут я затрону главную проблему. Онлайн. Его нету и от фракций пропал вообще смысл, ладно некуда вторгаться и тд, но костюмы и «пушки» фракций очень охото, а они бл стоят жетонов. Есть конечно прикол с жетонами в песке и то для обмена на один жетон надо их аж 3. Можно ещё найти жетоны на локах, но это хрень. В чём была проблема продавать их вещи за серебро на которое и так нехрена не купить и они просто лежат в мешках чтоб ты их не просрал. Ещё я молчу о том что цены на некоторые валыны вообще абсурдные за эти ебучие жетоны, что делает их получением на грани фантастики. Если вы хотели эти жетоны оставить то добавили бы фракам задания для одиночек. Например поднасрать где-то на локе или наоборот за дружескую фракцию устранить это подсёр от других. Была бы система как с бутылками где послания, но взамен был бы подсёр на ловушку или монстров. Так был бы приток жетонов и влиние на игроков в других мирах и отыгрыш фраки. 
4. И самый бесячий минус это слоты под персонажей которые убивает новые перепрохождения. Ведь слотов тупо нету, а удалять старых персов жалко. В первой части я имею персов 12 и то место есть ещё на эсперементы с билдом и отыгровкой. А тут это вообще плевок на реиграбельность, я бы отыграл больше часов в эту игру, но места просто бл кончились.

已遊玩15小時，還未破關，職業我依舊是選盜賊(情有獨鍾)，目前還沒試過玩連線，以下是自己的心得淺見
與一代相比：
美術畫風，畫面比一代豐富有層次，人物的捏臉特色有比較鮮明，方尖碑取代了至聖所的祭壇，分布算蠻多的補給和存檔都方便，傳送地圖的方式不需要在擺人偶，地圖第一代比較多隱藏的暗門，二代需要吞噬法師心臟來一邊推進，地圖的機關依樣要拿到特殊道具或是技能才能解開，兩者地圖的設計依樣都很有巧思和創意。
第一次玩一代的時候已經好久以前了，一開始玩印象是很難上手初期打BOOS都是翻滾，搓一刀慢慢磨，到後期越打越輕鬆，玩二代時可能已經有經驗和被其他魂類的遊戲調教過(很自虐的玩家)，感覺比較好上手，除了一開始的劇情殺，初次打BOOS就建立了滿滿的信心。
主線內容，二代的主線劇情比較明確，NPC直接說明下一步要做什麼，比較不會迷失，如果想單純的狩獵法師也可以，BOOS被玩家擊敗後的對話有會讓人感是到人物設計的用心(雖然我沒什麼在看)，人物刻劃比較細膩，內容豐富很多。(第一代玩到二周目還是沒有找到公主XD)

以上全是個人心得感覺，不是專業講評人

E' incredibilmente difficile valutare questo gioco senza paragonarlo a Salt and Sanctuary. E' valido, è divertente, è un buon metroidvania, la caccia ai maghi è una meccanica interessante. Ha alcuni difetti che pesano particolarmente solo perché Salt and Sanctuary non li ha, altrimenti sarebbero assolutamente sullo stesso piano. Diciamo che l'impressione netta è che Sacrifice sia per assurdo quello dei due uscito prima.

E forse è così che va giocato, immaginando di aver recuperato il predecessore di un grande gioco, immaginando che sia "vecchio", allora forse si riesce a sorvolare sopra tutti i vari difettini, ne cito qualcuno ma altre recensioni lo fanno meglio di me:

- L'hub è troppo grande, è NOIOSO girarlo. Poteva essere grande un quarto con tutti i PNG più vicini.
- Dover tornare all'hub per livellare è NOIOSO, perché non lasciarlo fare a qualunque obelisco?
- Il continuo crafting dell'equipaggiamento di base (pozioni, frecce...) è NOIOSO. Si trovano dappertutto questi materiali, non aggiunge niente al gioco doverli raccogliere.
- Il maledetto rampino una volta su dieci non si aggancerà lasciandovi precipitare nel vuoto, anche se siete certi di averlo lanciato al momento giusto. E' FRUSTRANTE.
- La tela per volare una volta su dieci vi farà precipitare dritti attraverso la corrente d'aria che dovrebbe sorreggervi. E' FRUSTRANTE.
- I nemici hanno troppissimo knockback, e lo hanno in troppi. Essere scagliati in giro per la mappa e morire volando in continuazione giù da finestre e balconi è FRUSTRANTE. Specialmente quando potresti salvarti col rampino e quello non parte.
- Il menù del crafting non ha filtri, è FRUSTRANTE cercare ad esempio una mezza lancia, di classe 3+, che scali bene su arcano: DEVI far passare tutti gli oggetti uno per uno per trovarla (se c'è!), e sono letteralmente centinaia.
- A volte i boss decidono che devi morire e basta. Spammano lo stesso attacco imbloccabile tre volte di fila e muori senza poter fare niente. E' FRUSTRANTE.
- I talismani, gli anelli e gli amuleti non fanno quasi niente, non ci si accorge neanche di averli.
- Gli oggetti consumabili sono tutti completamente inutili tranne le pozioni rosse e blu e occasionalmente l'antidoto: la frutta non dà abbastanza vita, quelli che fanno danno non fanno abbastanza danno, le schegge incolpevoli non aggiungono niente al gioco. Conservarli per lanciarli tutti addosso al prossimo boss è una pessima strategia, nessun boss lascia abbastanza respiro per farlo e comunque non ne vale la pena.

Infine, ho trovato i finali molto insoddisfacenti, ma questo è personale.

Non ho giocato multiplayer, potrebbe fare una grossa differenza.

Lo consiglio lo stesso, perché ho giocato fluidamente 40 ore nonostante tutto, è stato divertente e avvincente.Come detto all'inizio però, non si riesce a non paragonarlo a Sanctuary, che è un gioco migliore di questo su tutti gli aspetti.

Quem vier esperando um Salt and Sanctuary 2.0 vai se decepcionar. A mecânica de ficar correndo atrás de magos/bosses é repetitiva e cansativa. As fases se repetem, com os mesmos inimigos e o mesmo mago que você já derrotou dezenas de vezes.

O jogo tem menos conteúdo que o antecessor. O gancho melhorou um pouco a jogabilidade, mas a remoção do dash torna o jogo de plataforma menos dinâmico. Os magos são os únicos “memoráveis”, mas apenas porque você é obrigado a enfrentá-los diversas vezes em locais apertados ou até contra dois ou três de uma vez.

A dificuldade existe, talvez até maior que no anterior, mas vem mais da repetição e do cansaço do que de um desafio divertido. Os chefes “normais”, que não exigem perseguição, salvam a experiência por um tempo, mas logo o jogo volta à rotina de caça aos magos. A segunda metade melhora um pouco, com mais chefes de cenário e menos magos, mas eles ficam mais apelões e repetitivos.

OBS: A ideia das “Cinzas de Guerra” para armas elementais é muito boa e espero que seja mantida em um próximo jogo. Mesmo com a decepção de Sacrifice, acredito que, caso haja uma continuação, esses problemas apontados em várias análises não se repetirão — e eu certamente pretendo comprar o sucessor.

ソウルライクと某ハンティングゲームを混ぜたようなゲームです。

前作同様のゲームと思って購入するとかなり痛い目をみます。
楽しめず、挫折をして途中でやめてしまいましたが、ソウルライクの良さを消すような某ハンティングゲーム要素の足し算です。

魔導士を狩る・武具を育てる・スキルを覚えて進める場所が増える　
という流れにはなると思いますが、
まず魔導士が逃げる・それを追いかける上で道中ソウルライクならではのギミックや雑魚的との戦闘が多々ある。
魔導士も何度も逃げるので追いかける手間もさることながら、雑魚的のガードの強さ・ひるみにくさ・ガード不可攻撃の多さにやきもきさせられます。
しかも、魔導士を何度も倒し、素材を集め、武具を作るので、ひたすら同じボスを追いかけまわし、倒して…
というソウルライクの良さを消す仕様となっています。

新しいジャンルのゲームとして認識しつつ、理不尽さをかみしめながらクリアを目指せる人にはもってこい？かもしれませんが、ソウルライクというジャンルではないと自分は思います。

I loved Salt and Sanctuary. I'm really enjoying my time so far. I think the bad reviews are probably from people who were expecting Salt and Sanctuary 2. This isn't that, not really. More like Salt and Sanctuary mixed with MH or the OG Demon's Souls but set in the same universe.

Edit: I came back to play after a long break and YESSSS it's so good. I don't understand the hate. It's MH Metroidvania and it's so good. Co-op still has some players, I haven't had much trouble getting matched but I'm updating my review in hopes of getting more players. So much stuff to find. If you're on the fence, I say give it a try.

Edit 2: After finishing the game, I still enjoyed it but I have complaints. I probably won't go back and do all the optional content either. Mainly because I didn't realize that beating the game would auto start a NG+ run and I don't feel like doing it all again. But also, some armor sets and weapons are just worse than others? It really just killed my dive to play and try new builds.

おまけ評価

Depois de 80 horas de jogo eu achei necessario refazer minha analise, 
sim eu continuo a recomendar esse jogo pelos seguintes motivos 

1: hunting game, pode nao parecer mas caçar magos e usar partes do corpo dos mesmo para criar armas e armadura com skills unicas pra cada uma acaba tomando bastante do tempo do jogador permitindo customização e diferentes formas de jogar (o mesmo vale para os artefatos) O sacrifice passa a mesma vibe de um Monster Hunter mesmo sendo um metroidvania/souls like, você caça os magos farma items de cura(se achar muito necessario obivio) e busca melhorar os artefatos matando magos de nivel alto.

2: Dlc, a dlc alem da adiçao de bosses novos (por mais que alguns sejam os mesmo do inicio com um redesing) agrega bastante na lore do universo de salt e tem um dos senão o boss mais dificil do jogo junto com a adição do Boss rush de magos que facilita o farm de diferentes items e artefatos pro ng+

3: Lore, coisas simples que eu acabei deixando passar despercebido no Sanctuary sao mostrados no Sacrifice agregando pra lore do mundo em geral, principalmente no final que deixa o jogador com uma pulga na orelha e respondendo certas perguntas que tinhamos no primeiro jogo.

4:coop online, diferente do primeiro jogo o coop do sacrifice é muito gostosinho sem perdas de conexão e volto a apontar pro primeiro ponto do jogo ser hunting game, jogar com um amigo ou simplesmente outro player de determinada covenant permite aproveitar mais das skills e aplicar melhor os efeitos da arma de cada mago, minha gameplay foi baseda em causar o maior numero de hits possiveis enquanto meu amigo lançava os efeitos negativos de suas armas. gameplay muito gostosinha sem sombra de duvidas 

5: Preço, mesmo fora de promo não é algo que pesa muito no bolso pra oq ele entrega 

conclusão: Salt and Sacrifice, vai te divertir pra caralho por boas horas sem sombra de duvidas porem é necessario lembrar "o sacrifice é o dark souls 2 da ska" logo existe muitas mecanicas e a gameplay gostosinha. 
mas não é igual ao primeiro somente a camera do game e ser um metroidvania. recomendo pra todos que curtem hunting game e grindar coisinhas mesmo as mais inutil (sim eu farmei muita besteira nessa porra kkkk)

玩之前看了3-4个避雷视频，抱着赤石的心态来，意外还可以，成就设计的也很简单。
我都做好心理建设，没吃到石好难受啊

简单评价一下：
1.核心玩法——法师狩猎。地图上有很多被封印的门，需要吞噬冠名法师（主线boss）才能打开。法师一共有21个，说是法师，其实能文能武还能召唤。具体的流程就是有气流一样的波动引导玩家，追上去，打法师。法师大概率会招小弟，小概率攻击，法师需要受到一定伤害才会传送到下一个地方（伤害爆发够高法师会直接跑路），大约追逐传送4-5次，就追到头了。法师亮血条，场地封闭开始打boss了，追逐过程中打的伤害越高，法师的初始血条就越短。
每杀死一名法师，可以做它对应的装备武器，戒指护身符匕首（这3个效果都差不多，就是魂的戒指）。法师也会掉强化素材，和3种有词条要刷的饰品（仅通关看，有什么用什么就可以了）。不去刷饰品的话，每个法师杀几次高等级的掉高级强化素材，一点也不肝。
法师狩猎玩法其实还挺自洽的，发现冠名法师，追逐击杀，回家升级看看有什么新装备，每次看新的法师装备都挺开心的。

那问题出在哪呢？
1）法师追逐体验糟糕，这游戏还有野生法师，可能追逐法师的过程中，一堆野生法师乱入，场面乱得和大杂烩一样（虽然冠名/无名法师会和野生法师，地图本身的怪物，只会在狩猎法师中全地图随机出现的怪物（霾蚀者）4方互殴，可以坐山观虎斗）。法师也会不停地招小弟，清怪跑图也挺无趣的，地图跳跳乐上下来回跑（虽然不难）。最恶心的当属卡地形了：比如跳跳乐的终点被小弟们给堵住了，根本跳上不去，有些地板会塌落，失败一次就摔一次再来一次。比如跳跳乐的空中，刚好被有远程能力的法师卡住，跳一次被打下来一次，空中没有保护且还有坠落伤害，位置不对还可能摔死，有种被当苍蝇拍的感觉。
2）法师自己也挺逆天的，技能不带停，还会弹幕饱和式攻击，滚是很难躲开的，只能滚到另一边。个人感觉恶魔法师很恶心，弹幕打完还会残留在地面上限制走位；鲜血法师弃文从武，刺剑一堆突突突人就死了；时间法师减速法阵根本动不了。游戏人物是类魂式，有精力条的，但boss是无限精乱发技能的，颇有法环dlc疯狗boss不对称的美感。不过个人感觉，法师都可以一套伤害直接灌死，所以也就那样，追逐法师的过程中遇到存档点还可以回血补充血瓶，磕血瓶换血过不是很难。
个人主观推测：不知道哪里的攻略带的节奏说护甲都是纸，所以很多人只穿轻甲靠身法，那么脆被怪堆卡地形肯定很折磨，换上重甲真的随便打。从一周目来说，前期穿溺水套装，大地套装，中后期转型成龙套装，化圣套装，升升级，肉的梆梆硬。我穿上重甲，输出拉满随便莽莽，大部分boss都是一遍过。玩魂穿哈维尔大山羊，老哥们我做的对吗？

2.学我者生，似我者死。这游戏魂系好的不学，坏的全学去了。
1）升级是学的魂3，每次升级都要回老家，麻不麻烦。
2）地图是要通过符文排列产生对应传送门的，但除了隐藏地图都是直接给现成组合好的。不算老家一共7张大地图，每张地图没有连接。这不妥妥的恶魂拱心石吗？大地图之间无法相互传送，每次都要回老家，跑一小段路，选择地点开传送门，再进去，很繁琐。
3）里面第一个地图探索道具就是钩锁，只狼无疑是了。这2代抛弃了传统类银通用的工具打开，冲刺，二段跳等常用设计，本意不错。但是这钩锁对于注重垂直方向利用的2d游戏，真没有3d游戏（只狼）那么震撼。比如一个高台，传统是用工具打开或者二段跳上去，这游戏是钩上去的，差不太多。
4）学魂没有地图，怎么不学法环。在2d类银游戏里，没有地图体验是灾难的。这游戏有上锁的门和需要吞噬法师心脏才能解锁的门，你没有地图，我怎么还能记得住是哪些地方？尤其是有些门的钥匙在后面地图或者最开始没找到后面查漏补缺，没有地图标记，还要人浪费时间去瞎跑。（幸亏指南里有大佬做的，带重要项目标记的地图）
5）地图传送点过少，听说最开始就没有。这学的是魂1拿到王器前无传送，还是恶魂开头一个结尾一个根本起不到用处的传送点？
6）武器装备属性展示/道具作用，在装备/仓库界面无法显示。比如拿到新道具默认装备在道具栏里，但是在道具栏没法直接看到效果，得点进去找到看。比如在仓库里比较哪把武器攻击高，装备防御高，都没法直接看，还要拿出来看。就很麻烦。

不过，就个人而言，上述的大部分问题都能克服，还是玩魂培养出的耐心，很难有游戏能破防。玩起来非但没有赤石，反而有些爽。管他法师花里胡哨的，还是被我无脑莽穿，真是数数又值值。大部分感觉1代神作，2代垃圾。我个人反而是感觉1代很僵硬玩着难受，2代爽杀。

Played this game in EGS years ago.

There's no sugar coating it, Salt & Sacrifice got [i]issues[/i]. 

[h1]The Good[/h1]
✔️ Visual presentation
✔️ Varied combat moves
✔️ Mage farming is fun
✔️ Online multiplayer

[h1]The Bad[/h1]
❌ Devs decided to make Valley Herbs (et al) entirely redundant
❌ Ore Decoctions are, um, completely useless
❌ Shoddy balance and enemy placement
❌ Dead online multiplayer

[h1]Easy Fix[/h1]
👉 Remove hardcoded Valley Herb, Irona Ore, Haze Spiral and Wispleaf drops from enemies
👉 Hearthen Flasks should be replenishable on-the-go at a 3:1 or 5:1 rate (same goes for Haze Decoctions and Antidotes)
👉 Phlogiston Decoction should be seriously reviewed as it's currently strictly worse than Firebomb (same goes for Frostvein Decoction), while also increasing base quantity of all Ore Decoctions from 3 to 5 
👉 Throwable weapons (Deep Pods, Flasks of Judgment and Luminous Spheres in particular) should be significantly buffed in terms of dmg/scaling or entirely reworked (see Mechanum Bombs), while also changing the Thrower skill from Strength to something else (e.g. Conviction, Vitality or Luck)

These are only the most glaring issues, mind you. 

Normally, I'd turn to modding and call it a day. Unfortunately, publicly available modding tools are currently incomplete, so there's only so much one can reasonably do to fix things.

[h1]Conclusion[/h1]
Salt & Sacrifice got charm. It's got a lot going for it (combat, worldbuilding, gameplay loop, engaging multiplayer interactions). On many levels, it's "the better game" compared to its predecessor (Sanctuary). Sadly, it feels like all this amazing potential has been squandered by questionable design choices (especially post-release).

Whether you play Sanctuary or Sacrifice, do yourself a favor and check out [b]Nexus Mods[/b].

[hr][/hr]
[h3][Feedback] Salt and Sacrifice – Uma análise completa após platinar o jogo[/h3]
Após cerca de 50 horas de gameplay e finalmente conquistar a platina, gostaria de compartilhar minha análise geral sobre [i]Salt and Sacrifice[/i]. O jogo tem pontos fortes, mas também traz escolhas de design questionáveis — algumas herdadas do primeiro jogo, outras novas.
[hr][/hr]

⚔️ Mecânica de Combate: O Parry Faz Falta
Um dos elementos mais marcantes que senti falta foi o [b]parry[/b], que foi substituído pela mecânica de [b]perfect block[/b]. Em teoria, o funcionamento deveria ser parecido, mas na prática é extremamente [b]ineficiente e pouco intuitivo[/b]. A resposta visual ao acertar um perfect block é quase imperceptível — muitas vezes você nem sabe se acertou ou não. E diferente do parry, ele [b]não abre o inimigo para uma finalização[/b], apenas acelera o stagger invisível. Isso enfraquece o feedback do combate e dificulta a imersão.
[hr][/hr]

🌿 Árvore de Talentos: Complexa, Mas Frustrante
A árvore de talentos pode parecer impressionante à primeira vista, com dezenas de opções de evolução. No entanto, ela [b]se resume a atributos básicos[/b] como força, destreza, vitalidade e proficiência em armas. Embora visualmente expansiva, a árvore oferece pouca profundidade estratégica real.
Além disso, a [b]progressão por proficiência de arma[/b] é um retrocesso em relação a sistemas mais flexíveis como o de [i]Dark Souls[/i]. No Salt, para usar uma greatsword, você precisa desbloquear o [i]node[/i] específico (de 1 a 5). Não importa se você tem 180 de nível — [b]sem o node certo, não pode usar a arma[/b]. Isso limita a liberdade de experimentação e build diversity.
Pior: [b]trocar de build é praticamente impossível[/b]. Não existe uma forma real de resetar a árvore. A cada 5 níveis, você recebe [b]1 único ponto de redistribuição[/b]. Ou seja, é um sistema rígido que desestimula testar coisas novas.
[hr][/hr]

🔥 Pontos Positivos – Combate, Estilo e Sistema de Magos
Apesar das falhas, o jogo tem suas qualidades:
[list]
 [*]O [b]estilo gráfico[/b] continua agradável e coerente com a proposta.
 [*]O novo sistema de [b]skills nas armas[/b] (usando mana ou fúria) é divertido e dinâmico.
 [*]O [b]sistema de crafting[/b], baseado nos drops dos Magos, é bem interessante — sempre gera expectativa sobre quais sets e armas serão desbloqueados após cada nova caçada.
[/list]
O problema é que esse sistema [b]praticamente eliminou os drops normais[/b]. Em mais de 50 horas, dropei apenas umas 5 armas e nenhum equipamento. Isso tira um pouco da emoção do grind.
Por outro lado, o sistema de [b]Relíquias[/b] é muito bom! Existem relíquias ofensivas, defensivas e utilitárias com atributos aleatórios — e como fã de RNG, adorei. [b]O problema? O sistema de upgrade das relíquias não funciona.[/b] Literalmente: às vezes você faz o upgrade e ela fica pior do que antes.
[hr][/hr]

🏆 A Platina – Fácil, Acessível e Não Punitiva
A platina do jogo é surpreendentemente tranquila. Minhas 50 horas foram porque explorei tudo com calma, mas se você seguir um guia, dá pra platinar em [b]10 a 15 horas[/b]. As conquistas são majoritariamente obtidas naturalmente, jogando.
[hr][/hr]
[h3]🎮 Conclusão[/h3]
[i]Salt and Sacrifice[/i] é um bom jogo, especialmente se você é fã de metroidvanias. Ele tem decisões questionáveis e sistemas que poderiam ser muito mais bem aproveitados — mas ainda assim, entrega uma experiência sólida e divertida. Recomendo, mas com ressalvas.

I desperately wanted to love this game since I had beaten the first game back on my PS4 and was ecstatic to hear about a sequel, however my love for this game was met with extremely infuriating game mechanics. 

The main selling point of the game was the mage fights, I got really excited this since the concept, on paper, sounded good, in practice it's the most infuriating part of the game since I have to constantly look for where they are with a very easy to miss pointer since the game is already pretty dull colored adding in an ever duller pointer just makes it harder to find em. 

But what happens when you do find the mage? You'll be experiencing the most hair pulling gameplay since every time they warp somewhere they'll summon their minions, and their minions can just dogpile you over and over again until either you kill enough of them or you damage the mage enough for them to warp somewhere else.

So after damaging a mage to half health you'll then fight them one last time as a genuine boss fight, health bar and all, you'd think this is where the gameplay would get better, right? Somehow no, because they share a similar problem with every other enemy in this game, high aggression mixed in with high attack speed coupled with extremely high damage output and to top it all off, with the horrible knockback mechanic, they can knock you down and then combo you with unavoidable damage that can either out-right kill you or leave you in a such a state that you might as well let em kill you.

I don't know how they manged to mess it up since in the first game they nearly perfected it, sure certain bosses and enemies were actual garbage to fight but they were so few and far between all the other amazing bosses and enemies that genuinely challenged you. Here it just feels like an endurance test to see how much ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ you can deal with before putting a hole through your monitor or breaking your controller in half. 

It's even sadder since the Studio is still improving the first game but it seems they've just abandoned this one with little to no care, sure they had that giant update that adds even more content but it's lack of care for the difficulty that just killed it for me. There's some genuine fun to be found here it's just buried under some of the most unbalanced game design I've played so far, and hopefully I'll be proven wrong with an update or something but I just don't think it'll ever happen. 

Don't spend your money on this, just go get Salt and Sanctuary and enjoy that one, you'll find it to be actually fun and engaging and not at all infuriating to deal with.

My wife and I sank 14 hours into Salt and Sacrifice, and for a while, we were convinced we’d found the perfect co-op Soulslike follow-up to Salt and Sanctuary. The combat? Excellent. Tight, weighty, satisfying. We were having a blast carving through mobs, exploring the beautifully miserable world, and working together to take down horrific mage bosses. What could go wrong?

Well, the actual game design, it turns out.

The central mechanic is built around “named mage hunts.” To progress, you need to be on an official hunt. That’s right—not just find a boss and beat it—no, no. You have to apply to murder them first. If you stumble across one of these monstrosities and defeat it without starting the formal hunt? Congrats: you just wasted your time.

So picture this: you clear an area, fight your way past traps, annoying respawns, and multiple grueling boss fights. You finally kill a major boss—and the game says, “Oops, that one doesn’t count. You weren't on the clock.”

We killed four different bosses like this. Four. That’s a lot of controller-clenching effort to be met with a shrug.

The result is that progression becomes a cruel joke. You'll spend half your playtime backtracking through zones you’ve already explored, desperately trying to find a hunt trigger so that this time, your suffering will actually matter. There’s no graceful flow from one zone to the next, no sense of rhythm—just endless interruptions and invisible hoops to jump through.

We hit a point where game night became more about getting annoyed than getting anywhere. So we stopped. Not because the enemies were too tough or the mechanics too complex—but because the game seemed to actively resent us for trying to play it.

In short: Salt and Sacrifice has great bones, but it wraps them in a frustrating, opaque, progress-throttling loop that made us want to uninstall rather than persevere. We came for the co-op Soulslike experience and stayed just long enough to realize this one needed more time in the cauldron.

Compared to the first game, I think visuals have vastly improved, especially since it's not as drab and extremely dark anymore (to the point of inhibiting gameplay in the first game). The mage hunts (and mage bosses in general) are a pretty nice mechanic, and the "normal" bosses are mostly good. My biggest problem is build variety : while the addition of runic arts (think AoW from elden ring/ Weapons arts from ds3) is definitely nice, I'm not the biggest fan of how this (essentially) replaces the spells from the first game. However, we get a pretty nice ranged weapon mechanic and plenty of consumables to padd it out, so the loss isn't too great. 

Other problems I had were the fact you constantly get thrown around by nearly every enemy attack unless you're wearing heavy armour, flying enemies clipping through walls or being just outside my ranged arc of fire, and enemies often camping on the sides of platforms, making climbing on impossible (due to said volleyball effect). 

The lore... if you're looking for a (obvious) connection to the first game, you'll probably be disappointed. However it is told to you (mostly) more directly which is pretty nice, though I didn't feel what the implications of both ending choices were even after making them.

Would recommend to souls/hollow knight etc. fans, but who am I kidding, anyone who wants to play this probably has over 200 hours in that type of games anyways (otherwise idk how you came here)

What an absolute disgrace of a "sequel". If I wanted to play Monster Hunter, I'd play Monster Hunter. I want to play a proper sequel to Salt and Sanctuary, and this ain't it, chief. Everything that made the original great is gone. The character progression is simplified, as is the combat system, melee and ranged weaponry, and magic also. What it became instead is a blatant grindfest and pointless running around the map after the "mages". The latter are supposed to be the bosses of the game, but nothing about them that matters is unique. They're nothing like the bosses in the original that were memorable and creative. Here it's just a bunch of giant dudes in varying types of rags who have 5 or so attacks.

To make the game more "engaging" the developers have turned it into a numbers game. It's no longer a soulslike, but a very straightforward RPG in which your equipment and stats do most of the work for you. Only the game doesn't tell you what level you should be to take on this or that "boss". So it could be that you're chasing a "nameless mage" around a map for 15 minutes only to die to him during the actual showdown, in one hit wearing upgraded heavy armor.

I've never played any Monster Hunter games, but it being a very beloved series I'm sure it's leagues better than this in what amounts to its core game loop and main selling point. To me it seems like the developers liked Monster Hunter and in their infinite wisdom decided to asset flip the original Salt and Sanctuary into a poor copy because "wouldn't that be cool". No. No, it wouldn't.

Everything in this game is secondary to that aforementioned game loop, and for the love of me I cannot understand why it had to be so. What's the point chasing the idiot "mages" around the map if in the end you still need to fight them in a very small, flat and artificially narrowed arena? It gets old in the very first level of the game already after the first time you've done it. It's wasting your time in more ways than one.

Technically the point of this Benny Hill charade is to farm crafting mats to get gear that you then use to fight other bosses. Okay, fine, so far so good right. In its very basics perhaps, but once you get into the very fray it all falls apart by the time you reach the third level of the game. There's 8 upgrade levels to every piece of equipment. Bosses have elemental weaknesses and resistances and do elemental damage themselves. Basically you're incentivized to have at least 6 specialized sets of armor and weapons to cover all your bases in that regard. This is where the mindnumbing amount of grinding comes in. Drops are of course randomized, so prepare to spend dozens of hours farming upgrade materials. They differ by elements too by the way. So even more grinding.

The next major issue is that different types of weapons have different types of elements, and not all bosses can be used to craft the weapons that you use. I was using katanas and throwing knives and after defeating a dozen bosses I only had 4 of them. It's impossible to have a level 5 katana with every element, I'm sorry, but why not? Diablo II had this 25 years ago, but you can't have it?

Again, none of this would be an issue if it were a soulslike like the original, but it's not. The point of the game is to optimize equipment, and you can't do that because you'll be focusing on one weapon type, maybe two as weapons are stat dependent. To add insult to injury, armor is also divided into light and heavy, meaning half the time you won't be getting new armor from a boss. What a clown show.

It's like this game wants to have its cake and eat it too. It wants to be Salt and Sanctuary, but also be its complete opposite. A lazy opposite as well. Spells are boring and are weapon dependent. Most weapons feel the same and are not fun to play at all. The projectile weapons are all literally the same, and the only thing they differ in is how fast you can use them. I chose knives because you can spam them. That was the sole consideration. Also they took out guns from the game. Why? What did the guns do to you? Are you some kind of a gun hating liberal? Brrrrrrrr...

I have no idea how this franchise went from one of the best games I've ever played to one of the worst in just one game. What were they thinking? Salt and Sanctuary had an identity. It had clarity of vision. It had amazing gameplay and atmosphere and was extremely well designed from start to finish. On the contrary Salt and Sacrifice feels like an undead impostor wearing the skin of the original. It's dead inside, and I feel dead inside after giving it 20 hours of my time, give or take.

I'm glad I bought it on a very generous sale, as I've definitely gotten my money's worth out of it, but on the other hand I've been left feeling profoundly disappointed and befuddled by having played it. Engage only out of morbid curiosity, and on deep sale alone. Salt and Sacrifice is hereby bestowed upon with my highest [b]game undesign[/b] seal of disapproval. What an absolute shame.

Salt and Sacrifice игра, которая показывает почему гомосексуальные отношения это плохо. Да, я украл эту шутку из ролика ixbt, но черт возьми в данной шутке лишь доля шутки. 
Первая часть Солевых приключений была реально классной игрой: да были минусы, да неидеальна, да Человеческое дерево - но вот была в ней душа, которую я увидел и мне нравилось играть. Вторую часть я ждал, пытавшись не обоссаться под себя, ибо я когда увидел, что в игре будет нормальный кооператив... ну тут просто было взрывоопасное возбуждение. Однако беда не приходит одна: релиз в Ebik Games... ну ладно мы уже хавали это с Даркестом 2 и Хейдесом, но вот че-то меня дернуло купить игру на зеленом сайте под определенным парусом. Пожалуй это было одно из самых умных решений в моей жизни: я пытался 2 раза игру пройти и дропал со словами: "Кал". Но тут я решил разделить "удовольствие" со своим другом и спасибо ему огромное, без него я бы никогда не прошел этот игросодержащий продууууууукт.
С чего начать: ну с боевки - как по мне упростили, но особой разницы по сравнению с первой частью я не заметил, да заджаглить теперь досмерти не получится ну и пусть. Зато теперь все персонажи маги, потому как в оружии есть руны - это ваши спелы, и оно есть во всех оружиях... во всех... 
Про оружие получилось типикал ситуация как в соулсах: есть пушки, которые с боссов делаются, а есть оружия из ковенантов (и они реально мощные). С оружием из ковенантом мне не оч понравилось, в первой части на моей памяти такой вещи не было - любое оружие можно затрансмутить из душ боссов либо каких-либо расходников.
Визуал стал каким-то мультяшным, но да ладно я не Ваномас и в целом на графон мне все равно. Но есть такое послевкусие в виде белой вязкой жижи во рту от визуала 2й части, ведь визуал 1ой части был куда мрачнее и запоминающее.
Платформинг... Я блин не люблю метроидвании, и в первой части с платформингом было все плохо - считаю что это было самой слабой частью игры. Тут же... платформинг просто отвратительный. Я играл на клаве и мне было +- ок, но частенько тупорылый крюк-кошка не регал, а вот эти трусы на которых летаешь это просто такой рак, что я хз кому вообще это дерьмо нравится. Me apossando da fala do texugo de pica-pau, eu expresso meu sentimento ao primeiro jogo: Salt and Sanctuary.
"Eu já disse... nada supera, nada chega tão perto da perfeição! Quantas vezes eu vou ter que dizer? Põe isso na cabeça! É o melhor! É a minha grande paixão."

So I have returned and beaten the game. I'm going to go over what I do and don't like etc. yada yada. 

I will note I have a max level character, gotten all but one achievement related to the ending between two choices, and last I farmed the covenant items offline as sadly the multiplayer is pretty dead.This is also in a random order I'll mention what i disliked most in the write up.

The Do's
------------
#1. Color palette and art design is top notch. I love the designs more than in Sanctuary. The colors pop, background objects are well defined, rarely repeats too often, aesthetic wise clothes and armor feel more visually appealing. especially the royal outfit.

#2. The story is very very good. It is a prologue which at first wasn't very clear to me. That being said I won't spoil anything but suffice to say I enjoyed the new lore and am happy that I saw it all for myself. What was funny was I didn't realize we were elves well Sylvann and was wondering why my ears looked so off.

#3. I'm glad we have a designated hub zone where in ng+ the covenants stay around you don't have to find them all again. I also appreciate that you can farm the tokens for covenants because the multiplayer is sadly mostly dead. You get an invader here and there, maybe.

The Do Not's
------------
#1. The combat system. First i find the limited access to ranged compared to sanctuary both annoying and sad. In sanctuary we had guns, bows, crossbows, and magic. You could change ammo, had quite a few spells which were assigned to the hot bar. In Sacrifice you lose all of that mostly.

You get designated spells for certain staves. So no more customization you better hope to have what you need available in what staves you have on hand or get good at quick swapping. No guns no gun-blades either.

Bows and crossbows get a certain number of shots and can be refilled to a max of 30 from iron ore. keep 30 on hand and get more laying around harvesting stuff. I just find it silly to more or less kill range builds. You might ask why this change? We'll get to that.

Juggle and stagger even in max heavy armor with poise capped out almost and you'll still be staggered left and right. Sometimes they'll keep you in a juggle till death because the game has almost no iframes or reprieve period. And because almost every boss is spamming AoE or using very wide sweeping attacks and blocking is useless you better get good at either rolling or parrying as that's it. Shields feel very gutted compared to sanctuary.

#3. I'll just say it here most hated of the "Do Not's"
------------
The game wanted to be a Monster Hunter clone so bad. Look I love MH: Worlds (haven't played Wilds yet) it's an awesome game in it's own genre. It is not a souls like game however. The games first iteration was an awesome 2D souls like. it had charm, it was gritty, fun builds, etc.

Sacrifice added Mage Hunts. They summon adds non-stop, they will teleport to what I call 4 "fake arenas" where you smack them around, they summon adds, and they teleport away those 4 times before finally becoming vulnerable.

You see the issue is this game has a ton of climbing and verticality to it. So getting staggered/thrown off, and chasing something around and fighting all the maps adds and mages summons only to say get bonked off and die to fall damage is very very annoying. It does't sound fun when every boss fight is a Micolash from Bloodborne mixed with a horde out to get you.

These mages are essential to the story. Sadly another issue is there are close to 20 or so of them and they are all the same thing just different skins based on various elements, monsters, etc. You have to hunt them down for gear too! And just like MH you have to keep farming them if you don't get what you want. Oh also other mages will show up and summon stuff too. So have fun. Absolutely the worst addition to the game. They also make up like 98% of the boss roster which got very repetitive very quicky.

#4.
------------
Map design is confusing more than the first title and it's also no longer one interconnected world it's all sectioned off via a gate I'm guessing this is more similar to Demon Soul's (never played it) where you pass through these gates to get to different large areas. I found it rather boring I also did not care for obelisk system as it was like bonfires but only certain ones act as travel points and honestly I miss the shrines but I'm not sure how it would work with a hub world.

#5.
------------
Some of the weapon abilities are downright trash and others are really really good. I also found that if you aren't using a weapon with good runes or abilities you are better off using pure strength weapons because you can actually stagger back and keep them at bay a little. I found this to be limiting and I missed using one handed weapons and they're too short unless I'm hugging them.

The Over All
------------
While I loved the look of the game I can't say I'd ever recommend this title to anyone. I want to like it I really do but the limitations versus Sanctuary's arsenal, the enemies all staggering/juggling you constantly even with heavy armor/high poise, the incredibly repetitive and boring mage hunts you have to do (btw thy're also nameless versions to do too enjoy the same boss fight againx40), the grappling hook being finicky or the weird sail thing causing me to fall despite activating it in the air stream causing me to die, the lack of a interconnected world.

Over all I'll lower my score due to how bad and how repetitive the mage hunts were. I give it a 5/10 instead of a 6. This games is maybe average and sadly I 'd only ever get this if it was very very cheap or you are a die hard fan of Salt games by Ska Studio.

La chasse des mages (qui font office de boss) auraient pu être intéressante, mais sur le long terme ça en devient lourd. Les mages utilisent des patterns plus ou moins similaire si on oublie l'éléments qu'ils maîtrisent.

Eu gostei do jogo, mas sinto que tenho mais a reclamar dele do que elogiar. Eu poderia criticar a semelhança com o anterior, mas a ideia é essa mesmo, então não tem porque reclamar, um único ponto nesse sentido seria as animações e barulhos que os inimigos fazem, alguns inimigos fazem literalmente os mesmo sons que inimigos parecidos no Salt and Santuary, alguns fazem até a animação de combate, o inimigo é claramente diferente, mas se porta igual ao do jogo anterior. 

Outro ponto negativo é a dificuldade do jogo, ele em geral muito mais fácil que o Salt and Santuary, os únicos chefes que oferecem algum desafio são os da área extra, e como você pode passar pelo jogo sem nem entrar nela, achei meio desperdiçado. Mas se você explorar o jogo ao todo, você vai conseguir achar algum desafio nela, tanto em mobs normais, quanto em chefes. Até o chefe final que deveria ser o mais difícil ou pelo menos perto disso, você consegue derrotar na primeira tentativa, decepcionante levando em conta o chefe do primeiro jogo, o Nameless God mesmo não tendo uma dificuldade extrema oferece um bom desafio para o jogador.

O farm de magos acho desnecessário, os magos inomináveis e magos fadados, você consegue zerar o jogo sem nem lutar com eles. O incentivo é tão pouco que no máximo você vai lutar contra eles duas vezes para pegar os itens e depois só passa correndo por eles. Poderia ser igual Dark Souls, você ganha a alma e faz um item com ela, os outros, armadura, etc, você consegue de outra forma. Com o dinheiro por exemplo, outra coisa que você nem usa no jogo, quando você morre, acaba nem percebendo que perdeu ele porque nem faz muita diferença.

Mas no geral o jogo é bom, ele é parecido com o Salt and Santuary, mas tem suas diferenças como os magos e mais agilidade na movimentação pelo mapa. Salt and Santuary era bom, e esse jogo é parecido com ele, então não tinha como ficar ruim, mas tem os pontos negativos que foram destacados acima.

So where do I start?

I wanted to like the game but, my experience has been mixed at best sadly Steam doesn't let me post a mixed review so I have to give the game the short end of the stick.

The combat feels slow and clunky, you get ragdolled from left to right even with lots of poise I had normal enemies throw me around or guardbreak me frequently.
In fairness I gave up parrying and guarding quite early in my playthrough, the reason for that is that most bossfights felt like a dps race kill them before they kill you.

The mage mechanic was probably the most enjoyable thing in this game, there is the fun in chasing you enemy into a corner and observing it fistfighting the whole map in the chase until you can finish it off.
The normal bosses though felt like a random enemy spawn most of the time I always wondered if the giant open area would be a boss arena or not, also some boss placement felt off like in the end area having two bosses in random corridors instead of the giant open area or cathedral like room felt wrong.

The NPCs were fine if we ignore the multiplayer NPCs and the typical merchants, the rest felt ok for me I forgot about them most of the time though because after checking for new dialogue or some kind of reaction to anything for like the tenth time I stopped.

So the story, I was intrigued at the start and was really invested in the middle but the end portion let me down, I could chalk it up to the cryptic story telling or the vague dialogue but in the end it lacked impact for me to care even though i want to know more about the world, the mages and the whole plot.

I may have listed a lot of negative things and sure this review will be negative but I still got enjoyment out of this game I just felt like it could have been better, but in the end I still is an ok game in my eyes even I probably won't play it again for a while.

This game is so garbage.

A lot of the negative reviews are just people comparing this to Salt & Sanctuary (the first game), when this one is obviously meant to be its own thing, even though both take place on the same universe.

I’ve played both, and honestly, they both do some stuff better than the other. I like certain aspects of Sanctuary more than Sacrifice, and vice-versa. But comparing them just feels kinda pointless to me since this isn’t trying to be a sequel.

Anyway, let’s get the bad stuff out of the way first in case any of these are dealbreakers for you.

First up: the game gets kinda linear for two whole worlds.
In the first world, you’ve got all this freedom (after beating the first boss and getting the grappling hook), you get to pick between two mages to hunt, and after killing one, two more show up, and so on. There’s a lot of choice in the order you do things, and I loved that. You can even kill all the named mages and a few nameless ones before moving on, and there are areas you’ll have to come back to after getting tools from the next worlds. The only small gripe here is that the nameless Venomancer is locked behind world 4, so you can’t get all the nameless tokens for world 1 mages right away. But whatever, that’s minor compared to what happens in worlds 2 and 3.

Worlds 2 and 3 straight-up force you into a set order. Even if you rush to world 3 super early, get the tool, and go back, you still have to fight the named mages in the order the game wants. Same deal with world 3. After that, though, you can do pretty much any order after killing one of the 2 first available world 4 mages and world 5 doesn't give a ♥♥♥♥ they're just chill like that (they're not). So yeah, it feels like the game teases you with open progression, then takes it away for a while, which kinda sucks.

Second: getting flung around.
You’ve probably seen this in other reviews. Hell, the top review right now lists this as a reason not to recommend the game. So I won’t ramble too long on this, but yeah knockback can be rough. A lot of enemies have moves that knock you back, up, or down, and if you get knocked back near a cliff, you’re probably gonna eat a fall unless there’s something to grapple onto and you’re already mashing dodge like a maniac. Plus, the level design loves verticality so falls are big. Bosses do this too, and if you’re wearing light or no armor, they’ll juggle you with multi-hit attacks which is a huge problem due to the next negative (well not for me since I use heavy armor, unlike those light armor nerds).
Also the Aeromancer exists.

Third: difficulty spikes.
There’s a pretty sharp spike around the latter half of world 2. Enemies start hitting harder, attacking faster, covering huge areas, and they attack non-stop. Bosses do it the most, but later on, even regular enemies start pulling this stuff., well, later if you don't count mage minions as regulars that is. World 2 standouts for me were the Thaumamancer and Sanguimancer (also the temple entrance boss if you can't perfect-block).
Also no aerial blocking, because apparently the world 3 tool is so awesome it had to replace it (it isn't).

Now, is the game still worth playing with all that stuff?
Yes.

The multiplayer is my favorite part. You can play couch co-op, online co-op, or call for help from the Dawlight Cooperators. But be warned as calling for help can attract the Blueheart Runners, Chaos Feasters, or even worse, Shroud Alliance members. But don’t worry too much, because sometimes Sheriffs and Oathbound Watchers show up to help you and your dawnlight buddy fend them off. It’s super cool and makes the world feel alive. I just wish there were more players so I could ♥♥♥♥ with more people.

The weapon system is also really damn good. You can chain combos together if you know what you’re doing, and it’s fun messing around with different move sets, improving combos, and pulling them off on harder bosses. (tip: try holding up or down while using lights and heavies) The special weapons are awesome too, not just to get, but to figure out.

And finally, the character building.
It’s just great. You can end up with wildly different builds depending on how you play, and it’s perfect for doing multiple runs or challenge runs if you’re into that.

Do the positives outweigh the negatives? Hell if I know, but I do have a lot of fun and I'd say this is one of my favorite games even without multiplayer.

Im a huge fan of the original Salt n sanctuary, and yes Salt n Sacrifice is pretty different in terms of gameplay and mechanics, but it still has alot of the same charm. And is def a really good game. Also it seems like it has implemented some of the "monster hunter" mechanics into the game (but where you hunt mages instead) which was pretty fun to begin with, but became abit repeditive near the end, and i would def have prefered more lore and exploration instead. 

Anyways, It seems that the Devs put heavily weight on Multiplayer features with this one, and you have both invasions, pve, pvp, covenants, fight clubs, etc. which i personally find very cool, except from the fact that there are not nearly enough players around to do random invasions etc (atleast not these days). 몬스터 헌터의 보스가 도망치는것을 다크소울의 필드에서 따라가세요(비료옥, 섬광탄 없음)

닼소와 몬헌을 섞은 게임. 필드와 전투, 세이브 시스템 등은 닼소를 따라가고 멀티와 보스 추적사냥, 재료파밍 시스템은 몬헌에서 가져왔다. 나름 재밌을뻔했는데 문제는 보스가 도망을 친다는 점이고 두세번쯤 추적하면 사냥이 끝나는 몬헌과 달리 여기서는 맵 전체를 헤집으면서 텔레포트하고 잡몹을 계속 소환하며 그렇게 약 6번정도 추적해야 겨우 보스룸으로 이동한다는 점이 다르다. 

작년 에픽 선공개 출시때는 여기에다가 다크소울의 무자비함까지 섞여 그야말로 게임 이하였는데 격변수준의 편의성과 난이도 하향 패치로 가끔 짜증나지만 재미는 있는 게임 정도로 올라왔다.

2인 멀티를 지원하니 같이할 친구가 있다면 이 게임의 단점들이 꽤 희석되어 상당히 재밌게 즐길 수 있다.

[h1]4.5/10 法师与遛狗模拟器[/h1]

优点：
1.地图设计很好，四通八达的捷径与有限的篝火点构成了环形相通的地图，绕了一圈绕回起点的感觉真的很不错。除了勾爪的判定很迷之外攀爬跳跃手感甚至很不错。

2.武器与属性的设计完爆一代，这一代可供搭配的武器与能力也多了不少，并且几乎每把武器都有自己的战技，投掷武器与法术也不是装饰品，而是实打实的可以弥补伤害甚至当做主力输出的手段。

3.隐藏支线与重要收集虽然还是藏得有点离谱，但这次有了主城NPC的提示，好找了不少。

缺点：
1.法师玩法真是烂中烂，在一个地图上到处都是高性能小怪的情况下需要追着法师通过半固定的路径跑上四五次，期间每次瞬移还都会召唤小怪集火，就纯遛狗来的，溜到你没血瓶了，筋疲力尽了，再露出血条一套丝滑小连招当场秒杀，复活之后接着溜去吧。
虽然我知道制作组是在缝合怪猎玩法，但显而易见的，这套玩法的底层逻辑完全不适合2D游戏，更不适合2D魂。
当你发现你的想法没人做过的时候，第一点应该仔细思考一下这个思路的可行性，而不是觉得自己是天才别人都是啥B。

2.最大缺点也恰好在于地图设计优秀，但与这个弱智的法师玩法完全冲突。
继承于一代那广大，多元素，但缺少记忆点的地图，这让玩家本身跑图时就需要集中精力，避免漏掉什么收集，但是触发主线所需的冠名法师的地点却是固定的，并且往往距离传送点奇远无比，这让玩家要么找到下一个存档点再回头重走一遍来激活法师，要么就当场激活法师进行追击。但激活法师时地图上的所有小怪（包括之前清过的）都会立刻全部刷新，我觉得制作组的思路应该是为了防止地图上没有小怪与法师的友伤而导致玩家更难打，但实际的体验中更类似于法师与小怪双方一起群殴玩家，再加上缺少受身与空中无敌保护，导致在追法师的路上被小怪摸下悬崖或被直接空中连到死的情况比比皆是。
好不容易追死了法师，就会发现这一路上的岔路基本一个没走，又只能回到之前触发法师时的地点，再重新背一遍地图，走一遍岔路，又无聊又恶心。

3.主城太大了，想要强化完武器加个点再出发，得从地图最右边跑到最左边再跑到最中心，很难说有交互性存在。

4.谜语人+半机翻，真的很难给我仔细看剧情的动力。

5.加入随机词条的饰品就算了，饰品洗点还是强制全洗，想刷几个好一点的词条比登天还难。

6.还要加入PVP，还要加入入侵，还要加入类似黑魂的四个派系，还要做派系奖励……我觉得唯一应该表扬的是制作组并没有把PVP的奖励与完成度放进成就里，说真的，谁会想玩这种游戏的PVP呢？
如果你想玩，建议先打开黑暗之魂3，传送到聚集地，跳两个平台之后绕到房间左边捡一把叫做濡湿小镰刀的武器，强化满之后去老狼那边画个红符等着。

如果你说你就是喜欢被人遛着满图跑，喜欢被溜完之后两巴掌拍死再遛一次的体验的话可以买，但不推荐买，因为如果你想认真打，不开挂，默认所有法师全部初见过的话，做完全收集也[b]至少[/b]得被遛30遍以上，有这时间建议直接去开个新游戏，别在这当狗了。
