I wanted to like this game. I *expected* to like this game but despite pushing through my initial dislike, I honestly grew to hate it. Everything about this game seems hostile to your enjoyment. So much so that I started to question if I was misremembering how much I enjoyed the first game. 

The level design is terrible. The combat is simplistic and bland. The levelling structure is weird and unsatisfying. The weapons are boring. The bosses are samey and limited in design. The main gameplay loop of chasing down said bosses as they teleport around the level is just a huge chore. The knockback and juggling on hit is such a bizarre instance of game design, it all just leaves me scratching my head. Poise of enemies seemed inconsistent and unpredictable and stamina was tuned in a way that killed the pace of fights. Maybe I could've steered my build in a way to mitigate these annoyances but honestly, the game didn't give me enough to ever care to try.

I'm fine with trying something new but not if it sucks every last drop of fun out of the game. What a disappointment.

I previously played this game on the EGS, so while my playtime seems low, this review comes from the perspective of someone who has beaten Salt and Sacrifice. If you want me to say whether I like this game or not, well, yes, I do, and I hope you do too. But my review comes with a caveat.

If you are looking for a proper followup to Salt and Sanctuary that expands on what that game did right while alleviating or abandoning what it did wrong, you will not like Salt and Sacrifice.

Sanctuary was a metroidvania with soulslike mechanics, while Sacrifice is more of a Monster Hunter-like with soulslike mechanics. Instead of one big interconnected world, you are sent into smaller, still interconnected worlds that, while still well designed, fun to navigate and containing some really fun boss fights, aren't connected to each other, making this less of a metroidvania and more of a Demon's-Souls-but-2D kinda deal. Additionally, instead of making the big-ticket Mages isolated story bosses you need to destroy, they're structured like a Monster Hunter hunt. Beat on them, they run away, you chase them and beat on them more, they run away, rinse and repeat until they finally stand their ground in an isolated boss arena.

You are very much meant to kill the same type of mage over and over to grind out materials to make and upgrade elemental weapons. Which is a system I enjoy wholeheartedly, don't get me wrong, I'm a total sl*t for grindy games. But probably the most frustrating aspect of the mages is that they will spawn enemies exclusive to them during the chases that makes that chase so, so unpleasant sometimes. As much as I enjoy the hunts, it gets annoying really fast. This game definitely reeks of being a game that more heavily rewards multiplayer than single player.

That being said, there are still aspects of Sanctuary at play here. The leveling system is the same, there's some new weapon types and a whole new set of throwing weapons, you still get abilities that expand how much of a given area you can explore, the combo system is still present(albeit a bit watered down seemingly), the gameplay is still as weighty as ever, and there are still unique bosses that are one and done affairs that are pretty darn cool. And there's some neat lore reveals for fans of the first game.

Ultimately, Salt and Sacrifice is a followup that fails to be a proper successor to its predecessor. It's more of a sidegrade to the original game, doing some things better, some things worse, and doing some new stuff that won't always land. But on its own terms, I like it a lot. I just hope the next Salt game is a proper followup to Sanctuary.

作为前作的狂热粉丝，我对于盐与献祭的看法是复杂的。我本以为这将会是一款发挥前作所长，扩展前作世界观的作品。然而得到的却是一个缝合怪作品，一款和前作几乎没有关系的作品，无论是剧情、世界观还是玩法上。
 首先，本作其实有许多设计并非许多人说的那么糟糕。分离的地图，追捕法师的玩法其实自成一个还算可以的体系。追逐法师看似是一个烦人的过程，但是法师会引导玩家探图，处理掉本来十分棘手的怪物组合，而且指引玩家发现新的区域。分离地图确实失了银河城的精髓，但是单张地图的设计其实并不差。无论是捷径，还是通过能力以及法师门限制的分层次的探索阶段，其实给单张地图带来了多次游玩的价值。特别是在本作本身强调刷法师这一游玩方式的情况下，这一特点确实起到了很好的作用。可以看出，本作的设计其实也费了一番心思，也添加了许多新的玩法。但是，这套玩法带来的结果并不算好。
 其一是一些地方的怪物布置在对于无法师时探图还是过于艰辛了，特别是在第四章地图整体数值膨胀之后。有些小怪的连招真的是让大剑侠还不了手。其二是单张的地图整体性虽然可以，重复游玩价值也够。但是由于本作很多流程要求先去打后面的地图，之后再回来用新解锁的能力来开启上一地图的部分区域。再加上本作单张地图里各区域的区分度就有些差了，有许多区域的美术只能说十分相像。在这种情况下，没有地图导致的迷路实在是有些频繁。其三是本作整体难度偏低，大部分流程BOSS和法师都是初见就过，探图难度本身也不大。点名批评DLC，就摔死了两次，甚至有一个区域后来看成就时间才发现，连整个区域加BOSS的总时长才十分钟出头......其四是类怪猎的玩法与类魂的战斗本身兼容度不够，刷法师与其说刷素材不如说强化道具。本身感觉本作设计的大量无名法师，大部分情况下大部分玩家是不会去刷的。最后，本作画质上取得了进步，然而也确实失去了前作表现力十足的美术，个人还是觉得前作美术更好，尤其是在前作元素一致性上的贡献。同时，本作的拼接感有点强。法师玩法与本作部分的流程BOSS感觉确实像两个不同项目的拼接。或许，本作应该拆成两款游戏出会好一些。
 虽然点名批评了DLC的BOSS战，但是DLC让我看见了一点前作的味道。无论是整体地图四通八达，前后连接，还是偏神秘与宗教的氛围塑造，或许大部分玩家对于盐2的期望应该更像DLC的体验一点。希望SKA能意识到这一点。
 最后，尽管有着如此多的吐槽，其实本作体验还是没那么差的。多样的玩法，设计还算优质的地图，丰富的武器系统，2D联机怪猎......如果想要试一款剑走偏锋的银河城，本作其实还是有着比较独特的体验的。

首先，本人魂1，2，原罪，3，法环玩家，盐与避难所也通了，新的全随机我通了全敌人全物品随机不开缩放。
怪猎世界冰原崛起曙光通关。暗黑类我也玩，算是理论上这个游戏的目标用户吧，说以下感受。
结论：可以期待后期优化，但目前不推荐游玩。如果玩了盐与避难所可以接受，可以尝试2小时。
优点：
比较强的地图设计，在单地区的基础上，一张图支撑4-5次的重复探索和区域开放，保留1代的原味。
较强的角色性能，超长的初始绿条，较低的攻击后摇，即使面对多敌人混战、高攻击频率boss也有一战之力。
明亮的地图场景，色彩和光线比一代强很多。
怪物种类多，一个法师就带3种以上小怪，全流程保底100个敌人以上。
缺点：
过于平淡乏味的战斗体验，本作的boss设计非常糟糕，比一代还差。法师和部分boss基本纯沙包，没有追踪机制我碰到法师就当精英怪杀了。地图boss也没有印象深刻的点，角色性能太强我目前打地图boss除非一下把我秒了不然我都没死过。后面就开始小怪群、陷阱群、小房间弹幕躲避球。
跑图机制有设计但不多，如果第一次见法师可以接受追图，后面反复刷的时候，就不应该继续追踪跑图，而且地图刷法师还得看脸，野怪法师是无法追踪的，全凭地图记忆。
游戏中充斥着各种为了致敬怪猎而设计但完全没必要的鸡肋机制，但武器的战斗设计几乎约等于没有。
保留了一代的天赋树机制，这代却不能篝火升级，转换流派巨麻烦。魂的基础设计其实和怪猎希望塑造的大型狩猎战斗体系相悖。
在盐与献祭中，对于怪猎侧，没有狩猎体验，没有特色武器战斗机制，没有战斗交互体验，法师狩猎也不难。做装备体验感也比较差。刷护符体验更是依托构式。
对于魂侧，刷刷刷要素太浓，战斗策略太少，寻宝和boss成就感太低（地图上就没几个宝）。
地图规划体验差，说是重复探索强，实则路线几乎被限死。我连开新档换流派的想法都没有。

按照盐1的玩法，这就是一坨屎，按照怪猎的玩法：谢邀怪猎没打完
其实法师追逐战体验下来也不算差，但是整个游戏时间都在追着法师跑真的很难受
一周目最终boss难度甚至不如法师，重甲强化一半稍微躲一躲就过
地图设计其实还行，只是地图上红绿门有个指示就是最好，有很长一段时间都在找绿门（记不住）而卡关
关于绿门最绷不住的是第二个图左下角的门，为了开门打冠名法师结果门后又是俩。。。。。。。。。

This was a great game, finished two playthroughs. On the first I took my time, did everything and ended up with a high level character. It got a bit easy near the end, but it was still a bunch of fun exploring, customising, and being strong enough to take down multiple mages at at time. Then on my second character I did more a rush to the end boss and was happily surprised to see the difficulty get nice and challenging, especially in the 'regular' level bosses, where playing more of a glass cannon build can be rewarded with more of a quick boss rush style ending. The game is fun. It looks and plays a lot like the first game, Sanctuary, I'm a little taken aback by how many reviews claim it's "not anything like Salt and Sanctuary, etc.." By my impression they are very similar games. Where Sacrifice adds handful of new game features, which all seemed pretty decent to me. I think more or less during the majority of the game you are running around doing the same things you did in Sanctuary. I love it! It has the same addictive nature. The environments are large, gorgeous, and full of details. The monsters are very diverse and challenging. There is a larger selection of items. The combat and build accessibility is refined. In my opinion Sacrifice feels like a very well made game that the developers put a lot of time and love into making.
its a perfect mix of Metroid-Vania and Soulslike, very fun game.

1.这作地图分为五张大地图 每个大图不连通 每个大图内有三四个小图这个倒是连通的 前往每个大图的方法是你在营地（类似传火祭祀场）直接传送进入五大地图
2.本作战斗系统和上作区别不大 但是这作的人物就像是皮球被各种怪物打来打去的 怪物的攻击无论是上挑 下批 前冲 后撩都会让你往天上飞起来 而且你在落地前没有保护 也就是说怪可以给你一套空中无限连 直接给你搞死 你毫无办法
3.本作的BOSS战不再是以往的经典区域BOSS设计 虽然也有部分区域BOSS 但是本作的装备来源是法师BOSS 法师BOSS类似怪猎那种 先发现线索 然后追踪 然后在追踪过程中 遇到五到七次BOSS 这时候你可以攻击BOSS 但是无法杀死BOSS 只能和BOSS的召唤物战斗 然后杀死召唤物会掉落装备合成素材 然后再兜兜转转之后BOSS通常回到最初出现的地方 然后开启BOSS战 这时候你才能杀死BOSS 然后获取合成素材（没错 就是你想的那样 一个BOSS要打好多遍 才能做完它对应的一套装备）
4.本作如果你正在进行BOSS追逐战时候返回营地 追逐进度将被重置
5.本作的装备系统有些同质化了 
6.功能提示不足 功能上打死最终祭品BOSS之后千万不能摸树 一旦摸树选了吞噬就强制进入下周目

スルメゲーのだと思った説明をば

武器種が多い。
他のソウルライクと同じで、武器種が豊富なのはgood
同じ武器種でもステータス補正とかモーション速度とか武器属性とかが変わるから、とりあえツインダガーだから作成する！ぐらいの気持ちでプレイしたほうが楽しい。
例を挙げるとダッシュ攻撃の移動距離が違うや、強攻撃の速度が速いなど。
そのため、一律に後半武器が強い！っていうことはない。

攻撃速度がクッソ遅い代わりにクッソ高い攻撃力で相手を殴れる武器とか
強攻撃でチェーンソーモードにできる武器（前作の鋼のセンティピードとか不死鳥の尾みたいなの）もあるよ！(入手が簡単とは言っていない)

武器の火力に直結するステータスは、筋力、技量、魔力、信念、知力、運の六つで、
それ以外では生命力、持久力、頑強のステータスが存在している。
武器種によってステータス補正が変わっているのは前作と同じだね！
敵に囲まれて死ぬのが嫌なら頑強のステータス上げようね。対策があるのに使わないで文句を言うのはやめようね！
火力を求めるなら生命力、持久力、頑強、は不要だ！(無限周回)
軽ロリ最強♥

今作は武器の種類が近接で13種類、遠距離で6種類、魔法が2種類ある。
近接武器が2スロット、遠距離武器が1スロットで分かれているため、前作みたいに剣からの銃コンボ！は無くなった。火打ちの剣はリストラです。
その代わりに近接武器には神聖or禁忌の魔法が割り振られている。(禁忌を使うと不浄が溜まるとかは無くて、ただの種類分け)
武器の攻撃力と攻撃範囲を強化するエンチャントとか魔導師が使ってきた魔法(クソデカタツマキ)とかを使用できる。
フォーカスを消費して魔法を使う武器と、レイジ(敵を攻撃したら勝手に溜まっていく)を使う武器があるのは注意点かな？
フォーカス魔法は連打出来て、レイジ魔法は連打出来ない。ぐらいの認識でOK。

基本的な戦闘は近接で戦って、死にそうなときや空を飛んでいる面倒くさい敵を倒すようで遠距離武器を使う。（一週目は遠距離ちくちくでボスを倒せるから禁止しようね）
自分のお気に入りはツインダガー＆鞭構成です。近接と中距離で無双していけ?（隙自語）

最強の運ゲー周回要素として、アーティファクト厳選というものがあってだな？
簡単に説明すると「モンハンの護石」
ある一定のレベルまでは素材で強化出来るけど、それ以上(40レベルの物とか)はボスのドロップで狙うしか無いです。
この要素をつまらない！クソゲー！っていう人はそもそも周回ゲー向いてないです。引退です。

アップデートを重ねて不便だった部分が解消されてき、ストレスを感じる部分が少なくなっている。
ノックバック中に追撃ダメージを受けなくなってるとか、非戦闘時はスタミナ無限でずっとダッシュ出来るようになったり、
オベリスク間でワープできるようになったりで利便性はよくなっている。

確かに魔導士の追いかけっこパート＆雑魚パートは面倒くさいけど、
クエスト中だったら死んでも魔導師の位置は変わっていないから同じ場所に戻ればいいだけ。
防具を一式作るってなると野良徘徊の魔導師を倒さないといけないから少し面倒くさいかな。（防具なんて必要ねーんだよ！）

最後に、
マルチプレイは最高に面白いです。
PvPは強武器と頑強ステータス盛らないと勝つの難しいけど、それもまた楽しめるように。
オンライン協力はホストにステータスが合わせられるので、
100レベルのプレイヤーが1レベルのプレイヤーを全力キャリーとかはできません。
ホストのレベル＋5、まで下げられたはず。

追加コンテンツでPvP専用エリア＆ボスラッシュが追加されたので、ご友人と一緒にプレイするのがお勧めです。
マルチプレイなら蘇生もできるしね！
PvPエリアは人がいないので、時間の無駄です。侵入狙ってるほうがまだ可能性があります。

やりこみ要素、キャラの限界育成がしたい人にはお勧めのゲームです。

Up front, Salt and Sacrifice is NOT Salt and Sanctuary, despite what the store page, aesthetic and game feel may say because the game design is very different. While it wasn't really what I expected, even knowing it was a departure from Sanctuary, it's still a solid title if you are willing to meet it where it is... but some of those things you need to meet it on might be deal breakers.

The game design is entirely different than how I remember Salt and Sanctuary, and I wasn't enamored with how Sacrifice does things. Instead of having one big sprawling world to explore you get a hub to go to one of ~5 maps. It's a shame then that the hub area that was specifically crafted to be your town is a nuisance to navigate and the portal out of it is annoyingly far from the leveling statue and where the game puts you in the hub after teleporting there. Instead of exploring and hitting new bosses being the primary gameplay loop like Sanctuary, the core progression is tied to "Mage Hunts" which weren't my favorite design choice. You'll come across something on the map to get the option to hunt a Mage and once you accept it you are now in a worse version of the map where there are enemies that keep spawning in that are associated with the Mages, you just cleared your way here? Well it's not clear anymore and nothing will be until the Mage is dead. Once you take down the Mage you can make gear from their drops... which means if you want to swap your armor set or get upgrade materials you are going to need to grind out "nameless" or "fated" versions of that same Mage, which are much more annoying to initiate. This Mage grind system is likely why I had heard the game be compared to Monster Hunster, which isn't a bad comparison complete with turf wars of sorts. The Mages do not get along with each other or the normal map monsters and in my experience mages will frequently end up in the same area which makes things a hit hectic. 

I think there is something to be said for trying out different designs, I'm pretty sure the maps here have more shortcuts than Sanctuary (I also haven't played that game for a while, so I could be wrong) and I could see them focusing on making self-contained areas enabling those kinds of choices. I don't think beating up enemies to craft gear is a bad idea at all, but I did find aspects of the Mage hunts to be an annoyance (especially when I started falling behind on gear) such as the constantly respawning enemies, and having those enemies even kill me at a respawn point when I had put my controller down to get a drink after dying to a Mage causing me to lose all of that salt. In my limited experience and research it does seem like Salt and Sacrifice is better balanced than Sanctuary was with no weapon type being outright bad here. Ultimately, I had some fun with Sacrifice but I wouldn't say it quite has the same Metroidvania feel or as much of a sense of exploration and mystery due to the map design differences, but the core combat is solid and once you understand what the game is, it's a good game that just isn't what a lot of people really wanted.

尽管我尽量以宽容的心态对待这款游戏，但它确实还是十足的恶心到我了（这还是更新改善了很多体验之后的结果）。前期风法这种东西都懒得提了，后期原力法和书法简直是狗屎中的狗屎，一个技能能给穿着5级重甲的玩家直接秒杀，体术更是三段追踪带延迟判定震地波。纯粹的做嗨了恶心玩家，性能数值都是依托，逼着玩家和法师互秒。然后这个免费dlc更是幽默中的幽默，阴间小怪boss扎堆，就这个全图最常见的小怪，他会藏在各种天花板阴人，而且见面必定释放投技，但是制作组并不满足于此，对于这个小怪他们特别用心地移除了地面保护和投技保护，也就是说无论你被打倒了还是吃了投技，他们都能像疯狗一样砍你。那么这么难打的dlc给什么奖励呢？打完dlc最终boss给了三个三元霜核（差不多相当于锻造石5）。只能说改了这么久还是这样这游戏也只配得上差评了。

This game is pretty much crap. That comes from someone who liked the first game in the series "salt & sanctuary". While the first game had it's fair share of problems, it always came around with something that's actually good, be it charm or some nice exploration or fun weapon/rings combinations.

Salt and Sacrifice feels like a quarter of a game. Like they really had an idea for the first map of the game and started designing it, then after a while the ideas or the money ran out, and they put together some uninspired stuff, mixed up with whatever, wrapped it up with poorly thought mechanics and game design and shipped it. 

The combat ranges from passable to frustrating, be it because you can't block most stuff without taking damage or depleting all your stamina, be it because your dodge puts you in a different postal code than the enemy attacking or because everything dishes out a ton of damage. 

The game devolves rapidly into identifying the enemies you can stun lock and kill with light attack spam or the enemies you must dodge farther away than the hit (so you end up somewhere even close to hit the enemy back) and hitting 2 light attacks, never more. Almost every boss, elite, whatever can be beaten by this pattern... dodge in a further distance, hit 2 light attacks, repeat. And it's not that it's a cheap tactic that you use to win, it's literally the only thing that works. Dodge is wonky, you can't combo, blocking is too much risk for little to no reward.

In conclusion, this is what happens when you have some good ideas, but lack focus and polish to turn those ideas into something fun to play. They took the roots from the first game and ruined it trying to go for some Monster Hunter shenanigans that's not polished or developed either. The exploration is boring, the combat is bad and the unique mechanics detracts from the game instead of complementing it.

Very disappointing. Oh, also the art is kinda bad. The first one was ugly as sin... this one is a little better, but still very ugly...

(魔導司以外のボスは、一部を除いてとどめは必要ありません)
武装は近距離2種、遠距離1種が装備でき、装備レベルは前作のSalt and Sanctuaryと同じくスキルレベル制。
パリイはジャストガードで強靭度を削る、いわゆる体幹システム制となっているのであまり使わなかったものの、遠距離攻撃へのジャストガードは使ったほうが良い場面があります。

この作品の大きな特徴として、野良で登場する「名も無き魔導司」があります。
ストーリーボスとしてのネームド魔導司、その後に固定ボスとして登場する「定められた魔導司」とは違い、彼らは決まった地域にある程度ランダムなレベルで出現し、魔導司同士でも敵対します。
そのため、魔導司が4体同時に出現して地獄絵図になったり、同じ属性の魔導司が2体で喧嘩したりと、エキサイティングな展開になることも。

最後になりますが、自分は前作よりこちらの方が楽しめました。
前作と違うから低評価！という固定観念を持たず、別の作品として考えれば良作だと思います。

This game is a very mixed bag. For every cool thing it does it does something equally bad.
For every cool weapon moveset like rapiers or vanguard weapons, you get something terrible feeling for its weight like maces or glaives.
For every cool bit of level design like the first area or Corivus mire, you get some borderline evil level design like the outdoor section of the temple of Eleven or The Twin Giants where enemies will knock you to your death constantly.
For every cool mage like Hydromancers with their interesting moveset and attacks or Lumenmancers with their cool light aesthetic and good but not broken zoning moves, you get some real crap designs like Diablomancers and Electromancers with their absurd damage and odd movesets, or Neuromancers and Aeromancers with their absurd knockback and most common environments being areas full of insta-death ledges and similar pitfalls.

Like, I want to love the game, but there's so many questionable decisions even beyond this and annoying encounters that I just can't recommend it. The back-and-forth and whiplash is real and it makes me dislike the game. That's not even to get into issues like how sparse the multiplayer is so you have to do absurd grinds for any of the cool faction rewards, especially the 50 token rewards. Or other things like how the silver economy is completely broken and renders the currency useless past super early game. 
This game does have these building blocks of some cool things though, especially the multiplayer with cool things like the blueheart runners and general functions when it DOES work. So I am not swearing these devs off because of this, and I want to see them do good and continue to make experimental works in the future, but this game just, wasn't it. The whole epic games deal didn't help either, putting greed before one's own legacy and all that. A lot of people still don't even know this game exists because of how much of a publicity and good-will-blackhole EGS is. 

In the end. It's a mixed bag. if you're willing to sift through the bad parts to get the gold, or just really REALLY want a 2D monster hunter like, this exists. Otherwise, don't get it, and I personally didn't enjoy it enough to recommend it to others outright, even if I did like a good half of it.

Salt and sacrifice é a sequência de salt and sanctuary, porém não acho que podemos chamar de salt 2.0 pois aqui no sacrifice 
tem mecânicas diferentes comparado ao seu antecessor.
Inicialmente foi lançado para pc como exclusivo da epic e lá estava com valor salgadinho viu, mais de 1 ano depois ele veio 
para a steam e só um tempo depois que o valor diminuiu.
Quando soube que teria um salt novo eu fiquei bem empolgado, pois gostei demais do primeiro salt, porém como dito antes veio com valor alto e ainda só na epic, fiquei acompanhando e vendo quase nada para não pegar spoilers, o que via por aí de notícias é que não estava deixando as pessoas tão felizes por certas mudanças, mas ainda assim não fui procurar para ver o que era que estava deixando todos desmotivados com o jogo.

E finalmente chegou na steam no ano passado e quando teve uma promoção legal
peguei ele, e fui começar jogar só recentemente, e bom o que achei agora 
depois de terminar ?

Pontos positivos:
1 - A jogabilidade do jogo ainda está bem legal, está com uma boa variedade 
de armas e com os golpes diferentes de cada arma e ainda com as magias 
que o jogo tem está interessante, tem ataque leve, pesado, ataque no ar,
de longe com magia e armas a distância, bloqueio, rolamento, pulo, corrida, dash e etc.
2 - Variedade de builds boas também, o jogo ainda tem uma árvore de skills 
passivas gigantesca com diversos status, além também que ainda tem as armas,
armaduras e itens que você pode craftar com os reagentes que pega dos bosses 
magos e esses itens de craft dos bosses magos todos tem algum tipo de dano elemental diferente.
E ainda tem os itens/armaduras normais do jogo, fora todas as classes que tem, então dá pra brincar bastante de build aqui.
3 - A ambientação do jogo é bacana, apesar de não ter tantos mapas, os que tem eu achei 
que são bem diferentes e variados entre eles, tem mapa com floresta, deserto, área gélida, cavernas,
castelos, vilas, área montanhosa, é diverso mesmo com algumas repetições.
4 - A dificuldade eu achei difícil, acho que até mais que o primeiro jogo, sofri 
em alguns bosses aqui mais do que deveria mesmo estando forte, o meu primeiro personagem 
eu larguei ele pois fiz algumas escolhas erradas na build e ficou ruim, mas o segundo 
com quem zerei ficou legal com os erros que aprendi e mesmo assim alguns bosses deram 
muito trabalho, passei muita raiva em alguns dos bosses normais e outros nos bosses magos 
e até mesmo para alguns monstros de áreas que são muito chatos de lidar e isso combinado 
com certas áreas específicas que estão você passa um nervoso viu.
5 - Duração, é grandinho se você for querer matar todos os bosses do mapa e explorar tudo dele 
e desbloquear todos os acessos e atalhos, eu terminei por volta de umas 24hrs fazendo muita coisa 
e obviamente morrendo bastante também.
6 - Graficamente está bonito para o que o jogo oferece, é um estilo de arte simples, 
única coisa que acho meio feia são os personagens, todo o restante acho de boa, não ligo 
que não tenha gráficos realistas ou super lindos de encher os olhos.
7 - Trilha sonora e músicas são boas nos momentos que tocam.
8 - Bosses estão bem mais variados e difíceis aqui, tem alguns golpes e movimentos de bosses 
que se você levar é quase morte, e muitas lutas você tem que ter uma boa precisão e reflexo 
em dia para ficar desviando das coisas, foram divertidas muitas lutas mesmo sofrendo.
9 - Variedade de inimigos é diversificada mesmo com alguns repetidos de jeitos diferentes, 
eu vi muito tipo de monstro, e isso é bom para não ficar a mesmice de sempre.
10 - Legenda pt-br, se o jogo tem tradução pra mim, isso é ponto positivo, fica bem melhor 
o entendimento de tudo se você não entende tão bem em outra língua.

Pontos negativos:
1 - Caçada, o jogo agora tem um sistema novo que não tinha no jogo anterior 
que são as caçadas aos magos, você interage com um negócio que não sei explicar 
bem o que é pois tem de diferentes tipos, e assim que você interage aparece 
algumas informações sobre o que você vai caçar, com isso o mapa começa a ter 
alguns monstros diferentes pois começou uma caçada, e fica um rastro que você 
tem que seguir até achar esse mago que é um boss, quando você chega perto dele 
vai ter alguns monstros que ele summona que são do tipo elemental dele, geralmente 
são fortes e chatinhos de lidar, você então tem que bater no boss mago até ele 
correr e com isso você volta ao seu rastreio umas 4/5 vezes fazendo isso toda vez 
até ele chegar em um certo local que você vai enfrentar ele definitivo sem ele fugir, 
isso é uma caçada.
Cara isso é legal por ser uma novidade na primeira, segunda vez... Mas depois fica um porre, sabe porque ?
Pois vai ter os monstros normais do mapa te atrapalhando, juntos com os summons desse 
boss mago que são fortes e isso somado com algumas áreas do mapa você passa até mal 
de raiva e pode ter certeza que na grande maioria vai ser em partes do mapa que você 
precisa escalar várias coisas e que se cair de lá é morte porque tem muito bicho que te joga 
pra fora das plataformas e se morrer para queda ou monstros/mago vai ter que ir lá pro
checkpoint que estava salvo por último e fazer todo o caminho de volta pra pegar novamente 
seus sais e continuar de onde parou o rastreio dessa caçada, nossa que raiva que passei 
com vários magos viu, do momento da caçada até chegar nele é torturante isso
pois você já passou raiva o caminho todo até ali e quando chega no boss pra enfrentar 
ele, já passa raiva na luta com ele também, é uma ideia legal isso da caçada, mas o jeito que foi feito 
eu não curti e não achei divertido.
2 - Mapa, eu não gostei muito do mapa aqui nesse jogo, no primeiro jogo você parecia 
ser mais livre o mundo lá te dava uma impressão de mais compacto e tudo bem conectado, aqui no sacrifice parece 
tudo confuso, você tem que ir decorando os lugares que passou e os lugares que tem 
alguma porta para acessar depois e até você lembrar onde é vai requer andar bastante novamente 
pelo mapa todo e ter ainda uma boa memória, fora os teleportes que são ruins aqui que você não consegue 
teleportar diretamente para qualquer um dos checkpoints onde descobriu, tipo um exemplo aqui,
quando você está lá na zona que é o lobby do jogo e vai entrar no portal para ser teleportado pra 
algum mapa, já no início tem um checkpoint, e a medida que você vai explorando você vai achando outros, 
e se você descobre um outro checkpoint perto desse primeiro inicial do mapa você até consegue teleportar 
desse recém novo para o inicial porém simplismente não consegue fazer o caminho de volta para o recém 
descoberto, é como se ele não existisse, ele só vai funcionar se você morrer e ele ter sido o último com o que 
teve interação anteriormente, é muito esquisito, é como se só alguns checkpoints do mapa fossem fixos e pudesse 
teleportar só de alguns específicos e outros que estão entre eles simplismente não serve para teleport, isso deixa chato a locomoção e com isso acaba ficando confuso o mapa e direcionamento por você não lembrar direito onde é determinada zona para ir lá depois, já que não tem como teleportar de qualquer checkpoint para outro, colocasse pelo menos um mapa para você saber para onde está indo, porém não tem nenhum e nem outro.
3 - A história do jogo achei meio sem sal, não empolga a querer saber mais, o que você 
precisa saber é que você está indo para um certo lugar onde irá virar um inquisidor
e os o que os inquisitores fazem ? eles são as pessoas que vão a procura desses
magos que surgiram para derrotar eles. E ao longo do jogo você vai sabendo mais 
sobre as coisas interagindo com os npcs, mas não é tão interessante o desenrolar 
das coisas entende.

Conclusão
É difícil não comparar com seu antecessor, eu confesso que 
gostei mais do primeiro jogo, esse aqui tentou inovar em algumas coisas mas 
não me agradou em certos pontos, mas ele ainda tem muita coisa boa que o jogo 
anterior tinha, eu vou indicar em promoção, porém fique ciente disso.

Loved the first game; this just isn't it.

I have currently defeated two bosses, both forgettable and easy. I have found no secrets despite hitting every wall. The dodge rolls further past the enemy than my attacks reach, and there’s usually not enough time to catch back up to them before they start swinging again. The parry is gone, and now you just have a timed block that acts as a stagger, rather than the original system of block + attack, which just feels awful paired with the dodge roll. None of this was an issue in the first game. 

As well as this, the hub is super confusing to traverse, and is way too big. You have to go back to the hub to level up as well as do various other things, splitting the original camp in the first game into multiple areas. To travel back to the camp you have to teleport back, and upon reaching it, travel up to level up, then travel down through caves towards the portal, which you then have to put 5 specific runes in each time to unlock the ability to teleport back to your original location. This issue gets worse when you realize that there are two functionally different save points, one that you can travel to, and one you can travel from, meaning if you haven’t unlocked the former you have to carry on until you do, or lose progress in the map. This is functionally similar to Dark Souls 2/3, but made even more tedious, while the first game was functionally similar to Dark Souls 1. This whole system is just made unnecessarily inconvenient, and adds nothing to the experience. 

While I haven’t got much hours, I do not want to go over 2 hours and lose my refund period, and I don’t see myself enjoying this game after reading the other reviews describing more flaws than I’ve experienced so far. If I had to compliment this game, I do enjoy the grappling hook + wall jump. If I had to nit pick, I prefer the jump + dodge = fling yourself far bug in the first game, even though it wasn’t intended, making for some fun/fast movement. 

I just don’t see the appeal of this game. The combat isn’t anything special either compared to other games like Blasphemous 2/Hollow Knight. While Salt and Sanctuary was my first 2D souls-like game, and holds a special place in my heart, it’s been long enough that the standards for games like these have raised so much that this just feels out of place.

Salt & Sacrifice is a fun experience, whether solo or with the built-in multiplayer capabilities. The skill tree mechanics allow the player to feel more fully in control of their strengths and weaknesses, and its similarities to Souls games make it a must-try for members of that fanbase.
The graphics are unique in their style and the combat feels fluid with just the right amount of chaos, and the learning curve isn't too steep for newcomers to this style of gameplay. The crafting mechanics for creating your own gear place the players' success firmly in their own hands, and the platforming of each stage adds a twist of risk to every area you play in. The addition of elemental strengths and weaknesses is a strategy bonus to any playthrough.

I didn't play the predecessor, Salt & Sanctuary, and it's my opinion that the number of negative reviews for this game based on it being a sequel are similar to the reviews given to Dark Souls 2, which is saddening. Let's get one thing out of the way first, if you loved salt and sanctuary you might not like this game since it is more akin to monster hunter when it comes to progression and game-play.
Though with that being said the game feels and plays really really good, I can't really put it into words other than it just feels good. If you ever played dark souls we have all had that moment where it just felt like we shouldn't have been hit and it just feels frustrating despite you knowing that it was your fault however I have never felt that not even once while I was learning the game.
I wish I had a better way to explain it but it just feels good. I really love being able to fight bosses that I have already killed again, I also really love being able to get unique relics with different stat buffs and unless you are trying to midmax it is just a really nice little boost to your build.
I can't really think of anything bad to say other than it can be annoying to try and hunt down the mage you are looking for when it just won't spawn in. Though that would be an issue if the game-play wasn't so fun.
So I guess to end this take what I said with a grain of salt since I think what I said has been very opinionated though if you like dark souls and don't mind it being 2D and the same with monster hunter then I can't see why you wouldn't enjoy this game.

Edit: The game is still something I really enjoy but god is it stupid difficult in the later worlds and can totally see why people would still not enjoy this game. So one other thing that I want to add on to this review is that this game will require a lot of patience and determination to not give up from frustration.

中评。让人感到可惜的作品。它的优点不少：不逊于一代的优秀地图设计，丰富的武器和战技系统，非常平滑无需刻意刷级的难度曲线，比较简单的跳跳乐，还有跟一代一样晦暗的美术风格和背景设定。如果没有“法师狩猎”这个boss机制的话我想可能会是好评如潮也许超过一代的佳作。

但法师追逐战的负反馈掩盖了这些优点。这就犹如你在玩怪猎，狩猎一只大胸豺龙。可是一上手刚砍了没几刀大胸豺龙就嗷嗷叫唤出几只凶豺龙围攻你。你解决了小怪再砍一刀它就立马换区溜之大吉。在古代树森林里好不容易找到它，你又不小心一个跨区斩掉下悬崖……然后就摔死了。

所幸狩猎不会因死亡重置，于是又追上去，四五次重复的召小怪、砍几刀、换区后，终于大胸豺龙不跑了，还亮出了血条，终于能大战一场了……但那毕竟只是一只大胸豺龙啊，你没几下就把它砍死了。回头一想六成时间在跑图、两成时间清小怪、一成时间在骂娘，最后一成时间打boss本体。真是一场酣畅淋漓的狩猎啊。

而游戏中大概有八成的boss都是这种法师追逐战，并且可能需要为了制作、升级装备的材料反复刷这些法师boss。

制作组把分开来都很好玩的怪猎和类银的设计缝合在了一起。很可惜，反复追怪、刷材料的怪猎狩猎机制搭配上类银错综复杂的地图设计和锁钥机制以及2d类魂的掉落伤害和较为僵硬的战斗手感给我带来的体验比较糟糕，好像把分开都很好吃的烧烤和蛋糕混合在了一起。

一周目时间可能在25小时左右。打完最终boss摸下树就会进新周目要注意

THIS game is an example of what happens when spoiled marketing people come in who have never played this game and force devs to change their vision ultimately ruining the gameplay experience. 前作盐与避难所通关后就一直盼着续作，如今终于等到盐与献祭上steam，没想到能做这么抽象，硬顶着打了全成就，二周目真不想玩了。希望这个工作室还能有新作，并且能做好，画面上还是那个味。

Долго не мог определиться с оценкой, что на протяжении всего прохождения, что после, но в итоге решил всё-таки поставить "палец вверх". Но не столько из-за впечатлений, которые очень неоднозначные, сколько из-за того, что мне интересно посмотреть на следующую работу разработчиков. 

Коротко о впечатлениях. 

●Тут есть сюжет. Его мало, но это уже лучше того недоразумения, что был в 1-ой части. Игра прямым текстом объясняет, кто мы, зачем мы и почему возрождаемся после каждой смерти. Редко кто заморачивается с тем, чтобы объяснить эту игровую условность. 
●Дизайн уровней стал получше. Локации интересно исследовать, и даже паркур не так раздражает, как раньше, хотя под конец игры начинает надоедать. 
●Карты всё так же нет. С одной стороны, это плохо, с другой - тут несколько отдельных больших локаций, между которыми нужно телепортироваться, так что заблудиться или забыть какое-то приметное, ранее недоступное место, стало сложнее. 
●Бесконечный респавн Магов - cpaнь. Как будто одних мобов и ловушек не достаточно, ещё и эти долбaнные Маги повсюду, плюнуть некуда, чтобы в них не попасть. Наверное, это и ради того, чтобы показать, какой хаос творится в королевстве из-за магической Мглы, порождающей этих Магов, и ради фарма расходников для крафта, но*... 
●Музыки всё так же 2,5 трека на всю игру, но по крайней мере нет того неприятного шума в главном меню, вместо него теперь нормальная музыкальная тема. 
●Управление стало заметно удобнее. Теперь вполне неплохо можно сражаться в ближнем бою, и закидывать врагов метательными топорами в дальнем. И это не просто опция, дальний бой наносит солидный урон и пренебрегать им не стоит. 
●Вагон и маленькая тележка брони и оружия, и всё та же вcpaтая система прокачки, что и в серии Dark Souls. Хочешь улучшить снаряжение, но не хватает найденных ресурсов? 
*Фарми. Это же так весело и увлекательно, да, разработчики, в одиночной-то игре? А до макс. уровня сможешь улучшить всего 1-2 предмета. Видимо, не хотят разрабы, чтобы игрок получал слишком много удовольствия от игры, чтобы экспериментировал, меняя экипировку. Кто-то один раз придумал эту убогую систему, а все остальные её копируют. 

Ну и так далее. Тут примерно поровну плохого и хорошего, и я так не определился, чего больше. Авансом ставлю положительную оценку, т.к. игра заметно отличается от предыдущей, есть развитие, так, глядишь, третья часть будет шедевром.

TL;DR: This is Salt and Sacrifice's combat mechanics with a new gameplay loop and a greater emphasis on crafting to find your optimal weapons and armor. Solid 7.5/10 for me.

Salt and Sacrifice is the successor to Salt and Sanctuary, but it is not a direct sequel either in terms of the story or in terms of the gameplay mechanics. People going into this thinking this is just going to be a direct sequel that builds on the lore or continues the story or even in terms of the world will be disappointed. People have likened the gameplay loop of Sal&Sac to Monster Hunter. I don't have any experience with MH, so I can't really say whether this is true or not. I'll just do my best to explain the experience:

You play as a criminal who had the choice to either accept execution for some crime or become an undying Mage Hunter. Choosing the latter, your curse is to go about hunting these powerful magic users and to stop whatever force is behind the creation of Mages in the first place. Rather than having one huge world map like Sal&Sanc, there are multiple smaller areas to explore. And I do mean "slightly". Each of the maps that you can explore are pretty sprawling in their own right, and all of them have areas that can only be accessed by finding the right tools and upgrades in later areas. In most instances, these areas lead to optional bosses or treasures, making it rewarding for remembering that your new tool had a purpose from several worlds ago.

As you go from world to world, you find the mark of the Mages' influence, giving you the ability to track them down on the map. Rather than tracking them directly to a boss arena, you fight them all across the map, chasing them back to their arena where you then have the final showdown. Destroying the Mages and their minions will award you crafting materials, each mage having an armor set, several weapons, and several amulets and rings that boost various aspects of your character. Given that there are over a dozen weapon types, it becomes a bit of an interesting surprise as to when you're going to find the next best weapon or armor you are after. With the right stat investment, however, most weapons within a class are pretty balanced to one another, such that the fun of finding a new weapon may be that it more closely matches the stat investment you were after or its damage types give you more versatility against different kinds of enemies. It is here that I will address the complaint of "grinding". I personally did not find the game very grindy at all. You can re-fight mages using a variety of methods the game provides, and I think overall I spent maybe 2 of the 42 hours in my initial playthrough grinding mages for one specific top-tier weapon. I'd consider that a reasonable percentage of game time, overall.

With the bevy of weapons and armors in each class, the game felt like it gave me a lot of room to experiment and tweak my build for what I liked. No matter what way you are building there is going to be at least 2 main-hand and 2 off-hand weapon types that complement your build in some way. Then it's just a matter of gittin gud and putting the mages to rest.

There's also a healthy amount of bosses and side-quests that are completely optional and entirely missable. In my case, I missed 10 achievements on my first playthrough, 3 of which involved NPC side quests, and 4 of which involved bosses I just completely missed. As if the allure of trying out a new build with new weapons wasn't enough of a reason, this gives the game plenty of replayability and shows how much the game rewards exploration.

Finally, the story. I would say that the story here is even more bleak than its predecessor. The devs did a good job of spreading the lore into multiple aspects of the game, from optional dialog to the enemy design to the weapon descriptions; heck, for the true lore-gluttons, every node on the skill tree offers a blurb of text. And when you get to the end of the game, if you've taken your time and spoken to the people along the way, you'll be sufficiently moved by the endings and their implications.

In the end, this was a fun game, and in my mind, a worthy successor to Sal&Sac. I liked this enough that I will absolutely be doing at least one more playthrough to get all the achievements I missed and try out some weapon types I didn't get to try yet.

首先，放弃这是1代续作的想法，这就是一个套皮下的另一个游戏。第二，这是一坨混了屎的巧克力
问题包括但不限于：
1、恶心重复且不必要的追逐战。大量环境交互（陷阱/楼梯/跳跳乐）+原本的怪物+法师的召唤怪导致场面非常混乱，非常糟心
2、愚蠢的物理交互。主角仿佛纸片人，大家都会击飞，还有大量的陷阱会击飞，加上灾难一般的平台，你经常会从5楼直接飞到1楼，注意，是经常
3、非常烦人且不必要的陷阱：地刺还好，有很多的击飞钉耙，加上上面提到的1和2，组成了非常难受的游戏体验
4、奇怪的数据：你的人物成长远远跟不上地图区域和法师boss的强度提升，中期boss的伤害基本上都是1/2到1/3一下的存在，包括法师boss的召唤物
5、糟糕的寻路：想想在以上的所有毛病下，你还要不停的通过时而可见时而不可见的连线来找你要击杀的boss

还有不少毛病，不一一列举了。感觉制作组已经不打算修补了，真的是糟蹋了1代的口碑

前作で注文がついただろうマルチプレイを追求した結果、前作を評価してたプレイヤーがコレジャナイ感を持ってしまい滅茶苦茶批判が出てしまったゲーム。プレイしてて何とかならんのか、と思ったのは以下の通り。

1.マルチプレイを前提としたボスの挙動
プレイヤーが複数いて散開陣形をとって敵の隙を突く戦いが推奨される戦法な関係で、シングルプレイだと敵の理不尽な攻撃判定や起き攻めで常にイライラさせられる。装備とステータスを厳選してごり押しできるようになればエンディングまで問題なく到達できるがそういう装備はごく一部。

2.装備がワンパターンで択がない
ゲームの進め方がモンハンっぽいと評価されているが武器系統もまさにモンハン。武器種で攻撃モーションが共通のため、武器の総数に対して攻撃手段は驚くほど少ない。前作の火打ちの剣のようなギミック武器を期待してたため本当にがっかり。またグリフの性能がほぼ装備の性能のため、有用なグリフがあるまたはグリフ発動にボーナスがつく最高ランク武器以外に存在意義がない。前述の通りシングルプレイでは大抵の武器がボスの苛烈な攻撃に力負けする。

3.世界設定が雑
最後のマップにネタバレをしゃべる謎NPCを配置して終わり、って前作から劣化しすぎていないだろうか。あと前作みたいにパン食べて回復したかったんだが。とにかく世界観を構築するようなフレーバー要素が希薄でその種のワクワク感が皆無なのが本当にコレジャナイ。

まとめると、前作は開発者が想定していない要素が絶妙に絡み合って良さになってたんだろうな、そしてその良さを開発者が理解できてなかったからここまでダメになったんだろうな、っていう出来。確かにマルチプレイ関係は非常に気を遣って作ってはあるのだがその良さが伝わる前に怒りと失望が沸き上がってしまうゲーム。

This? I don't even know. This isn't Salt and Sanctuary 2 like I hoped, this is... nothing. I don't know how else to better put it. It's vapid. Shallow. Unremarkable. It has none of the qualities which made the original exceptional, to the extent that I really don't know why this exists at all.

The areas are uninspired, and too big for how little they offer.
The enemies uninteresting, and bosses especially a huge disappointment.

You're spoon fed lore and important terms by about a half dozen NPCs in the first hour which really takes away any of the setting's mystique. I really had no interest in what was going on around me, unlike in the first game. The mystery isn't engaging and everything just blends together to be really basic dark fantasy.

Suffice it to say, my disappointment is immeasurable, and my day is ruined.

Do NOT buy this game, it's a waste of your money. Buy Salt and Sanctuary. If you already have it, buy it for a friend.

Do NOT play this game, it's a waste of your time. Just play, or replay the original. Or any other game which has more substance than this.

A very tragic title.

It isn't Salt and Sanctuary. It takes a lot of the same mechanics and applies them to a Monster Hunter style game where you explore an area and hunt bosses. And it does it really well. The boss fights are punishing, but most of them reward you for going slow and learning the bosses and executing well. The weapons feel much more balanced than Salt and Sanctuary. There is no optimal weapon or build here. Some are strong, but not that much stronger than others. The game rewards you for trying different options and getting good with one that you like. 

The end of the game starts to feel extremely frustrating and unfair though. Enemies that don't have good counterplay for your weapon, enemies positioned at the edge of ledges just so you're unable to fight back. It was difficult and not enjoyable to complete.

The camera for two players is straight up broken with the platforming challenges.

I would describe it as a monster hunter souls mix. I highly recommend even if you have no clue what the first game is.

가벼운 무기로는 절대 뚫리지 않는 적의 가드, 프롬게임이었으면 적의 뒤를 잡아 공격하여 가드를 해제하겠지만, 이 게임, 구르기 한번으로 뒤 잡는게 편한걸 산정하였는지 적도 가드한채로 즉발로 뒤를 돌아버립니다. 결국 수동적으로 적이 가드를 풀고 나한테 돌진할 때까지 거리 벌리고 멀뚱히 서있는 수밖에 없네요.

가장 극혐인 요소는 이 게임이 2d인데 보스들의 패턴이 설치형, 장판형 패턴으로 도배되어 있습니다. 굴러도 구른 곳에 장판을 깔아놨네? 그럼 얄짤없이 처맞습니다. 이러한 패턴의 파훼법은 그냥 도망다니면서 거리 벌리고 장판이 사라질때까지 기다리는겁니다. 장판의 사각지대 따윈 없으니까요. 

이러한 요소들 때문에 가까이 붙어서 인파이팅한다는건 정말 리스크밖에 남지 않게 됩니다. 결국 전투도 딜찍 or 수동적인 플레이만 남게 되어 파고들 요소마저 사라졌습니다.

Als ein Spieler des ersten Teil (Salt and Sancutary) hatte ich große Hoffnungen und freute mich, als ein Nachfolger angekündigt wurde. Ich kam also mit Erwartungen an das Spiel und einige davon wurden erfüllt, andere weniger.
Im Vergleich zum Vorgänger wird in diesem Spiel deutlich mehr wert auf das Kämpfen selbst gelegt, so viel sogar, dass du die meiste Zeit mit diesen verbringen wirst.

Kurzform: Falls du nur einen schnellen Eindruck haben willst: Erwarte kein Salt and Sanctuary 2, dies ist eher ein 2D-Monsterhunter mit einem knappen Hintergrund vor dem Universum von Salt and Santuary. Die Story ist nahezu irrelevant und für die Geschichte gibt es nur eine einzige Entscheidung am Ende und die ist nahezu irrelevant.
Insgesamt würde ich dem Spiel 6/10 Punkte geben, ich wollte es zuende spielen, werde jedoch höchstwahrscheinlich keinen zweiten Run starten. Durch die repetitive Art verbringst du viel Zeit dieselben Minibosse immer und immer wieder zu töten, was schlussendlich als zentrale Spielmechanik der Spielererfahrung mehr schadet als hilft.

Was mochte ich: 
Das Spiel bietet anspruchsvolle Main-Story-Bosse, bei denen du als Spieler sehr wohl aufpassen musst. Der Grafikstil ist wie schon beim Vorgänger mit handgezeichneten Grafiken sehr schön und gibt dem Spiel eine gute Atmosphäre, die durch die Musik nur weiter erhoben wird. Die Level sind auch gut gestaltet, Collectables ordentlich verteilt.

Monsterhunter statt Metroidvania
Das Spiel wird nach der Einführung sehr repetitiv. Was ich damit meine ist, dass du gut die Hälfte deiner Spielzeit damit zubringen wirst, Bosse durch die Level zu jagen, die vor dir wegrennen. Diese Magier, wie sie genannt werden, haben jeweils ein Element, einer mag ein WIndmagier sein, der andere ein Nekromant. Dies ist zunächst sehr schön, wandelt sich jedoch schnell von Freude zu Frust, als dass diese Magier ähnlich wie Monster in den Monsterhunter-Spielen immer wieder auftauchen und deine Ausrüstung von diesen Magiern abhängig ist. Du kannst Ausrüstung auch normal finden, jedoch geht das Meiste durch diese repetitiven Magier. Weiterhin wirst du diese, egal ob du sie nun jagst oder nicht, im Level finden, wobei sie dir gut im Weg stehen können oder das Backtracken durch sie Regionen unnötig langsam macht. Diesen Teil der Gameplays kann man mögen oder auch nicht, je nachdem womit du Spaß findest, jedoch leidet in meinen Augen das allgemeine Spiel unter dieser Jagdtidee, da die eigentlichen Bosse, die du abseits der Magier treffen wirst, praktisch irrelevant werden und im Hintergrund verschwinden. Allgemein sorgt dies alles für ein sehr vollgestopftes Level, in denen an allen Enden überall etwas rumläuft und es sich mehr wie ein Schlachtfeld anfühlt, als eine ominöse Jagdt nach wirklichen Bossen. Da ich den eigentlichen Stil von Salt and Sanctuary mochte, stieß mir dies schnell schlecht auf.

Ohne Magier wärs besser gewesen!
Ein weiteres Problem ist, dass manche Gegner die Tendenz zeigen, immer wieder, nahezu ohne Cooldown angreifen, was in den sowieso schon oft engen und auch sehr vertikalen Leveln dazu führen kann, dass du von etwas gestriffen wirst und daraufhin in den Tod stürzt. Ebenso sorgt dieses stetige Angreifen dafür, dass du zwar einen anspruchsvollen Kampf findest, dieser jedoch aus den falschen Gründen schwer ist, da derselbe AoE mehrfach am Stück fliegen kann und dir praktisch keinen Raum zum Ausweichen lässt, wodurch es statt zu einem methodischen Kampf zu einer hektischen Prügelei verkommt. Da die Bosse alle ungefähr dasselbe Moveset haben, jedoch sich ihr Element unterscheidet, bietet dies keine wirkliche Abwechslung während des Spielens. Es sieht gut aus, verliert sich aber im Schema und irgendwann fühlt es sich alles gleich an, sogar soweit, dass ich den meisten Spaß in Regionen hatte, in denen es keine Magier gab.
Da diese Magier jedoch für deine Ausrüstung relevant sind und du auch den Großteil deiner Upgrade-Materialien von diesen Magiern bekommst, kannst du sie schlecht ignorieren. Entwickler, kehrt bitte zu eurem ersten Spiel im Stil zurück.

Weniger ist mehr!
In all diesem Sturm gehen dabei viele Eindrücke für die Region verloren, insofern hätte ich mir hier eher wenige, aber stärkere Gegner gewünscht, anstatt das ständige Chaos auf dem Bildschirm. Salt and Sanctuary hat das in meinen Augen deutlich besser gemacht. Durch die Bossinflation in Sacrifice verlieren die einzelnen Bosse jede Bedeutung und schlussendlich wirkt das Spiel mager, da die Story lediglich ein Hindergrund für die Magierjagdt ist und bis zum Ende nahezu irrelevant im Spielgeschehen verschwindet. 

Story/Inhalt/Handlung
Da ich mir nach Sanctuary mehr aus dem Setting erhofft hatte, hat mich dies hier sehr enttäuscht. Falls du das Spiel für das Setting kaufen würdest, dann würde ich dir eher andere Spiele empfehlen, denn die Handlung dieses Spiel könnte man in einem 5 minütigen YouTube Video zusammenfassen. Einerseits versucht das Spiel tiefe zu simulieren, indem es an vielen Stellen wie dem Skilltree einzelne Kommentare hinterlässt, jedoch kann man all diese optionalen Dinge mit "Magier sind Böse und müssen getötet werden" zusammenfassen. Der Hauptstrang fühlt sich ebenso an, du wird lediglich durch die Regionen gejagt, woraufhin du ein neues Level für deinen Teleporter findest. Dieses Spiel hätte auch "Magehunter123" heißen können, abseits von 2 Arealen fühlt es sich an, als ob die Entwickler die Handlung vollständig vergessen oder ignoriert hätten.

Multiplayer
Die Multiplayer-Erfahrung ist praktisch abseits von Coop mit Freunden nicht vorhanden. Da dieses Spiel im Schnitt die Hälfte der Spielerschaft von Sanctuary hat, wird sich das auch nicht groß ändern. Funktionieren tut der Multiplayer, jedoch verschärft er nur die oben genannten Probleme, wodurch ich dieses zu vermeiden suchte.

Abschließend: Falls du Monsterhunter magst, oder einfach nur hirntot irgendetwas dreschen willst, dann lohnt sich das hier vielleicht für dich, jedoch wirst du mehr Spaß aus einem neuen Run in Sanctuary ziehen, während du weiterhin auf Bloodborn for PC wartest. Schade eigentlich. 6/10

I took the other reviews with a grain of salt (no pun intended) and thought maybe it's a matter of taste. Well, if it is, my taste is I absolutely hate this. In the first area I mostly had fun - a bit like Monster Hunter, good pacing.

The second area was a bit iffy. Often frustrating, many unfair situations. If the signature of Soulslike games is "tough, but fair" or "if you die, it's your own fault", then this is not a Soulslike, and it begins to become evident here. Fighting multiple mages at once is fun at first but rapidly becomes a chore or a war of attrition. You attack them, maybe you die or retreat to get supplies, but it doesn't matter since most of the fights are persistent. Even so, most gear is crafted from mage parts, so you're going to be grinding. On top of this sometimes a mage may become completely entrenched on a platform with no way to climb up without being knocked back down.

By the third and fourth areas the problem was very evident: Running through the entire hub every time just to go to a different area. Too many traps that are too hard to see and too hard to dodge. Most of the traps just send you flying off a ledge, but tend to juggle you. There's also terrible platform layouts combined with frustrating controls - dodge and grapple are the same button, for example., or how grappling itself doesn't work straight up and has no direction control, or how inconsistent air attacks can be done.

I paid $10 for this but I still don't think it was worth the money. I hate to say it but my life would have been better if I didn't play this game. There's some good ideas in here for sure, but the execution is just not fun - this game is more like a job.

TLDR: I kinda liked the good points of the game but absolutely HATED the bad ones.

I bought and kept playing this game because I wanted a metroidvania so bad. Also the purchase had a discount so it was kinda worth it.

The game has some okay highs and some ABYSSAL downs. I'll list some of them below:

PROS:
- The gameplay is very similar to salt and sanctuary but the builds are funnier to play and I tested a lot of them (Light armor is still terrible).

- The game is short but if you take your time to look for and hunt the mages it can be quite a nice length.

- Overall exploration was good.

- Enemy variety was also good despite some repetitions here and there.

CONS:
- The Hub. The portal that takes you to other maps are SO FAR AWAY from the starting point. Why? You need to visit the hub everytime and EVERY SINGLE TIME YOU NEED TO RUN AROUND TO REACH THE PORTAL

- Overall platforming. The platform movement in this game is terrible and the game has quite a lot of them.

- FLYING F**KING ENEMIES. What's with those flying enemies that keep shooting you waaaay out of your reach and they also can fly through walls and even if you have projectiles (arrows, throwing axes etc) you STILL can't hit them if they want to hide behind a wall.

- ENEMIES EDGE GUARDING. Sometimes the enemies will walk around and will choose to stay right at the edge of a platform that you want to climb. You have iframes in the edge grab animation but the enemies will start attacking as soon as you finish the animation. If you're able to try and jump over the edge, the enemy will still hit you and whatever hits you in the air COMPLETELY removes your momentum, so you fall straight down. It is even worse when a large enemy decides to edge camp you because in this case you're COMPLETELY unable to grab the edge or jump over. You need to wait for the enemy to leave the edge or kill them with a ranged weapon. The worst situation that happened to me was a mage in temple of embers that decided to edge camp a platform that you needed to use the grappling hook. There was no place to stay on so I could fire ranged weapons. I needed to constantly jump at him and use my melee attack only to fall and take 60% of my HP several times until the mage decided to teleport.

- Enemies invading boss arenas. This one kinda explains itself.

- Traps... just traps everywhere. Traps in this game are barely visible and they're simply everywhere. The damage that they deal gave me the impression that they're not designed to exactly kill you. Instead, their focus is to simply deal a considerable amount of damage and knock you off of a platform so you need to climb aaaaalll over again.

- The mage system that is the core of the game. This is either love it or hate it because the mages are bosses that summon minions around the map and they keep teleporting around so you need to chase and kill them. The terrible thing is that they don't stay dead. They keep respawning even if you don't rest at the obelisks. Sometimes in the second map (Bol something) they even spawn exactly at the obelisk so you need to fight or avoid him right at the spawn point. And it's even worse when they decide to teleport near annoying enemies. The good news is that they'll fight each other but you still would need to wait for their fight to end or risk get ganked by all of them at once. Also it happened to me in one of the last maps (dragonsomething peak) that THREE mages spawned in the same area. It was a nightmare.

- OST is just as lacking as salt and sanctuary but the first game at least had the still palace and nameless god ost which was kinda nice. This game doesn't have a single good song

This is definitely a good game, worth to play, but I think first game (S&Sanctuary) was much better.
Few thoughts/remarks:
1. Game force You to run around same locations over and over again in process of slaying mages (mini-bosses). This can be very boring after few times.
2. You all time fight mini-bosses (mages), and BOSS FIGHTS ceases to be exciting - they became easy and routin.
3. BOSS-slaying REWARDS became less satisfying, because You don't get any unic rewards - only exp and few talismans. No unic ingredients, items (usually), - nothing.
4. CRAFTING became much easier, but lost elements of discovery, searching for ingredients, combinations - just farm mini-bosses and choose items from list.
5. PARRYING became useless. And as a fan it hurts me :D
A) Even weaker mobs need to be parried 2-3 times to became stunned. And You can't parry few hits in a row, because off cooldown, so You need to parry last hits of combo or take remaining hits on block for not getting hit.

打完了，说个比较形象的比喻吧，玩家将在这款动作冒险游戏里体验到真三国无双里的杂兵一模一样的感受，法师，拦路头目和精英都是无双中的那些赵云，吕布之类的无双角色，AI就像我高中时疯狂拍打键盘享受割草的样子，形象，太TMD的形象了。

游戏初版我没体验过，不过看过视频评测，倒地和采摘现在都有无敌帧了，法师不会在追逐终局乱入，即使有乱入也会被直接踢出场景，传送功能实装，不用追法师到吐，无意义的跑图时间节省下来了，重甲也不会被一次秒，而是能顶住一次跑远喝个血如此循环（AI是离远了就变SB，玩远程可以轻松拿捏）但最影响体验的核心问题并没有做好，比如近战玩法就是抢招换血，所有强敌都是疯狂按手柄X,并打完一套后接个Y变招接着搞你心态，他们不会停，绝不会停！

说个开始玩那段时间非常搞我心态的一个操作设定改动，下梯子的判定动作在这一作改了，要判定成功必须手柄左摇杆在按前进的同时下拉，不然是无法产生下梯子的交互动作的，很神奇吧，游戏不是操作越做越丝滑，而是越做越累赘。

推荐自己的简易通关方法，我刚玩前两个小时一度想要删游戏给差评，但后来我发现了这游戏TO的武器模组——巨锤，巨锤的重击作为起手是有霸体的，也方便抢招（重击是可以稳定出怪硬直的，普通攻击不仅没法一击打出敌人硬直，同时攻击范围非常短，你能想象到一把长矛的攻击范围不如一把刺剑的攻击范围长吗？幽默一样的攻击判定设计，什么样的脑子能想出这玩意来的），因为游戏怪AI的问题，近战利用霸体招拼血是最优解，接着就是加点物理战士老办法，加负重上限，游戏早期重甲最好的是石头法师换的那套，后期可以换恶魔法师那套，不过正如我游戏时长所代表的那样，我没怎么刷，我是混搭重甲一直打到通关的。

然后多人根本联机没有人，买的人都少，能堵着气再体验它的其他内容大概也没什么必要吧

我认为这款游戏在更新到2.0后目前最大的问题是玩法和AI问题，AI的优先级更高，因为AI问题，游戏所谓构筑的玩法多数不成立，远程瞄准很操蛋，即使是堆补正也看不出它有什么大的作用，那伤害真幽默。玩过前作对这个续作还是挺失望的，精美的画面风格和还算有趣的世界观却有一个不合理的玩法和烂AI，尤其是体验盐与避难所多次，PS4版还做了全成就，盐避的BOSS逻辑是在出招前有固定起手动作，变招前必有闪避和起身翻滚开的操作行为，即使是女巫它也有变招前的前摇，因此我很不能理解这一作会在敌人攻击动作设计方面出问题，那个设计成熟，极具特色的前作竟然是出自同一个开发团队之手？我很困惑，这也导致我对Ska的编程能力相当质疑

在最后，我对可以看到这篇评论的玩家真正想给出的是不推荐！不推荐购买这款即使大修也依然很烂的作品
 这就好比饺子里包屎，扒开就臭。

这游戏的世界被法力所形成的雾霾所笼罩，追逐元素技能的法师如获至宝似的汲取这纯粹的力量进而令肉身化为诡异莫名的畸形，而法力四溢的源头竟是神罚所外溢出的躯壳，终局我选了爬树结局，（愿我们这些支持开发团队的玩家能够得到这些开发者最起码的尊重，一款好作品离不开主创团队的努力和坚持，合作才能让一款独具特色的作品呈现更好的状态，而一款好作品则是会在每位玩家余下的人生中被记住）这也是我心中的期盼，期盼能有第三部作品重现一代的辉煌

This is my review of Salt and SACRIFICE
I'm not really sure why people are giving this game a negative review based on the fact that it's not "Salt and Sanctuary"... You wouldn't yell at a chef for serving beef stew after he originally served potato stew. They both have their own merits and downfalls. You certainly can't fully discredit either for being unlike the other. That's just asinine. TL;DR: This is more of a 2D Monster Hunter set in the S+S world, and it's definitely worth playing through, especially if you have a few friends to play with or find some along the way.

~STORY~
Salt and Sacrifice is a overarching story about Sacrifice. A world plagued by mages, and the more of them you kill the more seem to keep coming up. It all ties together by the end, if you pay attention. It's a decent story considering I still can't tell you what most of Souls-likes are about.
My main troubles with the story points are my main troubles with any souls-likes with stories. I can't seem to find when or where the characters will show up in the world. That part requires a guide for me, as it has in all the Souls games as well as Elden Ring.

~GAMEPLAY~
The actual gameplay isn't what most souls-likes are. It's more of a 2D Monster Hunter game. You hunt monsters with friends, you collect materials, you craft weapons with said materials.
The gameplay is smooth as butter. You choose an initial "class" which basically just starts you off in different places on the skill tree. You have many different weapon types to choose from, a lot more than the previous entry, and it's a lot more robust in terms of skills, magic, abilities, etc.
The other awesome fact are the different "tools" you find that helps you unlock different areas. It's a great system.
My gripe with this system is that armor still has 2 types. Light and Heavy. You have to unlock them in the skill tree. But bosses can only give you 1 set. So you'll end up not able to get an armor because you wear Light Armor and not Heavy and vise versa. It doesn't makes sense in this kind of game. Armor shouldn't have been split into 2 sets like this, or behind a skill tree unlock. The game is hard enough having to upgrade all the armor it doesn't need all this extra to weigh it down.
I do love the weapon system here, even with the unlocks in the skill tree, it's a lot of fun to get new stuff and try out what their skills are.
The downside to that(or upside depending on how you look at it) is that boss skills are bound to weapons, and you don't inherently get them or anything. The upside to that is that you swap weapons and you have new stuff to use without having to bind the skills individually. The downside is that your weapon type might not come with good skills. The real tragic downside though is if you don't have the skill tree unlocked for using focus skills, then they're just dumb weapons that don't have any skills. That doesn't seem right, but it's the way it is.
The game is too damn difficult for a monster hunter game though. Like in MHW, you kill these huge monsters and they are all initially hard, but then you get better gear and they feel like "Oh yeah, I could totally trash this mob in my sleep", I found a lot of mages aren't that. Especially because they are leveled depending on the zone. So a sanguimancer will always 2-3 shot you. Despite farming him and being decked up in fully upgraded pieces of HIS armor, he'd still mop the floor with me if I wasn't immaculate in my timing. There's something so sour about that. The monsters mages spawn also go up and become really tough towards end game, despite your cool flame resistant gear or whatever, those little dragons the Dracomancer spawns can still two shot you somehow....
The skill tree: Why? I have yet to really notice if dexterity or strength do much of ANYTHING in relation to actual attack numbers. Can anyone confirm? Do they? I felt this way in the earlier entry too. I upgrade gear and that makes a difference, but it seems the game doesn't actually care or the difference is so minute it might as well not care. So as it stands it's just a way for character customization and devoting yourself to one skilltree, instead of multiple.

~ATMOSPHERE~
I'm gunna lump music, ambiance, sound, and general vibes up here.
It's a 2D platformer, so there's only so much the dev could have done to really spice things up. Random mobs don't really make a lot of noises other than their attacks. Mages are silent also. Would have loved to hear more roaring and hollering. But I guess that's because it's a multiplayer game? I dunno. With that having been said, there's nothing quite as satisfying as seeing the screen shake, the heavy guitar and drums swell as you rip the heart out of a mage and crush or devour it. That's VERY satisfying, even after the 40th mage.
All in all my play of Salt and Sacrifice was very rewarding. Having beat it I will go back to play NG+. I somehow missed two types of enemies, and that's maddening, lol. I also didn't complete any of the NPC quests. It was definitely worth it for the reduced price from having owned S+Sanctuary. Having played it doesn't make me appreciate the former any more, like some may claim. It's its own game, and deserves the respect it was built with.
P.S. Two dudes basically made this whole game... I don't see why there's all this hate for the fact it's not what they were expecting. That's sooo rude. They worked night and day on this for probably a few years, only to have a thousand die hard Sanctuary fans give it a heck tone of flack because it wasn't what they wanted.
Well, message received, if the dev bothers to make another game(or can afford to) then I'm sure S+Sanctuary 2 will come out. It's basically a different genre of game anyways :P

This game is dreadfully mediocre because it takes a few different influences and meshes them together poorly. I'm not going to elaborate on everything here, but here is my basic gist of the game.

The Good: Cooperative gameplay, weapons, items, world-building, characters.
The Mediocre: Wandering mage hunts, enemy placement, platforming.
The Bad: Single Player, PVP, character building, enemy design, perfect guards, stamina system, equipment loads

While it has been stated that the game takes influence from Monster Hunter, it's pretty much just in the way you gather items and make your weapons; otherwise it's a souls-like through and through. Most of the bad is the enemy design crapping on everything else. Most regular enemies have 1-3 attacks while mages have somewhere around 3-6. Most enemies constantly attack, and attack quickly, not leaving much room for anything really. Mages will chain attack after attack until you are dead, sometimes releasing multiple delayed ones at once (screw the sanguimancer and electromancer especially). This makes for boring, frustrating single-player gameplay, and just okay gameplay cooperatively, because playing with other people is fun.

Here are the other main gameplay problems: 

Stamina is way too easy to deplete for how quickly enemies attack. With the speed of enemies in this game, it probably would have been better to just eliminate it all together, because you will all too often find yourself out of it, while the mage releases their never ending onslaught. You also lose so much stamina failing a perfect guard, that you basically need to be perfect at it to get anywhere. There's also the fun of having a slower reaction time to doing something else on a regular guard as opposed to a perfect. Stamina punishes the player, especially when alone while enemies are free-ranged spam lords of the worst caliber. The other problem is how long it takes to break enemy guards. Even with some pretty heavy weapons, tons of enemies will guard several strikes and then send you flying with a speedy strike.

The roll is too long for how much platforming there is, and too slow if you want to wear any sort of armor without equipment load gouging. You'll fall off of many platforms rolling because it covers a ridiculous distance for the type of game this is. If you have a moderate or heavy equip load, most mages will catch you with another attack right after dodged the first one because they are too slow. Heavy characters really only work well in groups for a good chunk of the game.

Single player just seems tacked on with how the enemies are balanced. They are made to smack around groups of people, and nothing changes when you play single player. It can all be accomplished alone, sure, but it's not fun, just annoying. PVP is much the same, there are a few factions, and it's just, who can spam quicker, or who's got that poise.

Good souls-likes usually have a few flaws in some areas, but shine throughout. Salt and Sacrifice is full of irritating flaws, with the saving grace that playing with other people is enjoyable. It's the only reason I keep playing the game, other than my enjoyment of building characters. There's definitely a good game in there, but it really should have been baked and tweaked because the character speeds and enemy speeds are at war with each other. Really this game acts like it wants to be a hack and slash, but is bogged down by its souls-like and Monster Hunter influence. If they really just slowed down the enemies a bit, it would be much better. It's obviously balanced for multiplayer, but I'd prefer an overly easy multiplayer experience and a relatively tough single-player over what it is: A pretty easy multiplayer, with a hot garbage annoying single-player experience.

This game only gets a recommendation because I'm still playing it for jolly cooperation.

This game is good. Is it Sanctuary good? No. But it's good. most of my problems lay with the fact that a lot of combats feel similar. While I didn't get a lot into the Runic Arts part of combat, the movesets of the weapons were somehow both too fast and too slow with little variation between moves. I also feel like this game isn't Salt and Sanctuary 2, but rather a totally different game that drew some inspiration from the original. When I bought this game, I expected something that expanded on the concepts of the first game, but got something totally separate. The familiar weighty combat has been traded for flashy movesets, and the beautifully crafted world disappeared in place of a level-based game, more like Demon's Souls than anything. The enemies are numerous and hit hard, and a lot of the bosses have frustrating, screen-filling attacks that shred your health.
While you may think that this all amounts to a negative experience, I found the thing that fixes it all: Multiplayer. Having another Inquisitor joining you on your journey turns this frustrating game into a wonderful time with a friend, and allows you to revel in the flashiness and charm. Not only this, but it allows you to split a boss's attention, effectively negating the aggressiveness of enemies. A Souls-Like experience is traded for a Monster Hunter-esque bananza, and anyone who is a fan of local or online multiplayer needs to try this game out. 7/10

[h1] É uma mistura de tudo, que no fim, gerou nada. [/h1]
Salt and Sacrifice é uma continuação de Salt and Sanctuary, não em questão história, mas em "legado" assim digamos. S'Sacrifice é bem diferente de S'Sanctuary, por diversas inspirações que o developer teve, mas isso vou abordar abaixo da review curta.

[h2] Se você espera um Salt and Sanctuary 2, não compre. [/h2]
S'Sacrifice é um jogo que eu estava ansioso para jogar, não esperando que fosse como S'Sanctuary, mas sim, que seguisse algo semelhante, não em visual ou em estilo 2D-Souslike. S'Sacrifice segue algumas coisas que players de S'Sanctuary notariam, designs de áreas, inimigos que evoluíram (literalmente), uma árvore de skills melhor (caramba, essa é uma das coisas boas), mas o resto do jogo é uma completa mistura sem pé nem cabeça, mecânicas extremamente diferentes (retiraram o parry), lutas infinitas, combos sem escape nem depois de passar séculos no chão, áreas sem sentido pra luta, o jogo é um horror.

[h2] Mudanças dentro do jogo [/h2]
O jogo recebeu as seguintes mudanças: [b] Lobby, Mapa, Parry (Perfect Block), Magias, Bosses (Caça) e Monstros [/b].

[h3] Lobby [/h3]
Agora para você upar, você precisa voltar ao Lobby, ou seja, tem que ir a um checkpoint e voltar para outro mapa, que fica fora da área "jogável". Lá tudo é passivo, só tem NPC's e alguns testes de mecânicas novas.

[h3] Mapa [/h3]
Diferente de S'Sanctuary, aqui os mapas não são interligados, mas sim, separados por portais, você precisa voltar ao Lobby, para ai então trocar de mapa, seja para farmar, explorar ou caçar. Tirando a mecânica de caça, que será explicada depois.

[h3] Parry/Perfect Block [/h3]
Aqueles que amavam jogar de Sword'n Shield perdem toda a prática agora, removeram o parry, agora é um sistema de Perfect Block, onde se você defender no momento certo, você nega o dano e fica sem stunlock, caso erre o tempo, você recebe o dano reduzido (em % por arma) e perde fôlego.

[h3] Magias [/h3]
Não existem de forma solo (pelo menos ao que eu entendi), elas são atribuídas a armas, a barra de mana se recupera DENTRO das armas, caso você troque, perde a magia.

[h3] Bosses [/h3]
Como vou explicar, o jogo te apresenta os bosses e na primeira tentativa de acabar com eles, tu consegue chegar até eles de forma normal, sem muito problema (se você ignorar as IA's que invocam monstros infinitos, combos aéreos e ranged spell span infinito), o real problema está na próxima vez que você visitar o mapa, os bosses ANDAM literalmente e eles spawnam naturalmente, ou seja, você pode estar lutando com o Boss X1 e o X2/X3 e sua trupe vão spawnar próximos, seja na outra sala, área ou até mesmo irão até você, podendo te ajudar a acabar com o X1 ou te causando mais problemas. Eles também não tem mais loot drop fixo, tudo agora é % (estamos no Terraria agora).

[h3] Monstros [/h3]
Diferente de S'Sanctuary, monstros que spawnam tem i-frames e podem te atacar enquanto saem do boss (ou seja, você pode morrer sem sequer causar dano). Monstros parecem ter uma carência de fôlego, vão ficar atacando infinitamente até você morrer ou sair de perto (para te atacarem a distância) e não vão te perseguir tanto (como faziam no S'Sanctuary).

[h2] Eu gostei da tentativa de originalidade, é um jogo legal [/h2]
Eu recomendo você comprar caso queira muito testar ou tenha fé de que vai gostar, fora isso, recomendo você passar longe do jogo, ele é um poço de miséria em vários aspectos, mas não deixa de te divertir.

Salt and Sacrifice получилась довольно неоднозначным проектом. С одной стороны, игра изобилует невероятным количеством гринда (Который еще и необходим для комфортного прохождения), запредельной сложностью на всех своих этапах, да и в целом баланс в игре поломан вусмерть.. А с другой стороны.. Ничего из этого на самом деле нет?
А теперь конкретно и по фактам.
Касательно гринда - он тут очевидно есть, но.. Есть и небольшой нюанс. "Гриндить" тут придется разве что тем, кто не в состоянии осилить базовые механики игры. Условный первый мир (Обучающий, можно сказать), не представляет никаких проблем в принципе, даже если игрок не придумал ничего лучше, кроме как ПЕРЕТАНЧИВАТЬ магов [spoiler] (Что, к слову, работает отлично даже без брони) [/spoiler] . Даже если так убийство мага всё ещё дается тяжко, для малышей придумано еще одно упрощение - полноценный бой с таким магом происходит при неполном хп у последнего. Убивать Именных/Сюжетных магов для прохождения достаточно лишь по одному разу, а ресурсов на крафт оружия, подходящего под класс, падает более чем достаточно. Касательно брони с магов - сугубо эстетическое удовольствие, статы с брони в целом то и не нужны, поломанный баланс игры, как никак.
Кстати о поломанном балансе. "Поломанный баланс" в игре такой же поломанный, как и руки тех, кто пишет о его существовании. При обычном прохождении на NG, проблем не возникает ни при обычной беготне, ни на файтах с магами [spoiler] Мерзкие враги с грабами не в счет, на то они и мерзкие, правда? [/spoiler] . Быстро убивают? Стоит попробовать уклонения (С IFrame'ами всё в порядке). От атаки нельзя уклониться? Окно для парирования секунды в 2(!) - к вашим услугам. Если же реакция хуже, чем у пенсионера - верный друг в лице блока как всегда тут (Весь урон это конечно не погасит, но пережить тот самый ваншот (Который на NG еще поискать надо), вполне получится)). К тому же мувсеты у боссов в большинстве своем довольно простые и легко читаются, более того, набор атак у магов схож [spoiler] Комбинация Физ. атак/Проджектайлы/Проджектайлы х2/Уникальная атака [/spoiler] - возможно звучит сухо для боссфайта, но из-за стилизации и подгонки под каждого мага не надоедает.
Отдельно пробегусь по "беготне" за магами, которая от силы занимает аж 3 минуты на каждого нового мага [spoiler] коих по штуки 4 на мир [/spoiler] , а вдобавок учитывая, что каждого такого достаточно убить по одному разу, сегмент с "беготней" за всеми магами в совокупности составляет +-час. В игре на 15 часов, сердце геймплея которой - охота на магов. Растягивание продолжительности небывалых масштабов.
Каким бы вылизыванием игры и её защитой не показался текст выше, на деле в ней более чем достаточно минусов, причем некоторые из которых и вовсе перекочевали из прошлой части. Но обо всем относительно кратко.
Необязательные контракты на охоту порой работают крайне странно, в результате чего магов приходится убивать на арене с мобами, в связи с чем боссфайт превращается в какой-то праздник ультранасилия с кучей светящихся проджектайлов и АоЕ атак. По итогу, уворачиваться приходится от всего подряд, только не от атак самого босса.
Что касается обычных сюжетных боссов - они крайне слабы как в плане сложности, так и в плане дизайна - они банально скучные. Про рескины [spoiler] Kraekan wyrm -> Kraeaxenar (Убогие хитбоксы сохранены) [/spoiler] и [spoiler] The coveted -> Sapblood heart (Вдобавок скучнейший бой) [/spoiler] лучше вообще не вспоминать, отсутствие боссов было бы в разы лучше их наличия.
Небольшое полуспойлерное ответвление касательно финального босса - [spoiler] Он просто отвратителен. Безымянный из первой части был в разы лучше [/spoiler]
А вот с дизайном и арт стилем у игры всё на уровне, по лекалам первой части. Так же мрачно, удручающе, персонажи выглядят неказисто, а окружение и локации отдают излишней гладкостью и простотой, что общей картине лишь играет на руку. А вот о музыке, в частности об эмбиенте, хорошего особо и не скажешь. Большую часть времени придется бродить под звуки ходьбы главного героя, его ударов и испускающих дух врагов. В остальном же - какая-то невыразительность. Благо хоть на боссах всё разбавляется каким никаким остом.
Последнее, что стоит отметить в минусах - такой же сломанный, как и в первой части, NG+. Вот где встречаются настоящие ваншоты от ловушек и грабов. [spoiler] 220/300 урона, при максимальном хп в 160 [/spoiler] - звучит не как сложность, а как отсутствие реворка со времен первой части.
По итогу лишь получается сказать, что Salt and Sacrifice - хорошая, пускай и не без минусов - соль, но плохой продолжатель идей Salt and Sanctuary. Потребитель банально ждал другую игру, вот и вся соль.

Enemies can really easily juggle or stunlock you or just straight up launch you off into a fall long enough to outright kill you. Especially if the game drops multiple mages into the same spot. 

But really what's worse about that is that this game doesn't really feel all that hard. I often kill mages with health potions to spare, and even then you can run back to an Obelisk to resupply, without it seeming to have any negative impacts on the fight. It's just... tedious. Upgrading equipment forces you into some really time-consuming, samey farming. If the mages that drop the required materials even spawn. Souls-like metroidvania Monster Hunter sounds cool until you realize that means Rathalos Plate farming.
Might be fun for multiplayer or co-op, I guess.

Le jeu est prenant avec des mécaniques solides. L'exploration est intéressante, avec les divers outils à obtenir pour progresser. Il y a plusieurs build possible et les armes ont des movesets bien variés (Combo léger et lourd en plus qu'il existe des coups secrets en tenant haut ou bas en frappant.) 

Pour ce qui est des boss, l'attrait principal de ce genre de jeux, nous sommes servis. Il existe bon nombre de gardien de zone qui sont 'classique' à cette franchise. Sauf que l'attrait est dans les Mages, venant dans toutes les combinaisons élémentaires possible. Ils vont se déplacer dans la zone, invoquez des ennemis pour les protéger et enclencher le duel seulement lorsque blessé. En soit, ils vont se battre intelligemment. 

Après avoir terminé le jeu, j'ai recommencé avec un nouveau perso parce que c'est juste si bon. Je vais faire d'avantage usage du multijoueur pour modifier mon expérience.
