Многие уже сказали: это не Salt and Sanctuary! Так и есть. От "первой части" осталось немногое: прокачка, боевая система и визуальный стиль. В остальном же, это уже не Souls-like, это Monster Hunter. Здесь мы будем охотиться на магов и изгонять их. Сразу скажу, стоит вам убить 1-2 по заданию, эти не-товарищи начнут появляться во всех углах карты.
Платформинг стал куда лучше и полезнее. Один крюк, открываемый почти в начале будет помогать вам оптимизировать путь очень сильно (по крайней мере, на первой локации). Остальные способности я не успел открыть, но могу отметить, что не видел тех, что помогали нам в прошлой игре разработчиков.
Сам геймплей стал более нудным, но более быстрым. После нескольких прохождений Санктуария, где ты видишь цель, но утыкаешься в препятствия, здесь ты просто напросто гриндишь. Очень много. Нужного босса догони, поймай, убей. Со временем это начинает немного надоедать, но как и с любой другой игрой про гринд, зайти на час-другой вечером самое оно. Прогрессия при этом стала куда лучше ощущаться. Больше вещей, ощущается их важность, циферки в броне стали значить. 
Графика. Она стала менее мрачной, более красочной, больше эффектов, деталей. Это очень субъективный вопрос, но я могу отметить, что обе игры, сохраняя похожий стиль, ощущаются абсолютно по-разному. Они обе уникальны и обе прекрасны. 
Звук. Новых композиций стало меньше. В каждом бою слышать одну и ту же мелодию утомляет. Но в контексте этой игры не грех поставить на фон лекцию и ощущение особо не изменятся.
В общем, не смотря на количество отрицательных вещей, которые я мог написать выше, мне игра очень понравилась. Я ждал её появления на этой платформе и не был разочарован. Интересно погрузиться в этот мир, узнать его лор и почему происходит ровно то, что происходит. Вероятнее всего, я проведу в этой игре куда больше времени, чем в предшественнике, но это не возвышает ни Жертву, ни уничижает Святилище.

[h1]游戏介绍[/h1]
《盐与献祭》是横板魂类游戏《盐和避难所》的最新续作，该作动作系统、人物养成、叙事风格沿袭了前作的优秀风格，画面清晰度和细节相比于前作也更加精细化了，音乐还是一如既往的优秀，而且本作还支持联机合作，玩家可以寻一好友一起受苦，难度也会稍微降低不少，但本作存在很多退步的地方，下面就简单聊一聊我的游玩感想。
[h1]游玩感想[/h1]
1、 地图设计问题。相互连通的大地图改成很多中型地图通过火鸡场传送连接，因为引入怪猎的“换区”机制，为了给BOSS换区服务而严重影响类恶魔城地图设计的水准，感受不到精妙的连接和机关，就是“小巷子通向各个大区域”，探图无聊。
2、 人物设计问题。怪物设计偷懒，改为地图守关BOSS + 无限刷新的同质化BOSS，有些不伦不类，并且BOSS几乎都是“召唤小怪、发射物、近战武器拍一下”，战斗无聊。和一代一样，角色韧性设置很奇怪，被怪打飞、被机关创飞。创建角色界面只显示职业名字、武器，但是却不显示各职业的属性。
3、 玩法设计问题。缆车，毫无新意，并且只能在“双脚站立与地面”时才能交互，改成“在起点范围内即可交互”玩家的体验会更舒畅；平台召唤，类似一代的火把平台，毫无新意。
[h1]总结[/h1]
作为一代的老粉丝不得不说续作完全偏离的我的期待，有很多改变但是感觉都没有做好，然而游戏本身的“探索”又有一定的吸引力，如果不是一代的粉丝，被画面吸引，那么请去玩一代；如果是一代粉丝，那么本作是无法满足你对延续前作体验的期待的，可以半价再入。

游戏本身谈不上难，只求打过的话各种武器战技、法术、投掷道具都可以让你较轻松的打过 boss 。但最糟糕的地方是各种大距离的击飞，我都不记得莫名其妙被打飞之后飞了十万八千里掉过几层地图摔死多少次了，那些地图根本是精心设计过让怎么来摔死玩家的。

广为诟病的追野生法师在这种地图环境下简直是恶梦。没有地图、引导等于没有、开荒期不熟悉地图、僵硬的角色加上跳跳乐地形，追着个到处跑的法师，一不小心被吹飞摔死——又要重新跑一次。真是玩得人想摔手柄。

DLC地图唱诗班区域真是集大成，本来就是从上到下走的跳台，在必经之中上安排可以把人直接扫飞的激光，你一下没抓到攻击的间隔，直接扫飞掉到最底层，万一没死，下面还有两只大怪等着你，你躲都没处躲。

Just beat the game at ~24 hours. I will recommend the game and don't agree with all of the bad reviews/feedback on the game. It's more like Salt and Sanctuary than other reviews would have you believe. However, there are some gameplay additions that make it feel different:

1) Hook mechanic like Sekiro.
2) A bunch of bounty type side quests for specific mobs to get items for gear like Monster Hunter.
3) A HP bar penalty if you die like Dark Souls 2.
4) A reduction of useable potions if you die to the same boss too many times in a row.
5) There are 1 shot mechanics, which is stupid af. You will get juggled by mobs and it's obnoxious.

I don't like 3/4 but the 1/2 additions are okay. However, the world is much more about exploration and upgrades vs a new ability automatically opening up a new area. The new weapon types and variations allow for a few various play styles (including a caster type). 

It's a rather short game. I have 46/56 achievements with my completion at 24 hours and could probably 100% the game in another 10 hours max.

Having said that, it's a welcome addition to the Salt series and I appreciate the devs taking some risks with new game play options. I hope they work on a 3rd and learn from some of the challenges/issues with Salt and Sacrifice.

他の方も仰ってますが、前作からどうしてこうなった？という感じでしょうか。
端的に言って、これなら「Salt and」なんてつけなければただの凡作程度の評価に落ち着いたと思うんです。
前作も小さな開発チームですから勿論完璧な出来ではありませんでしたが、所謂ソウルライクとメトロイドヴァニアのいいところを見事に取り入れ、歯ごたえのある雑魚敵や何度も殺されるボス戦の難易度、耳に残るbgm、恐る恐る進んでいく探索の高揚感、新たな装備や魔法を見つけた時の嬉しさ、そういうものが十分にありました。
今作はどうでしょうか？　雑魚は精々数が多くて召喚されるのが鬱陶しいくらい。ボスは逃げ回って雑魚を出すのが鬱陶しいもののボス戦自体が開始すると驚くほど弱い。いや、本当に弱い。全然プレイしてないじゃないかと言われればそうなのですが、私は前作では1周目、水被りの騎士に何度も何度も何度も殺される程度のプレイヤーなんですよ。それがどうでしょう？　チュートリアルボスはさておき、本編でボスを3体倒すところまで雑魚戦含めて1回も死にませんでした。ボス戦を3回やっても、です。勿論前作を何周もして他にEnder何とかや天穂の何とかやメトロイド何とかや、まぁ色々2Dアクションやりましたので、私が上手くなっている可能性もありますが、それにしたって弱い。ボスが鬱陶しいだけで弱いことが何よりも残念です。初見なんだから、あっさり殺しに来て欲しかった。なんで対応して回復して倒せる、これを3連続でできてしまうのか。Salt and Sacrificeなんて名前でさえなければ、「うーん、普通だなぁ」で済んだと思うんですけども。
モンハンに近くなったと仰る方がいて、確かにそれっぽいなと思いました。しかし、下手に前作のような2D探索要素をそこに混ぜた結果、ソウルライクとしては物足りない、メトロイドヴァニアとしても物足りない、モンハンとしても物足りない、何もかも中途半端になったという感じでしょうか。
2Dアクションが好きではなかった私に、前作が2Dアクションの楽しさを教えてくれたからこそ、ずっと待っていました。他プラットフォームで出たことを知り、その時に評判が怪しいことも知ってはいましたが……
なんでしょうね、次回作、に期待ですかね……

After 100% completion, I can honestly say that this game is excellent. Is it perfect? No. Are there valid criticisms? Of course. But the game is not nearly as bad as many reviews on Steam make it out to be. Apparently, many are still salty (yes, pun absolutely intended) that the developers did not make the same game twice. I like Salt and Sanctuary, but I am equally glad that Salt and Sacrifice is its own game. 

What to expect from this game:

(1) A plethora of different gear and weapons with a multitude of damage types (physical, fire, cold, poison, light, dark). You need to hunt mages and their minions for materials; the materials allow you to craft both armour and weapons. Some items can be found in chests, some can be bought with in-game silver from merchants. In some cases, you can unlock merchant items by killing non-mage bosses. There are named mage hunts, unnamed versions of the same hunts (tougher than the named versions), and fated hunts (which require tomes to unlock for each main area and reset every 24 hours).

(2) Quite a lot of platforming. As you progress through the game, you receive more tools to explore the maps.

(3) Soulslike combat in a 2D environment. Weapons have different attack combinations that cost stamina. You can attack, block, perfect block (parry), dodge, and jump. As you kill enemies, you receive salt (currency for levelling your character); if you die, you drop your salt in the place of your death and have a chance to retrieve it. If you die again before collecting it, it will be lost for good.

Criticisms:

(1) Poise has been useless so far, but the developers are currently working on making it more useful than it has been. In simple terms, even with high poise, you get ragdolled around like a toy pretty easily and stagger as much as in Dark Souls 3.

(2) In some locations requiring a lot of perfect platforming, ranged enemies and mages can stunlock you to death as you have no iframes during such actions as ladder climbing and jumping from platform to platform. You can get knocked off platforms and literally fly across half of the map. (This is not an exaggeration.)

(3) If you get knocked off a platform and die due to gravity, your salt will be where you died, that is to say, where you landed instead of where you were knocked off, which can be rather far away from where you were and need to go. In some cases, it is not worth trying to recollect the salt.

I am going to update this review in case my criticisms get addressed with further patches. 

In the meantime, I still highly recommend this game if you like soulslike combat and 2D platforming.

EDIT: I have mixed feeling about this game. I change my review from thumb up to thumb down for 2 times.

EDIT: It is not as good as Salt and Sanctuary, especially the game sometimes force you to do mage hunting missions. Hunting mission feels tedious at first, but after you build up you character, it feels a little better. But I would say the pacing has some issues regarding mage hunting missions and difficulty curve.

Base gameplay is fun for first 3 hours. It seems the cancerous in-air combos coming from enemies are removed. For the time being, I have not seen a more than 2-hit combo coming from enemies. So combat is still like the first game. 

The exploration, I would say it is worse than the Salt and Sanctuary. I skip encountered Mage Hunt opportunities for most of time, in favor of opening shortcut and finding items. So it is not super troubling for me, as I could decide when to start mage hunt at my own pace.

However, after 3 hours you beat the initial 3 mages, the mage starts randomly showing up and enhanced version of some of these mages are very tough and probably unfair (Water mage is spamming bubbles all over in your tiny screen and and hit boxes are crazy). I would say as the mage encounters are not very fun and sometimes frustrated to see so many enemies in screens (which you have to carefully push through). It feels really bad to see so many annoying distractions. 

What makes it worse is that progression of the game sometimes tied to hunted mage count, it is kindly ruins the fun of any Soul / Metroidvania game. Imagine in Souls' games, you had to collect a fixed number of things to progress!

However, after a few hours more it is more comfortable to mage hunting and it feels easier.

沒有想到必須點不推薦,儘管已經看到許多負評,這車還是翻得我猝不及防,先來個短評"除了美術風格外,這與一代不是一類遊戲,若是如我一般因一代而來者,可以放棄了",回看自己一代遊玩時數雖僅是二代兩倍,但當時體驗的是對遊戲熱情投入,而今這作是乏味折磨的草草了事,底下列幾個不推的點.
1.先從地圖設計說起,類惡魔城的地圖通常是富有探索樂趣的,精巧的安排各個不同區塊位置,相互的聯繫與捷徑,各區可能風格迥異的運作模式,讓玩家進入新區域會打起精神,或有柳暗花明的感覺,偶而安排的秘密或寶藏或多或少的讓這段探險值得期待,但這款卻偷工減料又粗劣的用個鏡門直接把各區割裂開來,同時每張地圖除了美術風格有差之外基本類似套路,乏味的跑圖打怪,各捷徑也是設置的非常不精巧根本不會有"原來通到這"的感覺;此外類魂的英高惡意有點類似jump scare,或是有挑戰性的少數路段,而這款卻是簡單粗暴的大量在必經之路上安排個敵人,例如必須向前跳躍攀爬一個平台,那麼平台上就會有個射手遠距離攻擊,在攀爬中又砍你兩刀,絲毫沒有那種依靠技術或謹慎探索的感覺,只是擺明阻礙.
2.遊戲中主要的BOSS"法師",就如其他評論所述設計這種傳送逃走的移動只有兩個字評價"拖沓",並且儘管聲光效果不同,攻擊模式稍有差異,但大致每隻法師都差不多打法,絲毫沒有與區域相關聯感,或差異性的新鮮感,模式糟糕之外還設計了刷素材做裝備這種像是名正言順重複利用怪物,以及重複跑圖延長遊戲壽命的做法.
3.戰鬥設計上,首先不知是是參考什麼設計了平衡但基本無用,大量怪物有固定的擊飛或擊倒,尤其搭配上粗暴設計的地圖,隨意就可以擊飛摔死;接著是防禦消耗耐力非常多,同時應該是彈反時機的完美防禦僅只減少耐力消耗,這高風險的行為不只無法直接觸發卸力反擊,連中斷敵方連擊都做不到,要完美防禦好幾次才可能觸發非常短的暈眩;最後我方防禦多數無法無傷之外還需要看方向,反觀敵方防禦卻都是全防無傷還無視方向全方位防禦,並且就連拿重型武器也需要好幾次才可將敵方耐力消耗完破防.
4.天賦與裝備,天賦樹雖然看起來與一代相似,但卻欠缺那種精妙安排值得研究走向的設計,流於粗劣的堆數值,裝備則是每種法師都出一整套裝備,除了需要無聊的刷素材之外,做出來的裝備也僅僅只是數值不同,沒有什麼特殊效果,認真的感覺到一種敷衍隨便,只為增加遊戲時長.
5.最後幾個雜項,地圖中有大量相隔一扇開啟的門,卻只能看到自己這邊的畫面,門對面卻全黑這種設計,而敵人是可以穿過門的,也就常常會產生被黑畫面中敵人攻擊的破事;飛行的怪物不僅打不下來還可以直接穿牆移動,又常常飄在無法近戰的高度,而我方的遠距彈藥卻是非常有限;遊戲中後期取得了漂浮布,竟然只有上升氣流有效,無上升氣流時連降落傘都不算真的莫名其妙;人物有攀牆跳牆的能力,但攀牆無法攀著緩慢下降就算了,連看準時機落地前攀牆跳還是會算掉落傷害.
說了這麼多,總之這是款失敗非常明顯的續作,尤其對玩過一代的玩家而言.

记忆里《盐与避难所》似乎是我在Steam上买的第一个游戏（虽然后来是在Ns上玩的），对于一代的感情不言而喻，因此对二代的期望也更高。
在隔壁独占期间就听说拉垮了，Steam发售后想亲自看看究竟是什么样。
总体来说，本作《盐与献祭》已经不能称得上是标准的类恶魔城，或者说缝合了奇怪的要素使得游戏显得不伦不类：
地图设计、人物养成、装备系统一定程度上沿袭了前作的类恶魔城 + 类魂
但是却又缝合了类似怪物猎人刷BOSS刷材料的玩法

谈谈优点：
+ 动作系统、人物养成、叙事风格沿袭前作的优秀风格
+ 哥特式画风在前作风格的基础上，色彩相对饱和一些，画面更加明丽清晰
+ 音乐音效优秀，虽然并不丰富，但是听感很舒服
+ 地图解密上（对应一代重力、隐藏平台）有全新的设计
+ 联机体验优秀，跨平台、数据继承OK

除此之外我觉得没有值得夸的地方了，退步的地方很多：
- 地图设计偷懒，相互连通的大地图改成很多中型地图通过火鸡场传送连接。
- 怪物设计偷懒，改为地图守关BOSS + 无限刷新的同质化BOSS，有些不伦不类，并且BOSS几乎都是“召唤小怪、发射物、近战武器拍一下”，战斗无聊。
- 因为引入怪猎的“换区”机制，为了给BOSS换区服务而严重影响类恶魔城地图设计的水准，感受不到精妙的连接和机关，就是“小巷子通向各个大区域”，探图无聊。
- 角色韧性设置很奇怪，被怪打飞、被机关创飞。一代就有类似的设定，体验并不好，探图难度不应该是一个机关直接把你打飞，而是需要更精致的机关和陷阱跳跳乐
- 创建角色界面只显示职业名字、武器，但是却不显示各职业的属性，请问不知道职业属性那玩家是在选什么？
- 地图探索的几个能力虽然有创新，但是恕我直言都很烂：
钩爪，操作手感和移动轨迹都令人无法理解的怪；
缆车，毫无新意，并且只能在“双脚站立与地面”时才能交互，改成“在起点范围内即可交互”玩家的体验会更舒畅；
平台召唤，类似一代的火把平台，毫无新意。
- 叙事风格和前作很像，但是内容远远不及，没有剧情设计，毫不吸引人，没有明显的支线任务和剧情。

作为一代的老粉丝不得不说续作完全偏离的我的期待，有很多改变但是感觉都没有做好，然而游戏本身的“探索”又有一定的吸引力，我的评价是“掺了海洛因的米其林答辩”
如果不是一代的粉丝，被画面吸引，那么请去玩一代；如果是一代粉丝，那么本作是无法满足你对延续前作体验的期待的，可以半价再入。

A warning if you're going in thinking this'll be Saltier And Sanctuary or something, this is more akin to a 2D monster hunter than to a dark souls. Salt and Sacrifice keeps the same aesthetic and combat style from its predecessor but reimagines it into a whole new setting with newer mechanics. The biggest thing is mages. You hunt mages. You track mages. You corner mages. You kill mages. You craft weapons out of mages. It's mages all the way down and is the crux of combat and the purpose behind exploration. 

The game is definitely enjoyable if you don't go in expecting another soulslike, but it does have its sore spots that might turn you off. It almost certainly did for me. For one, knockback is everywhere. Almost every enemy has at least one attack that'll send you flying and literally bounce you like a basketball off the ground, leaving you in a prone stat that, while keeps you invincible while you're on the ground, makes retaliating tough because most likely enemies will just knock you around again. You see this as early as the second region with the aeromancer- the wind mage. You fight him in a tree canopy high above solid ground and while his wind blasts don't hurt they'll toss you out of the area and kill you with fall damage. It can be infuriating and by far the worst aspect of the game since you knocking enemies back doesn't have as much impact other than satisfaction you spiked them like a volleyball. Even if you aren't immediately killed from fall damage, enemies tossing you off castle towers or floating platforms or from giant trees or into hazards is all over the game from moment one. It could just be the downside of a 2D game but it's certainly tuned up from it was in Sanctuary. That said, as I write this there is a balance update that should hopefully alleviate these issues if you're in heavy armor and wielding one of the heavier weapons.

Speaking of which, another issue to consider, albeit much less infuriating as the one above, is weapon variety. In Monster Hunter, from what I've been told as I haven't played it myself, all monsters' materials can make all weapon types. So if you wanted a flame greatsword or an ice greatsword you're set. This isn't the case for mages. Some mages lack the katana options, or dual dagger options, or great weapons, their armor sets are either heavy or light armor but never both, their ranged weapon is only one type so if you're trained in, say, a bow but all they have is throwing knives you either have to respect or you're SoL. It makes weapon options more limited because if you wanted ice daggers or a wind greathammer you'll be disappointed. Personally it isn't a dealbreaker for me since even early-game weapons can be upgraded to be feasible end game.

Now, despite this I give it a thumbs up because while the knockback is annoying as all hell (I remember a recent incident of fighting a large knight on a tower when all his attacks have knockback hard enough to launch me like Jigglypuff at 300% despite wearing heavy armor so it took me 10 minutes to beat him because of the constant climbing back up to get another shot at him) and the limited weapon variety is disappointing, they don't strike me as dealbreakers. Just something to get used to. The core gameplay of hunting down roaming mages as they rampage through the maps while exploring massive areas that only grow larger the more travel options you obtain doesn't seem to get old. There's no minimap but damage a mage hard enough before it escapes and it leaves a bloodtrail of sorts you can follow to the next location, and enemies normally found on the map by default aggro towards mages, who in turn aggro other mages in the area, creating a moshpit of chaos as 4+ mages in one section all summon minions and beat the crap out of one another. There was at least one instance when fighting the venomancer- poison mage- where a nearby knight enemy that was fighting it for a bit literally leaped into the arena with me mid-battle and started slicing the venomancer up when I was nearly dead. Not only did he get me the win but the poison from the venomancer killed him afterwards so I didn't even need to worry about him aggroing me now.

I'm rambling, bottomline is if you want a 2D monster hunter with more than a few sprinkles of dark souls for good measure, give this game a shot. Just watch out for knockback....

Salt and sanctuary was a game that I personally wasn't that big of a fan, clunky platforming, lack of map, questionable design choices hindered my enjoyment of the game, however despite these shortcomings i still really liked the art style, atmosphere and the bosses. I was kinda excited for salt and sacrifice since it had a lot of potential, but that was not the case. This game does so many things wrong and it's baffling how some of these mechanics made it into the final product. I played this game last year when it first came out and the experience was not pleasant. I had some hopes that some of the problems would at least get fixed with the steam release, seems like it was foolish of me to think that. My main gripe with the game is its idea of difficulty. The game thinks it's a good idea to have enemies who can bounce you around like a rag doll while you can't do anything about it. The game thinks it's a good idea that every enemy should two shot you, it thinks that it's a good idea to have 5 enemies in one spot who you can't just ran past them. It was bad on the release and it's still bad even though they had over a year to at least balance these problems. Mage hunts are still as tedious as ever, they never feel fun to do, and the game forces you to do them to progress the game. Some of these mages are horrendous, especially in the last two zones. Regular bosses are unmemorable and are easier than regular enemies in the zones that you are fighting them. I would only recommend this game if you have a friend who can play with you, it makes the experience slightly less frustrating. In conclusion i do not recommend this game, what a shame

[h1]Salt and... [spoiler]Grinding?[/spoiler][/h1]
Микс Demon Souls, какой нибудь не очень сложной метроидвании и Monster Hunter. Кроме стилистики, игра не имеет ничего общего с 1 частью.
[hr][/hr]
[list]
[*]Боевая система сделала несколько шагов назад. Нет возможности делать кастомные билды с оружием в каждую руку, нет талисманов на эти оружия, вместо этого можно носить только что то одно. А талисманы заменили на случайно выпадающие трофеи, увеличивающие случайные статы...зачем...
 [*]Каждая локация проходится абсолютно одинаково: Нашел и убил 1 мага - получил какое нибудь "сюжетное снаряжение" - нашел и убил 2 и 3 мага - где то в середине локи (может быть) встречается босс НЕ маг - нашел "ключ" к следующей локе. ВСЁ.
[*]Боссы (не маги) скучные, простые, незапоминающиеся. Просто большой моб с хп баром.
[*]Боссы (маги) скучные, простые, иногда ловишь рандомные ваншоты.
[*] Убийство магов это вообще отдельная тема. Сначала нужно бегать по всей локе, побеждая его "слуг" и преследуя гадёныша, потом спустя несколько минут беготни, наконец сразиться на арене, куда он всё это время и вёл... И так абсолютно с каждым магом всего их [spoiler](22)[/spoiler].
[*] Все локации очень сильно похожи друг на друга (кишка), очень сильно не хватает карты, хоть ее и не было в 1 части (и воспринималось это абсолютно нормально), тут она просто необходима, (благодаря элементам метроидвании) иначе игра превращается в симулятор камня, мимо которого ты проходил несколько часов назад.
[/list]
[hr][/hr]
В сравнении с 1 частью игра очень сильно оказуалилась, практически на каждом шагу можно пополнить фляги, они буквально никогда не заканчиваются.
Игра постаралась сделать упор на онлайн, но кто в нее будет играть. Здесь абсолютно нечего делать, кроме бесконечной и унылой "охоты" на магов.
Единственный плюс - очень хорошо получилось передать стилистику и визуально повторить первую часть, она вышла такой же красивой, мрачной и детализированной, на что я и повёлся...
Очень сильно ждал продолжения, не спойлерил себе игру с релиза в EGS и, как оказалось, зря... 
[hr][/hr]
[b]Не игра, а разочарование...[/b]

I wish I could just leave a review without a score because it's so hard to do with this game.

The recommendation comes with GIANT asterisk, because while I personally had fun I don't think that many people will and games is such a disappointment compared to Salt & Sanctuary.
The heavy oppressive atmosphere from first game is gone, the cool unique bosses are replaced by just a handful of notable examples and everything else just being Elemental-Force-Of-The-Month mages that feel very same-y, and whats worse, starts showing up during normal level exploration immediately.
Fun crafting system from first game where you transmutate your basic weapon to eldritch artifact is replaced by generic monster hunter mechanic of grabbing tons of same weapons but doing different elemental damage so you can dispatch mages weak to that element faster.
This game just feels at times like your generic AAA slop that was manufactured to meet as many current popular trends as possible without really wondering if it's gonna work.
Monster Hunter is popular? Let's add hunting Mages over and over again for materials.
Dark Souls is popular? Let's add souls invasion and co-op systems - because yes that is here too, and I don't know why.
Due to that, metroidvania aspects are now left lacking as game is now hub-based so the feeling of finding new areas and finding connections between them is very diminished considering you just teleport between them using your hub teleport and they are not connected otherwise.
At the end this game feels like it's lost it's unique identity and that's really sad considering S&S1 was phenomenal.

I still had fun which is why I'm recommending BUT I only had fun because I like -vania games, Souls games, and Monster Hunter clones. If I disliked any of those genres I'd probably dislike this game too, so if you are not like me you'd best avoid this, especially if you liked S&S1 as you'll only find disappointment here.

[h2]И вот почему:[/h2]

Геймплей претерпел не очень сильные, но от того не менее критичные изменения с прошлой игры.
Например, помните разные мувсеты, комбо и всё такое у одного типа оружия? Забудьте.
Теперь у вас есть несколько классов оружия, имеющих один единственный мувсет на весь класс.
Но самое смешное даже не это, а тот факт, что при этом оружие в рамках одного класса отличается по скорости атаки и расходу стамины. Это при том, что оружие, которое сейчас на вас экипировано, никак не влияет на нагрузку персонажа. 

[b]Механику оставили, но доделать заб[strike]и[/strike]ыли.[/b]

К сожалению, так можно сказать обо всей игре. Половина работающих здесь механик сделаны очень лениво, взять тот же крюк-кошку, который то не цепляется, то цепляется не туда, то не так...
И в целом игра представляет из себя качели. Как эмоциональные, так и геймплейные.
Первая локация игры после пролога, игра тебе будто бы говорит "Вот тебе босс, он проще некуда, со следующими тремя магами тоже разберешься, они простые."
Сразу после этого происходит катарсис, потому что игра последовательно выкидывает на тебя два скилл-чека - босс-осёл с крайне неприятным мувсетом, особенно под конец своего хп, и еще один маг, который засирает магией всю арену и ЛЕТАЕТ. Не очень высоко, но достаточно, чтобы атаки попадали лишь изредка, пока ты стоишь на земле.
И знаете что самое веселое? После всего этого, следующий маг, с которым вы столкнетесь, является просто грушей для битья. Можно сказать, что "ну, это отдушина за прошлые два боя" но нет. Это хреновый геймдизайн и еще более хреновый баланс.

Отдельно хочется выделить баги. Их тут немного, но зато какие.
Самый показательный пример - новая локация, появившаяся с выходом игры в Steam.
Один из первых боссов этой локации - True Herbalist кто-то там.
Так вот проблема в том, что в кооперативе босса нельзя пройти.
Вкратце - у босса есть две независимые полоски хп, и две модельки. Одну из моделек второй игрок не видит, но если её уничтожить, то происходит моментальный дисконнект.

В целом, игра создаёт о себе такое впечатление:
Чтобы искуственно растянуть малый хронометраж самой игры, разработчики решили добавить фарм магов с беготней за ними (начинает утомлять уже через 10 часов) и непонятной сложностью.
Игре это явне не пошло на пользу.

Я не вникал в процесс разработки игры, но очень похоже на то, что её делала абсолютно другая команда, и название было выбрано лишь для того, чтобы обеспечить себе продажи на фоне прошлого, успешного тайтла. 
Ghostrunner 2 очень похож в этом плане на Соль и Жертву: вроде та же игра, вроде выглядит так же, но динамика совершенно другая, добавился как будто бы ненужный хаб и весь геймплей ощущается также-но-иначе, в плохом смысле.
Лучше ли это, чем топтание на месте? Вопрос открытый.
Можно ли получить от игры удовольствие? Конечно, можно.
Главное не забывать, что тут оно идет рука об руку с корявостью и даже не кривой, а мёртвой петлёй сложности.

Можно много писать о том, что игра потеряла, не переняв из прошлой части, и что обрела, но суть остается той же:
Данная игра выглядит исключительно как коммерческий продукт, а не как что-то самобытно-крутое. Это не Salt and Sanctuary, и никогда ей не будет.

Salt and Sanctuary is one of my all time favorite games.

Salt and Sacrifice is not a bad game, but compared to Sanctuary, it is a complete downgrade with some really weird design choices. 

- Enemies and bosses have some spammy juggling attacks that could kill you once you get hit

- chasing mages throughout the map is tedious, boring and downright stupid idea.

- the game is divided into 5 separated main areas, i prefer an interconnected world like the original.

+ the movement here seems better and more fluid than the original

+ graphics are better than the original.

+ the world is still filled with lots of secrets to find.

overall i really enjoyed the game, as long as you don't compare it to the original, you will find a lot to enjoy in this one. i really don't understand why the Devs decided to ruin a winning formula, the game could have been much much better. despite the issues, i would still recommend this game to the people who liked the original.

Wow, I never review games, but I had the urge to make an exception here.

I want to start by saying Salt and Sanctuary is one of my favorite games of all time. I loved that game from the word go, and beat if numerous times in various ways. Even for the shortcomings that game had, there was still so much heart there and nothing that outright killed the experience or my adoration for the game itself.

This is not that game, nor is it a sequel. It's a hollow shell, the empty flesh suit of the game I love, draped over some grotesque bubbling mass trying to imitate the motions of a Monster Hunter game.

While a 2D monster hunter clone sounds good on paper, this game really did not execute on that vision at all.

Many other people have articulated better a lot of the grievances I have here, so it seems a waste to just repeat the same points you'll see on any other negative review here. I'll just say I agree with more or less all of them.

I just felt the need to say that this game didn't just disappoint me, 
It legitimately broke my heart.

Too bad it's a downgrade from "Salt and Sanctuary"!
So many things have been cut that it's only fun for a short time, e.g. Switch from one-handed to two-handed, no parrying, the skill system.

This game has been getting a bit of flak and while some of it is deserved a lot of it is not, so I will go into the good, the bad and the ugly.

[h1]THE GOOD[/h1]

[b]The Platforming - 8/10[/b]
The Platforming is a direct upgrade to Salt1, while sometimes challenging and sometimes frustrating, but ultimately fair in the end. The walljumps and inquisitor tools you pick up over time not only allow the game to more effectively build in verticality, it also rewards players for returning to older maps and explore them for additional goodies and loot. In the end a friend and I went through a trial area that tested your ability with the games platforming.

[b]The Coop - 10/10[/b]
The Cooperative play in the game is absolutely seamless, Attacks that enemies do as well as everything else that you can hit are all performed clientside, allowing you to play without issues with friends in cooperative play. I've played with a friend on another continent without any observable lag on my end.

[b]The Performance - 10/10[/b]
The fact that a modern game has the capabilities to be played with the minimum requirements being essentially [i]"a graphics card"[/i] without issues is honestly great. A friend of mine from brazil plays with the computer equivalent of a rusty toaster that dies when having discord and chrome open at the same time, and his computer ran the game without issue. The fact that it runes on hardware that's practically 20 years old is impressive to say the least.

[b]The Combat - 7/10[/b]
The combat is done well, it is reactive and it neither feels too strong nor too weak. Poise works for smaller enemies and allows you to tank a hit or two, but heavier opponents require either dodging, blocking or perfect blocking (the game's equivalent of a parry). I have to deduct some points for how some of the boss attacks seem a bit spammy and they ALL have the ability to ragdoll you, and it can get a bit jarring sometimes (looking at you [spoiler] aeromancer [/spoiler] ). Though ultimately the game has been extremely enjoyable for me.

[b]The Map Design - 7/10[/b]
The map design is honestly as always excellent. The hand-drawn art really brings forth the place you're in and it is clear when you know you can get to a place or not. The Doors that open up after certain bosses are clear in when they are accessible. But it is also confusing and often by the time new passages open up you will forget where they are. This leads to a lot of backtracking and getting lost, but I feel this was intended with how many secrets are strewn about.

[h1]THE BAD[/h1]

[b]The Multiplayer - 5/10[/b]
Note: With this I do not mean coop. I mean multiplayer through the used of multiplayer items in-game.
The multiplayer while not dead is somewhat slow, sadly. It can take a while before you are being summoned for cooperative boss hunting, or invading someone. However I believe they do a decent job at multiplayer balance. Seeing as you can be summoned at any range of multiplayer, but get scaled down to the level of the player.

[b]Armor Balance - 3/10[/b]
Armor feels like it is improperly balanced, namely heavy armor. It feels like the defenses do not give you enough to make up for the loss of evasiveness that light dodges give you. With some of the bosses light armor with light rolls makes it much easier to defeat them than medium or slow rolls do. I've finished the game with heavy armor and a medium roll, and especially in the DLC for some of the bosses light armor was a necessity.

[h1]THE UGLY[/h1]

[b]The game ending 0/10[/b]
Note: With this I do not mean the final boss
During the final boss after defeating him, you have no choice but to go into NG+, losing all key items. While this was likely predictable, it was still in a bit of a sour taste, as I wanted to do a few more [spoiler]nameless mages[/spoiler]. This means that by defeating the final boss, I was locked out of some end-game content, which was a tad jarring for me. 

[h1] The Result [/h1]
All in all, I believe this to be a good game. While not as good as Salt & Sanctuary in terms of boss fights and lore, it is still a greatly enjoyable experience. I will give the game a solid 8/10 up until the end after the final boss.

I'm kind of torn here, its a great game with some gigantic issues. I'll first say that the first one is better and if you haven't played it then don't play this one first. Full disclosure I'm not finished with the game and don't really know if I will finish it, I enjoy the exploration and love how bosses now drop materials so you can craft their weapons and armor. That being said, the bosses aren't great. I don't mind the mage hunts but they do get tedious, maybe the devs should tune down the sheer amount of them that you have to do. Plus, for goodness sake, stop spawning nameless mages during mage hunts! At times I've been fighting 4 different bosses at the same time. A lot of the non-mage bosses are complete BS, I'm talking about dragons who can cover the entire arena in a fire attack that you cannot dodge. Oh and that attack insta-kills you even with the best fire defense armor available at the time. This is only one example, there are lots of bosses that have attacks like this and fighting them pretty much comes down to hoping that they don't use that one attack that ends the fight. That all being said, some of the bosses are great and fun to fight and the worlds are great to explore. You can find NPCs hidden in the world and get them to come back to your "not firelink shrine" and become merchants or quest NPCs. All that being said, I enjoyed my time with the game and don't regret playing it for as long as I have, but I don't really have any desire to beat it. 

TL;DR Pretty good game if you liked the first one but be ready for some extremely unbalanced and unfun bosses.

Salt and Sacrifice is great. Just like Salt and Sanctuary and the other games Ska Studios makes, the game is very much so worth your money. While I can understand being mad over it having been Epic Exclusive starting out, something that is a terrible decision in my opinion in nearly every situation, but the game is great.

I've heard people upset that mages flee. This game takes a monster hunter inspiration with its mage bosses, them being like the monsters that spawn in the field and the minions they summon being used to further defend them from your onslaught, as the mages and their minions are friends to nobody. Not even other mages. Can it be annoying? Yes. But is this a problem? No. Not at all.

As for those who like salt and sanctuary more, I suppose I can agree. I went into this game looking for a good ol' Ska Studios game, and this game manages to hit a nice blend between Salt and Sanctuary and The Dishwasher with its gameplay, at least for me. Granted I forced Salt and Sanctuary to have a Dishwasher feel by speccing into light armor and multiple weapon types for good movement and speed fighting.

Also you can pet a cat deer and a deer...deer. Is nice.

Sarò biased perchè Salt and Sanctuary è uno dei miei giochi preferiti in assoluto ma non capisco che problemi ha la gente con Salt and Sacrifice. E' un gioco diverso, si. Quindi? Non stiamo neanche parlando di una differenza abissale tra un gioco e l'altro, è ingiustificato questo odio nei confronti del titolo.

[h3]PRO[/h3]
[list]
 [*]Sistema di combattimento divertente e pressochè alla pari del primo gioco.
 [*]Tanti mostri diversi e tanti equipaggiamenti diversi.
 [*]Multiplayer incentivato e molto divertente, ho avuto una sessione di 6 player contemporanei + 2 maghi. Il [b]CHAOS[/b] totale.
 [*]I mondi sono tante piccole zone che funzionano come i classici metroidvania, sono piene di shortcut e tesori.
 [*]Varietà di build onestissima. Le armi non hanno peso, tralaltro.
 [*]Fare il parry con qualsiasi arma azzera il costo di vita e stamina e tutto è parabile, trappole comprese.
 [*]Il gioco è bilanciato. Avete capito bene, è tutto bilanciato. Prendete troppi danni? Potenziate l'armatura. Non riuscite a schivare certi attacchi? Fate il parry. Non vi và di perdere i soldi quando morite? Comprate i sacchi d'argento (praticamente una cassaforte), come nel primo gioco.
[/list]

[h3]CONTRO[/h3]
[list]
 [*]Sarebbe carino se i maghi si spostassero solo 3 volte e ad ogni incotro spawnassero i mob di 1 tier più alto ogni volta.
 [*]Vorrei un opzione per resettare completamente lo skill tree, utilizzando un unica perla grigia (o come le chiama sto gioco)
[/list]

This review is in the experience of 2 player co-op. My friend and I beat the game together start to finish:

Pros:
 Large amount of shortcuts most leading to the start of a map.
 Can revive each other if you or your companion have used a guiltless shard.
 The first half of the game's level design is fantastic.
 The skill tree layout is improved over the first game.
 Getting +1max hp every level up makes you feel like you are getting stronger every level.
 They got rid of the cancerous fatigue system.

Cons:
 Players can NOT trade items. (if you did it to stop "twinking" then just make it so you can trade items if they were obtained while in a party)
 Unbearably tanky enemies with blue glowing eyes keep re-spawning whenever you look away. (and they drop blue pick ups that have a 1 sec uncancelable animation to loot 1-5 silver)
 Stat gain from leveling is very bad. (1 point in vitality gives you 2 max hp. 1 stat in endurance give you 1 carry weight)
You have to invest 50 levels into endurance a necklace slot and a ring slot that increase carry weight just to wear a full set of heavy armor(not the heaviest armor ether) and mid roll(yellow back pack icon).
 Poise is a one sided lie. (trash mobs stun-lock you with every trike even if you are wearing a full set of heavy armor and have artifacts that increase max poise. While half of the enemies in the game ignore a great hammer bashing their skulls in like its a fresh breeze)
 Second half of the game have very sadistic level design. (multiple jumps of breaking platforms with interchanging jump distances with traps that will kill you at the beginning, middle and or end of the platforms along with enemies shooting at you to hit you mid-air and make you fall to your death to make you do it all over again)
 Blocking is a one sided lie. You lose your entire stamina bar for blocking(except perfect blocking) one attack from a boss while enemies can block from both sides indefinitely.
 Some weapons are LEAGUES stronger then most so there is very little actual choice.
 Mage hunting is ... annoying to say the least. Instead of making them near immortal while chasing them and making them have a balloon that's easy to pop of heath when the fight actually begins. I would recommend you just have them teleport to a battle arena on their second teleport(instead of chasing them for up to 11 teleport) and give them some more health or just straight up make them re-summonable by a totem in their arena and remove the entire chasing mechanic in general. (and yes I know about the "boss rush" in one of the optional maps. It gives you basically no items so its a waste of time)
 De-sync is a huge problem. (Both me and my friend have good internet and have low ping between each other yet it kept disconnecting us during boss fights causing us both to be teleported back to base and making us have to chase the mage again)

It's not a bad game. But I would recommend Salt and Sanctuary over this.

TL:DR:
Armor is poop.
Most weapons bad.
No trade with friend.
developer_response:
Thanks for the feedback!

We've actually dramatically buffed poise and blocking (even more with vanguards), and have introduced hyper armor for slow heavy attacks.

We might revisit item trading. Seemed safer to leave it out, but maybe it would be fine?

review:
This is my second all-time dislike review... so you may understand the importance to me to write this.

I loved the first game, and bought this one hands-down with not a single doubt. And... man, why is this game WAY worse than the first one? I loved the combat in the first one. In this one it feels slow and clunky. Why having a 3-attack combo when ALL enemies will straight attack you back after the second one? Only some enemies are staggered when attacked, but for the rest is just a matter of attacking once - rolling to the other slide- attacking once - rolling to the other side. If you dare to try to combo twice.. you will get damaged. 

Why don't make the enemies cooldown after attacks longer (or their animations), so you can dodge their attacks and give them hell back for a while when you see an opening. These enemies have NO openings. They just SPAM the attacks one after the other. It is SO easily solvable: have an internal CD between attacks, or make the end of the attack animations much longer so you can use that moment to attack.

Bosses are spamming all the time and are not fair. The bubble mage would spawn bubbles all over the place and you have no place to hide: if you stay on your toes you will be hit, but if you roll, you will end up on another's bubble landing zone. I cannot stressed more how many times I have rolled out of an attack just to end up on another attack's zone. ALL-THE-TIME. 

So the game should only be based on blocking-attacking, and only dodging to position. But if I block with my weapon I get damaged nonetheless, so it is counter-intuitive to do that.

I really loved the first one, but I have stopped playing Salt and Sacrifice after 3 hours. And I am really, really bummed to do that. I wanted to finish the game, but I don't enjoy it.

Sadly not as good as Salt & Sanctuary, but pretty fun regardless. The setting is quite cool, but it's lacking the mystery of a lot of soulslike games because its story is so front loaded. Don't be afraid to just projectile and throwable spam bosses early on.

Probably a great sale game.

It's decent. Not quite as good as Salt and Sanctuary, but still worth your time if you like the first. The pacing is a bit strange, and the new weapon systems and variety feel slightly underwhelming, but the gameplay is still good, the art style is still fantastic, and overall it's still worth the asking price, especially if it can be gotten on any sort of sale.

他レビューにもある通り、モンハンに近い出来に仕上がっています。ボロクソにレビューしてる人ほどの出来ではありません。けっこう楽しめました。

ソウル系のベースにモンハンをやる２Dアクション…こう聞くと良さそうに見えるし、実際に要素としては各所に光るものがあり、実際にシステムやアクションなどは（モンハンだと思えば）楽しめました。
…しかしまぁ、これらの要素の噛み合いの悪いこと悪いこと。具体的に例を挙げるなら

・マップはソウル仕様の敵配置や罠があるにもかかわらず、モンハン並みにそこを駆け回りボスを倒さなくてはいけない。
・ボスは素材集めに何回か倒すこともあるモンハン仕様だが、これがソウル仕様のボスに合っていない。毎回苦戦を強いられることもある。
・２Dアクションのため拘束攻撃をしてくる敵が強い。これは前作も同じだったのだが、モンハン的なマップと敵配置によって何度も会うし捕まることになる。前作で言うならスピンドルビーストやマザーマーレに何度も会うようなこともある。

という感じで、どうにも噛み合ってない…。恐らく素材集めや製作などのシステムはエルデンリングからの影響のようにも見えますが、これが結果的にモンハンになっていたという感じですかね。もしゲームデザイン中に「これモンハンでは…？」ということに製作者が気づいていれば、もう少しモンハン寄せに整えられたんじゃないかと思ってます。

Alright, I don't do these normally, only other review was more of a joke one, but I'm going to try and write one of these, as I have a very good grasp of how this game works at this, played it when it released over a year ago, played it again at the end of last year, doing ng+ and then ng++ getting the platinum, and I finally got to experience the co-op and pvp invasion system with the steam release.

As it is, I would describe this game as a cross between demon souls and monster hunter. Not dark souls. Demon souls.

This itself is for a variety of reasons, the "Worlds" being smaller and disconnected from each other but more focused and tailor-made for the vibe each goes for, with plenty of really cool sites and vibes, the multiple stones you need for different upgrade paths, each tending to connect with a another depending on the kind of weapon/clothing, the focus on actually visiting later worlds before actually wrapping up earlier ones, having gained not only new mobility related tools, but also new and improved gear, (one could say that that one is more metroidvania-esque, but with the focus on different elemental damages and resistances, it reminds me more of world hopping in demon souls to get a proper damage type to trivialize another world, or when you have to kill and archdemon in order to get past the tower knight arena.). I could probably articulate it even more, but honestly, just the general vibe of both this game and salt and sanctuary make me think along the line of "flash demon souls". and of course, this game and it's predecessor have the customary, somewhat annoying, somewhat charming, jank to go with that sort of vibe, although I have never been one to be actually dissuaded by it.

But enough of that, let's actually get into the meat and bones of the game. there are more "classic" bosses, and then there are the mages themselves. the normal bosses are simply the, go to the arena and fight them, type of deal, not much to say on the front, a lot of them are pretty cool, and invite a bit of curiosity into the story of the different areas, like the hag boss with the puppets stuffed with the souls of children ( no, you get no context.), or the two that remained. The mages, on the other hand, are really where the monster hunter inspiration bleeds in. generally, you either find a area of distortions and corpse's left by a mage, and you start a quest to hunt them, or you find them milling about in the worlds as your exploring them. either way, you fight them, they summon some minions, you either kill them and deal damage to the mage, and they teleport to a new area, or you focus on the mage themselves and deal enough damage that they teleport away anyways and your left with minions in the aftermath. Or, you know, there's the third scenario where either the worlds inhabitants are disgruntled with the mage appearing and start wailing on them, or they get disgruntled with you instead and they wail on you. or a mix of both, because their caught in the crossfire of the mage and you. After the mage is finally cornered and properly dies of a forcefully induced heart attack. you get to loot the remains of the dispersed magic, and make gear from it. rinse, repeat. it's a simple loop on paper, but anyone who has played monster hunter know it's much more chaotic and fun in practice.

Now, there one thing that I would critique about this, and it is that this sort of gameplay loop does not lend itself well to 2d, especially with the customary vertical and platforming of 2d games. However, it is handled fairly well, and anyone who likes monster hunter or loves to see a boss get shredded because they decided teleport to the two heavily armored guardians in the blasphemers vault, and then found out that, it was in fact, not a good idea, all while you stand in the distance, will have a lot of fun with the actual gameplay, especially once they've found their forever weapon and armor (my favorite mages and their gear are the electromancer, and the dracomancer!).

Now, the game play loop aside, there is co-op and Invasions and pvp, and OH MY GOD, somehow, by some Vor-Yuile begotten, profane miracle, they have managed to finally do what no one else seems to have truly accomplished. They actually made really good, non-jank online seamless invasions and co-op, and the factions are actually really fun too! Now, before I hype anyone who understands what in any gods name this means TOO much, keep in mind that the net code and servers are dodgy at times (I swear it's curse, and you just can't convince me otherwise at this point), so your fun may be cut short. That, and some of the factions take longer to join on a session do to the requirement of an invader in the first place (fortunately, these ones get equipment that lets them get summoned whenever, where-ever while they wear it, so their not just waiting around). You have blueheart runner's, who aren't there to kill the host and and co-op buddies, but are encouraged to be hunted down by them because the bluehearts spawn various dangerous entities while fulfilling their task, which are annoying at the best of times, and really nasty at the worst. you have the sheriff's, who hunt the blue heart runners themselves, as a bit of push-back, because the the blue heart runners can just run instead engaging with the host forever. there are the shroud alliance and the chaos hunger, which are normal invaders and abnormal invaders respectively. shroud is just out to kill the host and the chaos hunger is out to kill or help anyone kill anyone else, as long as there is killing that can be done. there are the oathbound, who are there to help stop the shroud alliance from murdering the host, and there are the dawnlight guard, who are your co-op faction. all around, really fun, and it has been a blast doing invasions and co-op. There is also an area that let's up to six inquisitors fight each other in a free for all, or in a duel (I have not been doing this one yet, the invasions have been too fun so far to stop.).

honestly, I hold the game in equal regards with demon souls and salt and sanctuary, and other games I tend to hold close to my heart and come back to every now and then. without the co-op and pvp, it's as good as salt and sanctuary, worth playing through every now and then. with the co-op and pvp, its much better, and I would be willing to play this for months on and off for the sheer chaos and fun of it all, especially because (last little bit about the co-op here) you can password in a friend for co-op, and the two of you can share your progress completely and seamlessly until your done co-oping, AND you can still get a random co-op friend and start getting invaded while doing that, if you so choose.

Honestly, after having said all this, I can't recommend this game enough, even though there are in fact flaws, like the before said monster hunter chaos not doing so well in 2d, or some of the random haze-burnt spawns on hunts making traversal to mages way more annoying than they need to be, or you know, some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ insane final mage battle placements in the later areas (trust, no one saying this game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ actually know what their talking about half the time, because I can attest my self, you haven't seen ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game design in this game until you've fought the nameless diablomancer, or god forbid you fight a fated biblomancer in the temple of embers. THAT, is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, and everything else is rather fine, whether in comparison or talking about "Muh character gets juggled to death" or not.). But flaws aside, it's just a really great game, with really great online features, and with a really great story. I would probably talk about the story and lore a lot more, but I have enough respect and faith in other people to not spoil them in favor of them just wanting to find out the story on their own, or with another friend.

Is this game a good game? Yes, for the most part. It has issues, like enemies (and especially bosses) being too spammy, enemies being able to attack while spawning in, some enemy attacks having active hit frames before an animation even starts, having to chase down mages as they jump between rooms constantly, enemy attacks being able to just juggle/combo you to death sometimes, there's a bunch of flaws in the game that seem baked in or deliberately kept, a lot of which I would argue are pretty bad, and shouldn't be there. And yet I find myself unable to put it down because it has the sort of things you'd want from this game. Getting stronger, and beating stuff, as well as defeating certain things more easily than you did in the past, is pretty satisfying, if you like seeing a build slowly come to bear fruit, this game is pretty good at that. The co-op is actually pretty good, and it has a lot of quality of life things that bigger games be they bigger scale like Fromsoft or other indie games, lack.
Is it a good sequel to Salt and Sanctuary? No. In fact, I would argue it isn't actually a sequel at all. Instead of a souls-like metroidvania, this is more... Metroidvania Monster Hunter with souls-like mechanics. As a sequel to that game, it's actually quite frankly worse in a lot of places. 

Gear is so limited in so many ways compared to the first, and even build and playstyle variety is cramped down in comparison, you can't mix and match weapons with shields anymore, nor can you 1hand or 2hand weapons anymore. Factions feel more like game modes rather than a character roleplay aspect that actually affected some important things like in the first game. The metroidvania style unlocks are more utilised as area gates and shortcuts, and not things that can also help you out in a fight or make movement more fast and fun from what I've seen thus far. 

Even the character creation felt like it either didn't add more from what it was in the first game, or had less to it (If you liked the creepy smiling face, I'm sorry, it's not here.) than the first game, you don't even get an in-menu explanation for what your starting item is from your crime choice. I'm not really a fan of the timed blocking having replaced parrying either, the timing doesn't always feel quite right, and enemies are so spammy that I've found it considerably more rewarding, easier to do, and viable to just stagger bosses and enemies than get good with timed blocks. 

I think longevity wise, I'd play this more just because the co-op is significantly better in Sacrifice, especially now that you don't have to use remote play or parsec as proxies through local co-op like in Sanctuary.

My feelings for this game are mixed. As its own game, I would say it's solid but has many flaws, as a sequel to Sanctuary it's taken too many steps back compared to forward, and some of those steps are in such different directions from the first that despite feeling like you're playing a Salt and Sanctuary in some ways, it's strange that it's even labelled as a 'successor' to it. I do appreciate that it's not called Salt and Sanctuary 2, but I still understand the feelings of disappointment that people have with this game that it's not really holding up to the first, because in a lot of ways it really does not. In some alternate universe where this was called something else, or Salt and Sanctuary didn't exist, I think people would've liked this a lot more.

That all being said, I do recommend the game, but if you're expecting Salt and Sanctuary 2, either go in without thinking that, or skip this one.

Its like if you slammed salt and sanctuary and the worst aspects of monster hunter together. It's level based salt and sanctuary, but most equipment is locked behind "Mage" drops, which you get from hunting mages. These mage hunts are awful. They appear, ♥♥♥♥ out a ton of monsters, along with the stage's normal monsters, and then teleport away. Repeat numerous times, before they decide to stop and just fight you with only a couple attacks they have that they just spam over and over. 

In monster hunter, you can learn the monsters attacks and learn to avoid them. In Salt and Sacrifice, you can't. They come out with next to no warning. This applies to monsters too. The mage will spawn some monster that instantly grab attacks you, and by the time you kill it, the mage has run away, or spawned another monster to grab you again. 

It's the monster hunter equivalent of having a monster spawn a jaggi every time you hit it, and it constantly runs away, until it eventually stops and just spams a handful of moves it has, that come out with no warning.

The game has an idea, but the idea just doesn't work. Salt and sanctuary and monster hunter just do not mix. I'll be refunding, and reinstalling salt and sanctuary instead.

I waited for a time for its arrival on Steam purely because Epic Games is...Epic Games. Much of my excitement was...slowly diminished and by the time I hit the last area, I can wholeheartedly say that there are much potential that got buried under a few problems. Allow me to list some.

Exhibit 1: The Mages.
They are painted as dangerous beings that I found quite...cowardice. You hit them twice and they summon their minions, then fly away after attack once or twice. This repeats too much time for my liking before the actual boss fight start. Then you face with more often then not, two results: you kill them very fast, or they kill you very fast. The different here is that unlike you, they use cheap, petty moves that once hit, just combo into another attack. Most of the time, I don't feel rewarded for killing a Mage, it comes more as a "♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ it's done". And then you look at the list and now you realize you have a lot of the same thing to do. Some of the Mage is quite good and fun to fight. Most of them is middling. All of them is a chore to pursue.

Exhibit 2: Blocking enemy.
You can guardbreak enemy though you most often won't. Barring the basic goblin with the sword and shield, the moment an enemy guard, you may as well walk away. Wailing an entire stamina bar at them won't budge at all, and you more often than not, may get punished for that. The problem exacerbates when most of the summoned minions from the mage also have the same blocking problem. And being punished for playing the aggressor only to be heavily punished by a blocking small fry feels like ♥♥♥♥.

Exhibit 3: The world itself
It maybe due to the fact that Mages can roam around, but the world is much, much more empty then I expect. Now putting enemy literally everywhere isn't a good idea, but some of the area gives the feeling that "something should be here". That "something" can be some mage. But you, more often then not, won't see one there. 

Oh, and the trap. Traps are put in place they should be put. That's good. You won't see them though. They are so well-hidden that you won't notice them until you are already hit by it. And even after that, you still won't notice, because they blend too well with the environment.

These are some of issues I have with the game. There are more problems with the game, but I think these are the core problem that should be fixed. I like this game, so I would like it to be more than this.

As much as I want to love this game, and in a lotta ways I do, it really really really does not want me to enjoy playing it. I am a huge Salt and Sanctuary fan, and hope any comparisons I make below ideally are made between genuinely comparable portions of each game. I have not beaten the game, and will still try to, but I have developed feelings strong enough to feel like I needed to leave a review.

Pros:
-The levels, while not a fully open world like sanctuary, are still massive and sprawling and I found quite fun to explore. There's lots of platforming that visually spices up the exploration, as well as some really stunning areas I've seen (Dreadstone peak and altarstone in particular look great).

-Enemy placement, while just boneheadedly cheap in some spots, is generally fair enough only get you once with the surprise and keep you on your toes without feeling like you're dying [i]constantly[/i] and are also visually quite pleasing, fitting each level's design in a way that is reminiscent of sanctuary, but in a less desolate kinda way. 

-Combat is quite changed, but not in a way I find disinteresting. It keeps the souls style stamina bar and rolls, but weapon classes almost feel more like monster hunter weapons in how niche they are (like staves having specific spells tied to each on and still functioning mostly as a melee armament). Each class I've tried out has a fair few combos that I get use out of regularly in different situations, and I find myself properly wanting to and actually using the different weapons and gear I make/find

-The art is more or less a straight upgrade from sanctuary, as far as I'm concerned. It's the same style but detailed in the right places and with a helluva a lot more polish. ♥♥♥♥♥♥ love it.

The Cons

Ok it's not actually a list of cons it's just a big con with a lot of moving parts. The main gimmick of the game, hunting mages? Completely unfair and incredibly frustrating. Brings the game to a screeching halt and is genuinely so bad that I find it hard to keep playing a lot of the time despite absolutely loving the rest of the experience. 
It's specifically that the mages are bosses on their own and are dangerous on their own, but now they also teleport all over the level (with all the regular mobs that are also there) while [i]also[/i] spawning their own mobs and actually fighting themselves. This is just frankly too much ♥♥♥♥ going on, and it's so incredibly easy to get blendered in the blink of an eye. This is exacerbated by the fact that you get thrown by so many attacks, and any long distance you fall after being thrown you take full fall damage for, and the whole duration of the animation you're being thrown you are completely vulnerable to any enemy attack as long as it is not from the original enemy that threw you. And if you might remember, there's like trillions of enemies ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ everywhere if you're fighting a mage (which is required for both progression as well as getting access to a lot of the sick weapons in the game that I've found so far). It's so ball-bustingly miserable that I find myself rage-quitting quite literally every time I have to deal with a mage after and hour, and it takes multiple sessions to get through any hunt. Nothing is quite as ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ awful as fighting your way past the same set of enemies (because you cannot ignore them, it will ♥♥♥♥ you) 15+ times only to make one mistake and ToD'd within a minute and have to do it all over again. I have no ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ clue what they were thinking when they were balancing the mages but they throw such a massive wrench into an otherwise fantastic game that I cannot in good conscience say I recommend this game to anyone.

ติ๊กแนะนำไป แต่เป็นการแนะนำแถวๆ 7 คะแนน เทียบกับภาคแรก sanctuary ที่ผมให้ 9

สิ่งที่ผมอยากเสนอแนะแก่ผู้อ่านที่ยังไม่เคยเล่นตระกูลนี้เลยก็คือ เล่นภาค2(Sacrifice)ก่อน ก่อนแล้วค่อยเล่นภาค 1 (sanctuary)คุณจะรู้สึกได้ถึงการพัฒนาไปข้างหน้าขึ้น 

สิ่งที่ทำให้ภาคนี้ดูแย่/ดีกว่าภาค 1 (ซึ่งแล้วแต่จริตคนเล่น)ก็คือการพยายามเปลี่ยนคอร์หลักของเกมจากแนว 2D Souls-Like ให้เป็นลูกผสมแนวมอนฮัน อย่าพึ่งตกใจผมจะสาธยายให้อ่าน จริงๆผมก็แฟนมอนฮันไม่น่าจะมีปัญหาอะไรแต่ผมมีครับ คือมันดูฝืนไปนิด มอนที่เราต้องล่า(ในเกมเรียกว่า Mage)มันจะสุ่มๆเกิดในแมปที่ระบุไว้แล้วย้ายไปมา(เหมือนมอนฮัน)หรือบางทีแม่งไม่เกิดเลยก็มี ไม่มีอะไรบอกเลยจนกว่าเราจะวิ่งไถแมปไปเจอเอง ซึ่งในมอนฮันมันมีสิ่งอำนวยความสะดวกมากกว่าในการตามล่า มีการเลือกระบุเจาะจงว่าเราจะล่าตัวอะไร แต่อันนี้มันไม่มี ลุ้นเอาเองว่านี่กุจะไปเจอตัวอะไร คือในทางทฤษฎีผมไม่มีปัญหากับระบบตามล่า Mage แต่ในทางปฏิบัติบางทีมันไม่ไหวจริงๆ ไอการเจอหน้า mage ปุ๊บ ฟันได้ 2 ทีแม่งวาปย้ายทีอีกเนี่ย มันเหี้ยมากนะมอนฮันเค้ายังไม่ทำแบบนี้เลย 90% ของการต่อสู้กับเมจคือการวิ่งหามัน -> ฟัน 2 ที -> วาปย้ายที่ -> วิ่งหาใหม่ วนอยู่เงี้ย ไม่รู้ dev เค้าคิดอะไรถึงมองว่าการทำแบบนี้มันจะสนุก แถมหลายครั้งไอตัวที่ต้องการจะหาก็ไม่เจอก็ต้องวาร์ปกลับฐานแล้วมาใหม่ มันน่าหงุดหงิดเวลาที่คุณต้องการล่าตัวเฉพาะเจาะจงเพื่อเอาชิ้นส่วนมันมาคราฟหรืออัพเกรดอุปกรณ์ อีกจุดที่เหมือนมอนฮันจริงๆคือการล่าพวก mage เอาชิ้นส่วนมาไปปลดล็อคอุปกรณ์ใหม่ๆให้คราฟ หรือเอามาใช้อัพเกรดอุปกรณ์ ซึ่งบางชิ้นแม่งก็ดรอปยากมาก แล้วต้องใช้ในการอัพเกรดงี้(มอนฮันสไตล์)ทำให้ต้องล่ากันเหนื่อยอ่ะ อีกสิ่งที่เหมือนแรงบันดาลใจกันมาก็คือ เกมจะใช้ระบบ Hub (ฐานหลัก)แล้วก็ออกประตูไปยังแมปที่เราเลือกซึ่งมันจะมีทั้งหมด 5 แมปใหญ่หลักๆ(เห็นว่า DLC เพิ่มมาอีก 1 แมปก็เป็น 6 แมป)ให้เราวิ่งสำรวจตามล่าพวก Mage ไม่ได้เป็นแมปต่อๆกันยาวๆเหมือนภาค 1 อีกแล้ว พวกยาอะไรต่างๆ เค้าก็พยายามเอาฟีลของมอนฮันมา คือคุณต้องเก็บวัตถุดิบที่ใช้ปรุงยามาก่อน แล้วถึงจะทำยาได้ พวกยาฮีล HP ยาฟื้น FP ยาแก้พิษ ไอเทมเติมกระสุน ระเบิดเขวี้ยงต่างๆไรงี้ ไม่ได้มาสำเร็จรูปแล้ว 

ส่วนเรื่องของบอสไฟต์ หรือ ศัตรูปกติทั่วไป รู้สึกแย่กว่าภาค 1 พอควร มันค่อนข้างซ้ำซากและไม่ใช่ความยากท้าทายแบบ 1 แต่เป็นความ น่ารำคาญแทน เช่นพวกศัตรูบางตัวมันตั้งการ์ดใส่เราเหมือนไม่มีสตามิน่ากันได้ไม่รู้เหนื่อย แล้วก็ต้องรอให้มันใจอ่อนเลิกตั้งการ์ดเราถึงตีมันเข้าซักที ไม่มีคำว่าโดนตีจนการ์ดแตก มีแต่คำว่ามึงเลิกตั้งการ์ดยัง (ในขณะที่เราสามรถโดนตีการ์โแตกได้)แล้วพวกบอสบรรดา mage ทั้งหลาย ไม่ได้มีลีลาสเตปอะไรที่ท้าทายเท่าไหร่ แต่จะเน้นความยากแบบชุ่ยๆแทน เช่นให้พวก mage มีเวทที่โจมตีแบบโฮมมิ่ง ไล่ตามเราอัตโนมัติ หรือทำดาเมจแบบทิ้งไว้เป็น AOE บ้างเป็น Trap บ้าง ส่วนตัวบอสแม่งก็ยืนเฉยๆ ลอยไปลอยมา เหมือนกันหมด เทียบภาค 1 ไม่ได้ที่บอสมันมีลีลา มีความท้าทายกว่านี้

สิ่งที่แย่/ดี(ซึ่งแล้วแต่จริตคนเล่น)อีกอย่างคือสายเวทไม่สามารถเลือกติดตั้งเวทได้ตามใจชอบเหมือนภาค 1 แล้ว เวทมันจะผูกกับคทาที่ใช้เวท ซึ่งสูงสุด 3 เวท อิสระแบบภาค 1 ได้หายไปแล้ว ส่วนสายอาวุธอาจจะดีขึ้นเพราะแต่ละอาวุธก็จะมีท่าเฉพาะติดอาวุธมาให้ด้วย แต่ มันต้องใช้เกจ rage ซึ่งจะได้มาจากการโจมตีศัตรู และจะลดลงเรื่อยๆเมื่อเวลาผ่านไป นั่นหมายความว่าคุณก็ไม่สามารถสแปมท่าได้รัวๆตั้งแต่เริ่ม อย่างสายเวท คุณต้องฟันๆเก็บเกจก่อน ซึ่งผมรู้สึกว่ามันขึ้นช้าใช้ได้ (เห็นมีประดับช่วยบูสอยู่)

im posting this not as i true negative but more like i think the game is a c+ or a b- especially when compared to the last game. if this is your first time playing a game from this game studio, then the game is alright and i would hesitantly recommend it. but if you have played the last game(salt and sanctuary) you'll know this game is unfortunately an over all downgrade. so far bosses have been bland and unremarkable(and that includes NON-mages) hit stun can be atrocious some times, i understand punishing the player but enemies can and will stun lock you.

more inconveniences
-being unable to level up or access the skill tree at any teleport point.
instead you have to teleport back to the hub walk a good amount and then level up. it seems they try to improve the distance with the first tool you unlock. but if that the case, why not just have the statue be where you spawn in the hub.

-the crafting system
specifically healing and usable items
basically you get so many of the things required to make them and the fact that they are auto made on revive that it may as well not exist, which begs the question as to why it was added.

-mage hunts
like the level ups, these cant be initiated from a safe point. they feel uninspired, the higher difficulty ones only do more damage and gain like one move if i am remembering correctly. this makes them either to easy or gratingly difficult(mainly due to the projectiles that can cover a decent size of the stage/ boss room).

- the crafting system
the weapons just don't feel as unique as the last game. before it felt like even basic weapons had a chance to be cool since you could turn them into some special later but here they just don't feel that way. i get that's a part of the monster hunter genre and there some spectical in min maxing like that, but in the previous game( and the monster hunter games) it feels like the weapon you chose (class and damage type) was an important choice but now it feel alittle more stale if you will. partly due to the mage weapons having similar or the same weapon arts
 also, not sure why there are no guns or off hand weapons. and aim is very bad on controller.

there is probably some i missed and none of the things i listed or forgot to list make this a bad game, in-fact there a lot of positives, like the lighting being improved in most areas and the art style looks slightly better to me but i just think it could be better personally so id only recommend if you know what your getting into.

Can start this review off with the fact that, this game has a pettable cat. - 10/10.

Anyone to compare Salt & Sacrifice and its sequel, Salt & Sanctuary would tell you the same thing - You must be crazy to leave a negative review of this game by comparing it to Salt & Sanctuary. They are both incredibly different games. That yet still both work cohesively well together, and yet with very different gameplay & focuses. However, and still further yet, both remain incredibly fun and cohesive, immersive, fun games with interesting worlds & story-building.

This is my second purchase of Salt & Sacrifice after it's post-Steam release off of its Epic-exclusive purchases. Everything from its unique, beautiful & atmospheric art style to the design for the enemies, bosses & items the Inquisitor will be using to slaughter their way forward to devour wretched hearts, to mini-bosses and small details go a long way. The bosses are quite fun, and there's quite a lot. Story-telling is everywhere, the world presents itself really well, and there are NPCs who will tell you all about the world, old gods, and inquisitors path to ruin. The progression is almost Monster-Hunter esque with the mage hunting and craftable mage rewards, unique to each one in design and function. Weapons also have runic abilities, and are quite well done in both visuals and function. Build theorizing lacks a little bit in variety, and there aren't a whole lot of weapons for each weapon type - But this is is game that is constantly expanding and improving, and has more than enough to make up for it as is - Co-op is incredibly fun, the game runs well and has AMAZING sound design and music at times where it is present - The UI is incredibly simple to move through, and works really well to compliment the rest of the visuals. - Game difficulty is well-centered, a good mix between too hard, well mixed, or easy. It sets the player up well for success, typically giving you the effective weapon type to hunt your next mage before you have to, and the difficulty is extremely well-paced for newcomers, with learning curves that are pretty feasible - Rolling is generous, and they even go a step further to let you grapple onto Mage faces and destroy them up close for even more openings, a feature I did not expect them to utilise so incredibly well with player utility items used in and around for map exploration.

Salt & Sacrifice offers immersive level design with more emphasis on exploration & metroidvania elements, there's wall jumping, grappling hooks, a glider to soar through wind currents, and even a 'luminstone' to reveal hidden platforms to traverse. There are tons of unlockable alternative routes to go through, and it makes the maps just so much more cohesive. There is convenient fast travel after non-mage bosses, and the new update 'Traitor's Tomb' expands both the story and content much further, there is much more polish. For a team & studio as small as Ska Studios, of which people fail to realise is not a Triple AAA company or large team, are crazy to think that this game isn't big for how much work goes into it. It nails replayability, difficulty, and diversity. Each update heavily improves its functionality, flow & fluidity, and polish.

I do not have anything more than lots of praise to Ska Studios, another Ska Studios project that goes above and beyond my expectations and beliefs of what they are capable of, constantly improving both their own work and their games. With lots of community feedback that they feel they are very tied to, and how active they are in listening, responding to, and working on improvements.

《盐与献祭》是横板魂类游戏《盐和避难所》的全新续作，以独特的游戏难度、自由的地图探索以及多武器、多魔法的战斗方式为卖点。

虽然游戏是2D的展现，但打击手感尚且可以。

再加上以魂类游戏的深邃故事作为基调，对魂类玩家颇有好感，1代的口碑不错，而2代便在此基础上，继续加强了演出效果。

[h1][quote=]
[table]
 [tr]
 [th]　欢迎关注 [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/38287879]网易UU鉴赏家培养计划[/url][/th]

 [/tr]

[/quote][/h1]

[h1]前言：[/h1]
再说游戏前，要先大力表扬一下本游戏。对比前作本游戏的话面舒适程度真的是几何倍数提升，有非常大的改进。

前作也很好玩，但美中不足的是，前作的话面表现制作者可能要表现一种荒凉孤寂的情景，就由大量的烟雾效果以及高亮的白色场景。

而《盐与献祭》则与之不同，初次打开游戏，色调明艳，画质清晰，与前作有天壤之别，眼睛不难受了，特大表扬！

[h1]游戏简介：[/h1]

与前作的海难幸存者不同，在本作中我成为了一名死刑犯，而王国严厉的法度无法容忍我因为醉酒误事导致的后果，我将被处以极刑。

但是即使是死刑犯，王国也愿意给我第二次机会，让我变成一名贼配军，真的是太仁慈了。

这还要说到处刑的前一天，一个衣着华贵的大人找到了我。他告诉我即使是废物也有利用的价值，而你你这个酗酒废物还是一名圣骑士，那么你你愿不愿意被王国再次利用呢？

虽然结果可能不比现在就烧死你强多少，但是你至少不会死的像个废物，你是为了王国而死，充满了荣耀。或者你明天就去死好了，你这种垃圾整个王国的监狱里多的是，你就去死好了。

在大人物戏谑与不屑的口吻中，我看到他看我的眼神和看垃圾没区别，我不甘心就这样被烧死或者吊死，我可是一个圣骑士啊！

于是我选择了唯一的自救机会，我选择成为王国的刺印调查官，饮下法师之敌药剂，成为一个在物质世界真正意义上的法师之敌。

于是在我的中二脑补中，游戏开始了。

[h1]游戏内容：[/h1]

如果主角知道自己进后要面对什么绝对不会认为王国有仁慈的一面。

游戏中我们将会创建一名因为罪孽而即将处刑的死囚，但是却在类似我上述内容下成为了一个刺印调查官。

游戏为玩家提供了相当多的开局职业模板，并且有一个与前作随葬品机制差不多的罪行选择，而每种罪行代表一个初始饰品。

通过职业组合与相应的道具足合可以非常有利的帮我们度过前期。

游戏的动作系统和前作稍有进化，并且前作和本作在横板魂类游戏中动作都算是相当不错的，本作还加入了更加丰富的打击与击杀音效，代入感比前作强不少。

本作依然是轻攻击+重攻击的模式，并且继承了前作中武器连段的设计，让动作体验保持在了一个相当不错的水准。

武器，装备道具则是在前作的基础上增加了不少新内容诚意比较足。就是感觉远程武器如果不重新设置键位可能玩起来稍微有些不协调。

在道具方面本作可能或许收到了近些年比较流行的玩法，出现了比较多的资源采集内容，也让游戏的体验不再是单纯的刷魂刷钱了，丰富度大大提升。

[h1]总结：[/h1]

《盐与献祭》的游玩体验真的是远远强于前作《盐和避难所》，优秀的色调以及更加舒适的视觉呈现让我从新审视了这款作品。

如果说前作只能给6分的话，这作的画面可以在前作基础上加一分，细节设定和新内容丰富度又可以加一分。

这8分中包含了小团队对内容创作的不易，已经愿意改进前作不足，跟随世代潮流而进化。

我认为这款游戏是值得一玩的，不管是从魂类爱好者的角度还是横板动作游戏爱好者的角度来说，这款游戏目前来说都是很不错的。

所以说值得体验，推荐大家尝试。

As a huge fan of Salt & Sanctuary (but not such a huge fan of splitting my game collection) I was waiting for what seemed like forever for this to come to Steam - and fans of the first game will be happy to know it doesn't disappoint.

The hand-drawn graphical style is as sharp as ever and the enemies are fresh and new and not just retreads or skins of ones from the first game (for the most part....but spoilers so I won't elaborate).

Combat feels good, but it has one downfall: it can get very frustrating being stagger-locked by 2 or 3 small, nearly trivial enemies at once and dying before you can get out to counterattack.

The crafting system is robust and plentiful, with new items, and elemental tiers to craft opening up after you defeat certain Mages. Mage-farming is a bit odd, and sometimes when going back to farm one, it will blink to an area you can't access, which, I have to say, is also a bit frustrating, but it's usually a quick task to port back to camp and reset it for a better outcome.

The music is fantastic and really lends itself to establishing the overall atmosphere of the game.

The one major problem with the game is lack of much tutorial information. Most features and tasks are just mentioned once by an NPC and then it's up to you to figure out exactly how they work or where to go next, et. al., which is kind of a bother since there's more systems in Sacrifice than there were in Sanctuary. Luckily, since the game has been on Epic for over a year, there's already an established wiki and plenty of walkthroughs to get you up and on your feet.

Overall, this is a great game and I'm having a really fun time playing through it. If you're fan of 2D Soulsborne games, this is hands down the best series of them out there and I highly recommend it. Overall I would give it a 9/10, but of course ymmv.

I hope this review is helpful to anyone looking to dive into this franchise - or worried the sequel doesn't live up to the original.

There are so many major inconveniences and annoyances. For example, the game doesn't show you your class' starting stats. Why? The first game didn't show it either but at least it tells you the name of the starting item you select. Salt and Sacrifice does not. It tries to be quirky for the sake of being quirky and makes you choose a starting "crime" which influences your starting item. But you can't even hover over it or select it to see what the item even is. 

On that topic, Salt and Sacrifice doesn't tell you anything, just for the sake of being mysterious. Because it doesn't tell you your starting stats for a class, I had to start the game, then check what my stats are. Then I wanted to compare the stats to another class so I had to exit to the main menu and start a new save with another class to see their stats. There is no reason to withhold this information from the player, especially if they're gonna see these stats in literally a few seconds anyway. This is just one example, and this alone is enough to make players need to open up a wiki. You lose the sense of mystery you are trying to create if you withhold absolutely everything from the player because then it just becomes tedious and they'll have to resort to using a wiki.

Some other annoyances in my limited playthrough:
- You have to teleport all the way back to the hub area and walk up a non-trivial amount of steps to access your skill tree. In the first game you could do it from any checkpoint. Why do I have to fast travel to the camp to check my skill tree?
- Skipping the opening cinematic skips the tutorial and the first boss (that you are supposed to die to, but still. If you are on a second playthrough and have already seen the cutscene but have the skills to take on the boss, you'd have to sit through the cutscene)
- To use ranged weapons with a controller, you need to do some yoga with your fingers. You first hold the left bumper, aim with the thumbstick (do not let go of it or it will reset the aim to in front of you), and then press the X button. I don't understand this decision. Your hand is in such an awkward position that attempting to press the X button will cause the thumbstick to wiggle a bit and throw off your aim. Like, why can't it instead just be the right trigger to fire? You're already holding the left bumper so the game knows you're trying to aim, so it won't confuse it for you trying to dodge roll (also mapped to right trigger).
- Massive lore dump at the start and huge hub area with locked doors and unclear paths everywhere, as opposed to the first games linear focused start and better interconnected world
- The tutorial pop ups only show up when standing on a very tiny spot. If you move just a few steps they will disappear until you step back on the area. The problem is there are enemies here running towards you to attack you so you will be trying to fight them off instead of being able to read the popup
- In fullscreen mode, if you tab out of the game (which you will do often to check guides because the game doesn't explain anything), theres a high chance it will bug out and not let you open the game. You'll have to force close it. In borderless, when you tab out of the game, if starts to flicker white really fast. 1000 flashbangs a second.

Lots of people compare this to Sanctuary, and I don't think that's helpful. This game isn't really a SEQUEL, except for sharing many systems and names with the first. Instead, this game merges the souls-like combat and RPG levelling from the first, with monster chasing and farming systems from Monster Hunter-type games.

There are still traditional bosses who gate the metroidvania-esque upgrades which allow your player to access new areas and treasures, but they are much fewer in comparison to the mages (the monster hunter monsters), and honestly the bosses that I've fought so far are fairly plain in attack design. This leads to people who come expecting this to be primarily a souls-like to be disappointed.

On the other hand, the mages are actually quite varied, each with their own "element", summon monsters, and attacks. They DO teleport around a small area while you chase them and get a couple attacks in. I personally don't mind that, as it gives the player some time to heal, buff, and deal with summoned monsters. They also usually just teleport slightly off screen, so you're not out of the fight long. 

One system I personally don't like as much as in Monster Hunter is the grinding. In which I mean that so far, there is virtually none. After killing a mage for the first time, their drops, combined with quest rewards, are usually enough materials to make the 1 or 2 items from their "tree" you want. And if you don't have enough resources, I've found that killing them one more time - which is usually quicker than the first time - will get you enough. I know a lot of people don't like the grindey nature of MH games, but I wish there were slightly rarer drops that give you a reason to fight each mage a few more times.

Overall, I think this game is a better MonsterHunter-like than it is a souls-like. I like this game as a MH-like as much as I liked Sanctuary as a souls-like. And that's saying something, since I don't think there are any good MH-likes available.
